“Not a problem. Blacklight Biotechnology will contribute a humanitarian aid fund worth five hundred million US dollars to this country.”

Chen Chen nodded. Once again, he had not the slightest hint of hesitation.

“Really?!”

Zurama was excited. The Parliament had initially thought they needed to incur huge losses and even sacrifice their dignities before Chen Chen would agree, which was why they had sent Zurama to test the waters.

However, no one would have thought that Chen Chen would agree so swiftly. He had yet to list any of the conditions the Parliament thought he would...

“Of course.”

Chen Chen left his office table and sat on the couch as he gestured to Zurama to join him. “Although I’m not a citizen of Namibia, I’ve collaborated with this nation for a while now. Logically and sentimentally speaking, it’s my duty to help.”

Chen Chen smiled. “There’s an old saying in Mainland China that I’m fond of – As I treat others with sincerity, others shall do the same too. Today, I’ve provided a helping hand to this country. I wonder if this country could agree to a few of my requests as well?”

The inevitable had arrived...

Zurama smiled wryly and said, “Do list it out. As long as it’s not too unreasonable, the Parliament will consider it.”

“Of course. it won’t be anything unreasonable.”

Chen Chen nodded. “I hope you still remember about the forty-years leasing period of this fifty thousand hectare worth of land. Now that I think of it, a forty-year period is too short. If it’s possible, I wish to extend the leasing period by another fifty-nine years.”

“Well...”

Zurama held his breath for a moment before he nodded reluctantly. “Understood. I’ll bring this up in the next Parliament sitting.”

“And one more thing.”

Chen Chen continued saying, “Throughout the trial operations of the nuclear power plant, I’ve noticed a severe problem. The electricity generated is just too little for my projects. I hope that from today onward, I will have full control over the distribution of the electricity supply without any regulations from the government.”

“You!”

Zurama could feel his entire face freezing up.

The only reason Namibia would allow a foreigner to establish a nuclear power plant on their land was to accelerate the development of their country. Now that the government had no say when the plant was built, what use was there for that nuclear power plant in the first place?

It was something that no government could compromise on.

“My apologies, I’m afraid the Parliament wouldn’t agree to this.”

Zurama was stern as he said coldly, “I’m afraid you’ll have to revise this condition.”

“Let’s not rush to a conclusion too soon, Minister Zurama.”

Chen Chen responded as if he had anticipated that response. He smiled nonchalantly and said, “If the Parliament is concerned, we can meet in the middle. I’ll allocate another five hundred million US dollars to invest in a one million kilowatt thermal power station in Windhoek’s industrial area. This will contribute to the development of Windhoek, what do you think?”

“...”

It was then when Zurama finally quieted down.

Chen Chen knew that he had hit a spot and swiftly seized the opportunity. “I recall that the thermal power station near Windhoek was built thirty years ago. The machinery is aged and worn out, which causes limited electricity supply in Windhoek. With another power station built, the old power station can be upgraded. By then, perhaps there’ll be sufficient electricity supply to even power nearby cities. This is a win-win solution...”

“What about the electricity bills?”

Zurama asked abruptly.

“I can cede full control of the power station’s operation to the Parliament.”

Chen Chen pondered a while longer and continued. “As for myself, all I need is to collect a lease fee rate at... One million US dollars per month!”

Zurama was shocked and asked, “Are you sure about this?”

From Zurama’s perspective, a million US dollars lease fee was the equivalent of giving the entire power station for free!

“Yes, no hidden conditions attached.”

It was then that Zurama began settling down. He took another close look at Chen Chen and sat back on the couch.

It was undeniable to Zurama that the influence this young man possessed had been growing at a rate where the Namibia Parliament could no longer control.

Zurama could still recall reading the semi-annual statement that was submitted by Blacklight Biotechnology to the tax department. It was written that the net earnings generated by the hundreds of sanatorium under Eco Science City in the first half of 2023 were an astounding 1.8 billion US dollars!

What kind of figure was that?

In 2022, the annual GDP of Namibia was only 1.5036 billion US dollars, which indicated that just the net earnings of an institution under Blacklight Biotechnology were enough to surpass the total production value of Namibia!

In addition, Zurama had also recalled that Eco Science City was one of the various subsidiary units of Blacklight Biotechnology. It was said that Blacklight Biotechnology also owned a branch in Switzerland and a headquarter in Mainland China, with the headquarter specializing in the production of technological products that garnered hundreds of millions worth of profits.....

Furthermore, they both agreed to a condition that Blacklight Biotechnology would enjoy a full tax exemption in the first three years and a half tax exemptions in the subsequent five years. This meant that no matter how profitable Blacklight Biotechnology was in the first three years, the Namibian government could not benefit at all.....

Zurama had even thought if he could just claim the city and turn it into an industry that belonged to Namibia. For sure, his country’s power would increase by another level, right?

Unfortunately, the Parliament did not dare commit such an act. No other countries would dare do such a thing or they risk losing all of their public legitimacy.

Looking at the productive youngster before him, Zurama sighed and said, “Understood. I’ll bring these conditions up for discussion in the Parliament. I just hope that you’ll do your best in finishing the vaccine as soon as possible, Sir Chen.”

For the first time, Zurama had addressed Chen Chen as “Sir”.

“The vaccines will arrive in Namibia Airport within three days.”

Chen Chen nodded and continued. “Not only that but we’ll also be providing disposable medical equipment. I hope that you’ll consider my requests heavily as a return favor.”

“That’ll be for sure.”

Zurama answered solemnly and thus, the initial stage of the negotiation was concluded.

Now that the duo had reached a consensus, the tension was way less oppressive and smiles had returned to both their faces. Meanwhile, the sound of water boiling in a teapot was heard from Chen Chen’s desk.

“What sound is that?” Zurama was amused.

“Perhaps you’re new to the tea culture in Mainland China.”

Chen Chen stood up and went to retrieve a Chinese tea saucer before proceeding to place a couple of tiny teacups on it as he began brewing the tea.

“Tea culture?”

Zurama said curiously, “I’m familiar with tea leaves that originated from Mainland China, but what’s a tea culture?”

Chen Chen smiled and did not utter a word. He followed the instructions that he had read from the Internet to brew his tea. The fragrant aroma of tea leaves began to spread across the room.

At last, Chen Chen poured the light green liquid into a teacup and passed it to Zurama and asked, “Would you like to try?”

Zurama raised his brows as he received the teacup over and swallowed in a single gulp.

He was immediately greeted by a refreshing hint of flavor...

“I’ve never drunk tea that tasted like this!” Zurama exclaimed.

“You’re over complimenting.”

Chen Chen said humbly, “By the way, Minister Zurama, is the death statistic of this country out yet?”

“Not yet, the Parliament is still lacking the resources to conduct the analysis.”

Zurama’s face turned solemn and said, “Mr. Chen, in your opinion, how severe is the death toll in Africa?”

“Very severe.”

Chen Chen lowered his eyes and said, “Although official news had placed the death toll to be within 30 million people, these statistics are merely from the neighboring countries of the pandemic’s epic center. The countries that were first hit by the pandemic such as Rainbow Nation, Angola, and Namibia have all lost the resources to compute their statistics. Worse still, some smaller nations have even perished.

“In other words, the real death toll has far exceeded 30 million people.”

Chen Chen reiterated, “Although 30 million seemed shocking, it’s still a relatively comforting number compared to the realistic figure. The true death toll is probably ten times more than this...”

“Sigh...”

Although Zurama had prepared himself mentally, he was still appalled when he heard those words coming right out of Chen Chen. As Zurama stood up, he could feel a numbing sensation pressing onto his scalp. “Mr. Chen, thank you for hosting me. I should leave now...”

“Okay, I imagine there are still many matters that demand your attention in Namibia. I shall not insist on making you stay.”

Chen Chen personally accompanied his guest to the entrance and watched as the frailing minister gradually left his sight. He could not help but shook his head.

Sometimes, humans could be complex and unpredictable creatures...

Just like this minister who was in charge of overseeing two departments. If Chen Chen had not socialized and interacted much with Zurama, he may have thought Zurama to be the righteous official he seemed to be...

chapters.title: Chapter 220: Fully Automated Medical Platform
chapters.body:
Chapter 220: Fully Automated Medical Platform

Chapter 220: Fully Automated Medical Platform

Time passed by quickly.

Three days later, the first batch containing one million vaccines and disposable medical equipment arrived in four transport aircraft at Namibia’s capital, Windhoek. The supplies were sent by a subsidiary unit of Blacklight Biotechnology, Tara.

On the sixth day, the second and last batch of vaccines arrived on time.

On the eighth day, the Namibian government began to resume its function.

On the ninth day, the country’s military units were mobilized to clean up all corpses that were left in the open as well as to locate remaining survivors.

On the fifteenth day, all the refugees in Eco Science City were fully dismissed. The government had also begun the restorative process to rebuild the nation from the ground.

Having been confined to the indoors for so long, the staff of Blacklight Biotechnology could finally walk out and feel the comforting bath of warm sunlight and the caresses of the refreshing breeze.

As the initial occupants of Walvis Bay had either died from the pandemic or escaped overseas, the coastal city had now resembled a ghost town. Aside from patrolling soldiers, what was left of the city was merely citizens huddling up in corners.

As if the entire country was reshuffled, Namibia’s already scarce population had shrunk even further.

At least, the worst was finally over.

Looking past the quiet Eco Science City, Chen Chen shook his head slightly.

He could not help but feel he was out of luck. When Eco Science City had managed to gain popularity within half a year since opening, they were immediately met by the pandemic.

Most businessmen would have been made bankrupt under similar circumstances. Fortunately, Chen Chen possessed a huge safety net that allowed him to endure the challenges.

As time would heal all wounds, Eco Science City would also one day return to its former glory.

Besides, the Blacklight virus that had shaken all countries had also cemented his company’s presence worldwide. Chen Chen’s company was the first to discover the Blacklight virus and to invent the vaccine. At least one-third of the world’s population now knew about Blacklight Biotechnology.

This was especially true when conspiracy theories mushroomed on the Internet, with many claiming that the Blacklight virus was a biological weapon developed by the Northern American army and many others claiming that the virus was released by the Swiss. Some even claimed that the virus was developed by Blacklight Biotechnology in the African Continent.

As different narratives took off, netizens all across the globe quarreled furiously on the Internet. Yet, no one was able to produce any shreds of evidence.

At last, governments from all over the world had to make an appearance and show conclusively that the virus was indeed just a natural evolution of its predecessors. It was only then that the commotion was put to rest.

Not long after, under the announcement of the World Health Organization, countries had soon wrapped up their vaccination research and initiated their large-scale animal testing.

The clinical trials would only begin once the animal testing indicated that the vaccines were harmless.

It was during this period that many also began to understand the true significance of Blacklight Biotechnology’s ability to research a vaccine a month and a half ahead of time.

The announcement had showcased the remarkable technology and skills Blacklight Biotechnology possessed. Many who had initially brushed off the company were starting to take it seriously.

On the other side, Little X had returned empty-handed for the first time.

After more than 10 days of scanning, Little X did not manage to detect any abnormal information about the pandemic in the Earth Federation’s information hub, disproving Chen Chen’s hypothesis.

“Was I thinking too much?”

Chen Chen contemplated. It could be that he had indeed overthought, but it could also be that some classified pieces of information were simply untraceable on the Internet.

After all, the Internet was like a massive ocean. To find a needle in a haystack was simply an understatement when it came to looking for specific pieces of information on the Internet, even for Little X.

Furthermore, actual secrets were typically disconnected from the Internet whereas individuals who were privy to that level of secrecy would only belong to an exclusive inner circle.

Perhaps it could be some figures within the Earth Federation who was standing above the confinement of human society...

“Cough.”

Since the search did not yield any result, Chen Chen decided to not bother himself with this issue for now. He was now in the nuclear power station, staring into the USB drive in his hand as he contemplated in front of his monitor screen.

Another month had passed and the USB drive had now contained copious amounts of electrical energy, emitting countless little spots of lights that the object resembled a galaxy.

If the trend had continued, would that mean that the USB drive would turn into a completely glowing silver object if it was fully charged?

Who would have known? Perhaps the USB drive would go through yet another change as it reached that level.

However, if Chen Chen had wanted to make the USB drive glow completely, he would need tens of thousands of times more energy to accomplish that. It was not something he could afford to explore.

Then, he proceeded to inject the USB drive into the computer and transferred another movie into his USB drive.

Elysium.

Soon, the movie was successfully uploaded into the USB drive.

“Very well.”

Chen Chen breathed a sigh of relief. After visiting the Resident Evil dimension, the USB drive still had enough charge to carry 3000 kilograms of matters over.

It was enough to obtain a movie object that Chen Chen had long set sight on. On second thought, it would not even be a difficult task.

The movie was set in the year 2154. It was a time when Earth was split into two social classes. The first social class was the rich and elite, who resided in a man-made celestial object called the Elysium.

The Elysium took after the design of a Stanford torus, a concept proposed by NASA in 1975. It was situated in low Earth orbit, where it would rotate once per minute so that its residents experience the same force of gravity as they would on Earth. Having a breadth of three kilometers and a diameter of sixty kilometers, the structure could house a few million residents in its capacity.

It was a beautiful environment where people could live a carefree life free of any disease and sickness. It was a haven built only for the most elite of society.

Meanwhile, as a stark contrast to this social class, there existed the poor. When environmental pollution had rendered Earth nearly uninhabitable, the rich moved to the Elysium, leaving countless poor residents on the planet.

The Earth was suffering from massive environmental degradation and overpopulation. The residents were forced to resort to laborious work to make ends meet, living an arduous and tiresome life.

Such was the opening scene of the movie.

However, Chen Chen felt that the movie’s stance was too extreme or even ridiculous. After all, a society that possessed the ability to transfer billions of tonnes of materials into Earth’s orbit had probably achieved the technology to harness nuclear fusion.

At that level of scientific advancement, coal combustions would have long been irrelevant as they tap into an infinite source of electrical energy. By then, Earth’s environment could only improve from there onward, not the other way round.

Besides, if humanity had the ability to construct a Stanford torus, they would be capable of migrating across the Solar System, setting up permanent residences on Europa, Dione, and even on planets like Mars. How was that not a more effective solution than constructing the Elysium from scratch in space?

Furthermore, rich people and poor people were not the only types of people that lived on this planet. Where did the director think the middle class went?

However, there was no need for discussing these social ideologies in depth. After all, quantum mechanics dictated that there existed an infinite amount of parallel universes. There were no limits to how ridiculous civilizations would develop for humanity.

The most unforgettable feature of this movie was not the movie’s social commentary nor the protagonist’s killing spree. It was the feature of a technologically-advanced machine.

This machine was a fully automated medical platform – the Medpod 3000.

According to the movie’s settings, the Medpod 3000 was claimed to be able to cure all diseases that had plagued humanity since the beginning. A young girl stricken by leukemia needed to only lie in the machine for a few minutes before she would fully recover her health.

Not only that but the Medpod 3000 also possessed the capability to repair bodily damages. When the movie’s antagonist had his entire face blown off by a grenade, his comrades only needed to place his body on the Medpod 3000 for a quick scan before his facial bones, skins, and all structures were swiftly repaired back to perfection.

In addition, the Medpod 3000 was capable of maintaining various bodily functions, enough to extend the lifespan of its users by two folds.

Chen Chen had long wanted to acquire one for himself. However, he was impeded by the lack of power his USB drive contained. Now that this problem was solved, Chen Chen immediately hopped back onto this project.

After all, the sudden onslaught of the pandemic had made Chen Chen realize how important a healthy body could be...

The biggest enemy of the 21st century, cancer, remained an incurable medical disease till now. Even Chen Chen had failed to cure it despite attempting the shortcut method of acquiring a solution in another dimension.

However, if he could acquire the Medpod 3000 machine from Elysium, it would serve as a proper insurance for Chen Chen.

At that point, Chen Chen was fully determined to follow through. Instead of opening a pathway to another dimension, Chen Chen played the movie and paused the frame right where the Medpod 3000 was shown.

Then, he slowly extended his hand and reached directly for the medical equipment...

“Let’s hope this works.”

Chen Chen murmured. Although the computer was merely a 15.6 inches laptop where a single person could not fit into the screen, it worked the same way as the previous black hole. That was, it did not matter how big the screen was, it had no relation to the size of the portal that formed between two dimensions.

Under such circumstances, a person was able to pass through the portal even without lowering their backs.

Chen Chen’s right hand had already passed through the screen. He came into contact with what felt like the surface of a metal equipment. With a slight jerk, the equipment could be lifted easily by his hands!

That was interesting.

Chen Chen was awestruck and immediately pulled the equipment out!

Light flashed across Chen Chen’s sight before a peculiarly built red machine appeared before his very own eyes!

“Boom!”

Meanwhile, the movie on his computer immediately shut down on its own.

“It was a success!”

Chen Chen took a deep breath and once again marveled at the miracles of the USB drive. Not only did it allow objects to pass through a tiny portal but it also gave a normal person the ability to move an object that weighed a few tonnes.

After all, common sense would dictate that even the strongest human that existed would not be able to single-handedly move an object that heavy.

Chen Chen took a closer look at the machine that was now placed between him and the computer screen and observed that it had a length of three meters and could be split into three distinct parts.

The first part was a rectangular box that stood perpendicular to the floor’s surface. From what Chen Chen could tell, this box acted like the processing center of a computer and contained all the hardware settings of the medical equipment.

The second part was a ring structure that was hollow in the center. Its outer diameter was coated in red whereas the inner diameter was coated in white. It was also the largest part of the medical equipment and looked to be incredibly heavy.

The third part was a platform where users could lie on.

The three parts were connected by the same foundation to form a singular unit. One could even see the label “Armadyne” written on it.

Armadyne was the name of the company that designed and produced the Medpod 3000. According to the movie’s settings, Armadyne had taken 102 years to develop the Medpod 3000 model from its first predecessor, the Medpod 1000.

The Medpod 1000 was just a piece of medical equipment that could assist doctors in surgery. The Medpod 2000, which was developed forty years later, then reached a level where it could cure cancer.

The latest model, the Medpod 3000, had then developed abilities that could virtually cure all diseases and illnesses except bringing someone back from death. This was the reason why after being exposed to radiation, the movie’s protagonist had to travel to the Elysium with only five days left to spare.

Seeing the equipment materializing before his eyes, Chen Chen could hardly contain his excitement. At that time, several Black Knights had already stepped forward to inspect the machine.

Upon their initial assessment, the connective port of the equipment was built below the seat. As Chen Chen was coercively retrieving the machine from his screen, the wires had since been disconnected. Fortunately, it was no big issue since they needed to only reconnect one wire.

Judging from the cable’s thickness, they were also able to determine the power and voltage needed to operate this machine.

Following that, the Black Knights, who had electrician’s programs installed into their chips began performing the duty of an electrical engineer.

Soon, the Medpod 3000 was fully connected to an electrical source whereas upon the flipping of the switch, the machine’s ring structure began emitting a gentle white light.

“It has been activated...”

Chen Chen did not shift his gaze away from the medical equipment and said, “A0012, take off your clothes and lie on top of it.”

Following Chen Chen’s instruction, the Black Knight immediately removed its clothes and crawled onto the platform.

Beside the platform was an operation interface. Chen Chen walked over and observed that there were four options available in the interface –

“Maintenance”, “Restoration”, “Inspection”, “Activation”.

Chen Chen gave it some good thoughts and decided to select the “Maintenance” option.

Then, the Medpod 3000’s ring structure emitted a low buzzing sound as if it was about to initiate its main processes. However, a red light soon beamed on the operation interface as the words “Unable to connect to the Internet” flashed across the screen.

“...”

Chen Chen was speechless. He had the Black Knight step off the platform and personally inspected the machine. However, he was unable to locate any Internet cable port.

Besides the main port to power the machine, there were no other ports available.

“In other words, does that mean we have to connect it via wireless Internet?”

Chen Chen pondered a bit more before lifting his head swiftly. “X, try hacking into the machine’s system.”

“Understood!”

Little X was always ready. Upon Chen Chen’s instruction, it quickly sprung into action. However, it was only a few seconds that passed before Little X answered, “Error in formatting. The Internet data frame used by this system was not the 802.11 local area network protocol, but another type of wireless network interface controller. I’m unable to access their system.”

chapters.title: Chapter 221: Elysium
chapters.body:
Chapter 221: Elysium

Chapter 221: Elysium

Little X was unable to access the machine’s system. It was not because the machine had certain security layers but that the wireless Internet protocol was simply incompatible.

It was similar to how a laptop was unable to connect to WIFI if its wireless network interface controller was damaged.

The fully automated medical platform was a terminal wireless Internet machine. Its protocol technology was not compatible with the IEEE 802.11 that existed in the real world. As a result, Little X was unable to gain access to its system.

“Upon verifying that there were no other connective ports, it’s deduced that the setting receives its input via wireless Internet signals.”

Little X analyzed and said, “However, since we still needed the machine to receive Internet signals, it must be compatible to receive signals in the form of electromagnetic waves. To solve this disconnection issue, there are probably two ways around –

“The first is to create a converter that’s capable of converting the IEEE 802.11 protocol that we have into an electromagnetic signal that’s compatible with the fully automated medical platform. By then, I’ll be able to access its system.

“The second is to physically replace the fully automated medical platform’s hardware into one that can connect to our wireless Internet signal.”

Chen Chen raised his eyebrows and said, “We can give it a try, but I doubt we’re able to open up this machine.”

He turned toward the Medpod 3000 and observed that the machine was seamless and fully integrated. It was difficult to spot any signs of manual assembly. It was evident that the craftsmanship of the dimension was remarkable.

“Besides, even if we were to forcefully pry the machine open, the complexity of its hardware is probably something that no modern technology can handle.”

It was not that Chen Chen was lacking confidence, but that the equipment was one that surpassed all modern scientific theories and understandings. After all, it was a machine with the precisive ability to manipulate the human body on a cellular level.

The machine’s abilities to prolong a human’s lifespan and restore bodily damage within a few seconds were indicative enough of its complexities.

This involved black technologies that could rival the existence of PK armor.

“We could shine an X-ray onto it and observe what the internal structures are made of.”

Chen Chen said, “Do you still remember that there was a scene in the movie – when the stowaway ship secretly landed on the Elysium, a woman and her daughter broke into one of the rich resident’s rooms. As she placed her heavily injured daughter onto the Medpod 3000, the Medpod 3000’s first course of action was not to attend to her injuries, but to scan the citizen mark on her wrist.”

Chen Chen raised his wrist and continued. “That’s to say that when the Medpod 3000 is treating a person, not only must it access their personal medical files, it also needs to access a central medical database and to rely on the support of a cloud server’s computational abilities.”

Chen Chen realized that this was trickier than he thought. He said, “If there are no other ways, I guess I’ll have to personally make a trip.”

“But it’s a dangerous move.”

Little X hastily tried to dissuade Chen Chen. “What’s wrong with sending the Black Knights over?”

“The Black Knights only possess simple intelligence. Perhaps it can surpass the average person when it comes to logical thinking and computational functions, but we’ve yet to test its ability to handle complex situations thoroughly.”

Chen Chen said, “And that’s only one of the reasons. If I enter the portal and encounter any danger, I could still return immediately and the risk would be manageable. The biggest risk is that the other party across the dimension would gain control over our portal, which is why I need to station guards and try to connect the portal to somewhere secluded on the other side.”

Then, Chen Chen gave it some deeper thought and said, “Hence, the best method is to first send two Black Knights over to test the water. If there’s danger, then they shall immediately return to safety. If not, they can throw an item over here.”

“Does that mean if there’s no item nor Black Knight coming from the other side, then it’ll imply that the Black Knights are in danger?”

Little X asked.

“That’s right.”

Chen Chen nodded and said, “If that’s the case, we’ll shut the tunnel down immediately. Otherwise, when we’re confident that the coast is clear, I’ll personally enter the portal and think of ways to solve the fully automated medical platform’s connectivity issues.”

“Are two Black Knights enough to keep you safe?”

Little X was still concerned. “Besides, one of them has to guard the portal, which means there’ll be lesser people to cover for you.”

“I’ll put on the PK armor this time.”

Chen Chen waved it off and said, “This equipment had been collecting dust ever since I acquired it. It’s time to put it into action. Judging from their combat abilities in the movies, this armor is enough to give me ample protections.”

“Understood.”

Little X said helplessly, “I’ve estimated the weight of Medpod 3000 to be approximately 1.5 tonnes, which means that you still have another 1.5 tonnes capacity left to travel through the portal. The combined weights of you and two Black Knights would only exhaust around 500 kilograms capacity. I’d still suggest bringing more manpower with you.”

Chen Chen nodded and said, “You’re right, now isn’t the time to conserve energy. Let’s do this instead – station five more fully equipped Black Knights to guard on this side of the portal. If reinforcement is needed, we’ll toss an item back as a signal.”

Chen Chen then looked around and proceeded to retrieve a couple of weights. “Let’s use these weights as our signals. A 50-gram weight would indicate that things are normal on the other side, a 100-gram weight means that we need reinforcement whereas a 200-gram weight means that you need to immediately seal the portal. How’s that?”

“But if we seal the portal...”

Little X’s tone was shaky. “After all, according to your hypothesis, the USB drive operates on principles that are based on the concept of parallel universes. If we seal and reopen the portal, will it still be the same portal? Will its position change to a different dimension?”

Chen Chen froze as what Little X said made sense. He had made multiple rounds of retrieving the NZT-48 drug from Limitless and STEM chips from Upgrade, and Chen Chen had acquired more than a hundred STEM chips. If each movie only corresponded to one parallel universe, where did all these STEM chips come from?

As quantum mechanics would dictate, there existed an infinite amount of parallel universes in this world.

If that was true, would that imply that the parallel universe the USB drive connected to was different each time?

If Chen Chen had crossed to the other side, it meant that once the portal was sealed, Chen Chen would be left stranded on another world, never to return...

“You’re right. We absolutely cannot shut the portal down.”

Chen Chen finally nodded and said, “If that’s the case, let’s just use the 50-gram and 100-gram weights. If there’s other information to be communicated, I’ll send a Black Knight over even if it means consuming more energies.”

After a prolonged discussion between a human and an AI, a consensus was finally reached. One of the Black Knights then returned to the Spire Experimental Base to retrieve Chen Chen’s PK armor while Chen Chen proceeded to download The Predator (2018) as he prepared to equip the other two Black Knights with PK armors too.

After all, it would be unwise for Chen Chen to neglect his safety just to conserve a little bit more energy. What more, one could never have enough of these tactical armors. It was a matter of time that they would come in handy.

An hour later, the Black Knight returned with his PK armor whereas Chen Chen had also acquired two sets of PK armors after downloading the movie.

As Chen Chen strapped one of the armor gear onto his arms, the sound of the gear interlocking was heard as the armguard shrunk to fit his arms nicely.

“Voom!”

An increasingly intense buzzing sound was heard as blue light flashed across the armor, spreading upward toward Chen Chen’s shoulders.

At the same time, Chen Chen frowned as he felt a burning pain radiating on his skin. Fortunately, the pain was still tolerable.

As the blue light gradually grew upward, the black armor soon covered Chen Chen’s body with the last being his face.

Then, as the armor was deployed over his face, Chen Chen’s vision first became pitch black before it was replaced by an infrared vision that was identical to what the Predators were seeing.

The other two Black Knights that were assigned to the mission had also put on their PK armors, bearing the same mecha resemblance as Chen Chen.

“Even if this armor is constructed by nanostructures, there’s probably an option to remove the helmet.”

The moment Chen Chen had that thought, the armor that was previously covering his head began splitting and revealing Chen Chen’s face.

“It’s true indeed.”

Now that his vision had resumed to normal, Chen Chen walked to his computer and played the Elysium movie.

However, he did not immediately jump into a portal. Instead, Chen Chen dragged the movie’s progress bar forward.

To land in a safe spot, Chen Chen had to station the portal in the Elysium instead of on Earth.

Whereas according to the USB drive’s characteristic, Chen Chen had to pause the movie exactly at a scene where the Elysium was featured to access the location.

It was similar to how Chen Chen must pause the frame where a piece of movie equipment was featured if he wished to acquire them.

When Chen Chen previously entered Resident Evil’s dimension, he immediately opened up the portal without pausing anywhere. Due to a lack of choice, the portal seemed to pick a random location to spawn at and it ended up in the train station.

After giving it some thought, Chen Chen then paused the movie three minutes and forty seconds in. The scene was filmed in a luxurious mansion where a blonde dressed in a red bikini was shown.

“Ok.”

Chen Chen flashed an OK gesture toward his back and clicked on the screen!

Then, Chen Chen saw a wave of darkness extended outward as it took after the shape of a dark whirlpool that was rotating in a clockwise direction.

A black tunnel had appeared.

Looking at the black hole, a realization hit Chen Chen. The tunnel was still a pitch-black in his vision.

It implied that despite his chromatography vision, the tunnel was still the same color.

While Chen Chen was immersed in his own thoughts, the two Black Knights armed in PK armors had vanished into the tunnel without any hesitation.

This time, Chen Chen needed to only wait for a minute.

After a minute had passed, a metal object suddenly emerged from the other side, creating a clunking sound as it landed on the floor.

It was the 50-gram weight that the Black Knights had brought with them.

Chen Chen then activated his face shield while three pairs of laser sights protruded from his back, putting him in an offensive preparation before leaping into the other side.

Chen Chen immediately felt his entire vision blanked out as he lost his sense of gravity. It felt like he was falling into an endless abyss.

However, just like his previous trip into Resident Evil’s dimension, the sensation was brief. Before Chen Chen could fully react, he saw the light at the end of the tunnel.

He landed in a dusty room that had French windows built all around its perimeter.

Meanwhile, two Black Knights were committed to guarding the entrance of the portal.

Chen Chen took a glance across his surroundings and observed that the room took after a minimalist design, which matched the interiors of the Elysium residence in the movie. Outside the room’s window, Chen Chen saw a hill that was a few hundred meters tall and had several mansions built on its valley.

In his room was also a Medpod 3000 platform that was covered in dust.

There could be no doubt. This was definitely the Elysium’s universe.

Chen Chen compared whatever he had seen to the movie’s imageries and concluded that they were almost identical, be it the scenery outside the window or the interior of the room. The USB drive had successfully transported him to the Elysium’s dimension and had even placed him at the exact location Chen Chen paused the movie in.

However, the timeline seemed to not be in sync with the movie...

Chen Chen kneeled and gently touched the floor’s surface.

The floor was covered and coated in dust that was half an inch thick. It indicated that no one had been here for at least a few years now.

Em...

Chen Chen paused for a moment before he attempted to switch on the Medpod 3000.

Unfortunately, no matter which button Chen Chen had pressed, the Medpod 3000 did not respond in any way.

Had the power supply been cut off?

Chen Chen pondered silently as he walked out of the room. He was greeted by an enormous and luxurious living room. Although the living room’s interiors were simple, it was evident that the design came from a highly technological society.

Chen Chen was standing in a bungalow mansion.

The only element that ruined the beauty was the thick layer of dust that had been coating every object of the mansion. It seemed like the mansion was abandoned a long time ago.

“It’s impossible that anyone would abandon their houses in the Elysium, unless this isn’t the right location...”

Chen Chen murmured. He walked carefully to the front door and gently opened the door.

Chen Chen now had a clear sight of the outside world.

The sun was still emitting its warm sunlight as usual. Far away, Chen Chen could see structures of gigantic pillars were erected as it extended all the way into the sky. Unlike Earth, the horizon was not the flat line that one typically observed on Earth. Instead, it extended upward.

That was to say that Chen Chen was now in an enormous ring structure.

Other than that, Chen Chen could also observe a blue planet suspended in the sky. It was none other than planet Earth itself.

All these observations could only point toward the fact that Chen Chen was indeed on the Elysium.

However, unlike the movie, the Elysium that Chen Chen was in looked dilapidated. Tall weeds grew everywhere while many mansions seemed to have fallen apart, leaving a wasteland in its place.

Could that mean that the Elysium had been abandoned?

Chen Chen activated his armor and began searching for any signs of lifeforms.

However, no matter where Chen Chen looked, the armor’s interface merely had “No Lifeforms Detected” written across its screen.

Suddenly, Chen Chen was struck by a horrifying thought. If what he thought was true...

Could it be a coincidence? Or was it fated?

Chen Chen took a deep breath and ordered the Black Knights to stay hidden and guard the portal entrance. He then turned to walk toward an enormous tower building.

That building was the Armadyne’s headquarter, which was also the central governance center of the Elysium.

chapters.title: Chapter 222: Intrusion
chapters.body:
Chapter 222: Intrusion

Chapter 222: Intrusion

As Chen Chen passed through several mansions, he would occasionally drop by for a quick inspection. Unfortunately, there were still no lifeforms to be found except for a few robot butlers scattered around.

Just like the houses on the ship, the robot butlers had been unpowered for a long time while vines grew all over their bodies.

It seemed like more than a century had passed. Even the robot’s bodies were beginning to rust.

“Looks like something happened here in an abrupt manner.”

Chen Chen made this conclusion after observing many past traces of a normal life. For instance, most mansion’s doors were still left open, which implied that its owners were living a normal life before having to abandon this place.

On the other hand, the robot butlers were also seen to be mostly suspended in a posture that resembled their former duties. Some were carrying cups of coffee whereas others were attending to an opened pot on the stove.

This implied that the residents of the Elysium had probably rushed to leave this ship. So urgent was the emergency that most of them could not bring their robot butlers with them...

Yet, something was still at odds with Chen Chen’s conclusion.

Even if a catastrophe had taken place on the Elysium, perhaps leakages of radioactive materials, poison gases, and even viruses – why was nobody sent from Earth to clean up this place after such a long time?

Could it be that an entire man-made celestial object was abandoned just like that?

Chen Chen once again set his sight onto Earth as he visibly became more perplexed.

There could only be one reason if no one from Earth had come onboard the Elysium when it was completely unguarded and that was if Earth itself was also in destruction...

“Does that mean that the root of the entire problem originates from Earth?”

Chen Chen had cracked his head trying to unravel this mystery but failed to arrive at a satisfactory answer. He decided to continue his journey toward the tall tower building.

Strictly speaking, the Elysium was not a huge place.

After all, it was a ring-like structure that had a diameter of sixty kilometers. At most, it could only fit the population of a city. It took only an hour’s walk before Chen Chen arrived at the base of the tower building.

As usual, vines grew rampantly near the entrance as it gradually spread into the main hall.

The entrance door of the building opened as two security robots were seen lying near two sides of the door. As veins wrapped around its bodies, the robots were also showing signs of decay.

“Did this government-owned tower suffer the same fate?”

Chen Chen was feeling more tense now. If even the security robots were not evacuated, the situation must have been dire.

“Kap, kap, kap...”

The six laser sights on Chen Chen’s armor were activated as he walked cautiously into the building.

Although there was probably little threat in the building now that so much time had passed, Chen Chen thought it was better to be safe than sorry.

As the power supply had long been cut off, it soon turned dark when Chen Chen walked deeper into the building. Fortunately, Chen Chen’s vision was unaffected even without a light source as the PK Armor came equipped with an infrared thermal vision.

As Chen Chen walked deeper into the building, he began to come across an increasing number of security robots. They were armed with Cousar Crowe rifles that could penetrate metal surfaces that were a few centimeters thick. These were incredibly destructive weapons.

Fortunately, these armed robots had long exhausted their powers and were laying dormant on the floor.

Chen Chen had yet to come across any human beings.

Not even a single corpse.

It could not be that these bodies had rotted completely as for as long as they were not buried, there would surely be traces to be found, such as leftover skulls or some dried out corpses.

Yet, Chen Chen had seen nothing close to that.

The government tower was different from the mansions on the Elysium. It had a structure that bore a closer resemblance to that of a spaceship. Most of the building’s walkways had airtight doors separating them into sections. As Chen Chen ventured further, he observed that most of these airtight doors were initially shut.

Due to the airtight doors’ ability to seal the rooms better, Chen Chen noticed far lesser signs of decays in the inner rooms. He had even seen a coffee mug resting on a table in one of the leisure rooms.

Of course, there were no more liquids left in the coffee mug. What was left were only the black sediments that solidified in the mug.

“Everyone has left at the same time. There were also no signs of panic.”

The more Chen Chen looked around the room, the stronger Chen Chen’s intuition became. His intuition told him that the people on the Elysium did not leave due to a disaster at all. Rather, they had all left quietly.

It did not matter whether they were drinking coffee, swimming or in the middle of other routine tasks. It was as if suddenly, everyone received a message at the same time and they all stopped their tasks and left without saying a word.

How was that possible...

Chen Chen felt uneasy and continued exploring. At last, he arrived at the entrance of the Elysium’s control center on the third floor.

This was the same room where the movie’s protagonist had overridden Elysium’s system.

The room was completely sealed off.

Luckily, this did not bother Chen Chen at all as he aimed a cannon on his right shoulder at the door. A loud blast was heard as a blue orb was momentarily seen!

“Boom!”

A large hole that was two meters in diameters instantly appeared on the steel door while molten steel could be seen around the circumference of the hole.

Such was the destructive ability of a plasma cannon.

Chen Chen waited for the molten steel to cool down before entering. To his surprise, the lights of the control room switched on on their own.

The once pitch-black room was suddenly illuminated by the increasing intensity of lights.

“Warning! An intruder has entered the premise! Initiating checking of defensive mechanisms.”

A female robotic voice was heard and was accompanied by a deafening sound of alarms throughout the entire building. “Warning, checking of defensive mechanism incomplete! Checking of defensive mechanism is incomplete!”

“Is this building tapping into its reserve power supply, similar to the Hive in Resident Evil?”

Chen Chen came to the operation interface and saw pieces of transparent boards that displayed his face and the words “Illegal Intruder” beside it.

He wondered if the system possessed the same intelligence as the Red Queen.

“Can you hear me talk?” Chen Chen raised his head and asked.

“Warning, warning! An intruder has been detected. Please initiate defensive mechanism immediately!”

The voice continued blasting its warnings while completely ignoring Chen Chen’s question.

“It only screams when met with problems. It definitely doesn’t have higher intelligence than the Red Queen.”

Chen Chen complained. After all, aside from the machines guarding outside, the movie did not feature any other signs of AI. Even then, this system was a pleasant surprise to Chen Chen.

After all, the existence of this intelligence system suggested that there was still a power system available. This meant that the system could easily be hacked by Little X.

Furthermore, if Little X could not breach into the system, Chen Chen could use the same method the movie’s protagonist, Max, used to override the system from the control center.

According to the movie’s plot, after overriding the system, one could easily allow anyone to become a citizen of Elysium. Indeed, the movie was concluded when the protagonist had given everyone on Earth citizenship in the Elysium.

Then, Chen Chen turned around and exited the main control center. As he made his way to the exit, the airtight doors would close and lock themselves behind him. Eventually, the entire tower building was completely locked and sealed.

chapters.title: Chapter 223: Where Did Everyone Go?
chapters.body:
Chapter 223: Where Did Everyone Go?

Chapter 223: Where Did Everyone Go?

Several hours later, Chen Chen returned to the tower once again, this time carrying a hard drive and a wire in his possession.

The hard drive contained a clone of Little X, X-112. On the other hand, the wire was the same wire that the movie’s protagonist used to connect his brain to the main control center. The wire was remodified so that Chen Chen could connect the hard drive to the control center’s system.

This time, it was no longer a smooth-sailing journey.

When Chen Chen returned to the Elysium’s government tower, he saw that the entire building had already been sealed from the outside.

Upon determining Chen Chen as a threat, the internal system had placed the entire tower in lockdown mode.

However, this did not deter Chen Chen at all. As he aimed his cannon onto the gate, the blue orb once again melted the front door, forming a large hole in the center.

“Warning, an intruder has been detected, please immediately deploy defensive mechanism!”

The alarm sounded once again.

Chen Chen was already familiar with the route this time. Once he accessed the building through the front door, Chen Chen soon arrived in the main control center and connected the hard drive into the main control center’s system.

Within a couple of minutes, X-112 then overwrote all the system data with its own. The red light on the hard drive drive immediately turned green.

This was a setting Chen Chen had put in place as a form of insurance. If the hard drive emitted green light, it would indicate that X-112 had succeeded. If it was a red light, it meant that X-112 had failed to infiltrate the system.

If the hard drive first had the red light, then the light got turned off for a moment before turning to green, it would indicate an unpleasant scenario. Usually, it meant that the other party’s system had infiltrated X-112 and taken its place.

At that moment, the once robotic voice now turned into the innocent voice of a young girl. “Sir Commander, I’m X-112, we meet again.”

“Long time no see.”

Chen Chen smiled. Ever since X-112 had previously infiltrated the Red Queen, this was the second time Chen Chen worked alongside X-112.

“I’ve replaced the entire control system of the Elysium. I’m awaiting your orders now.” X-112 continued.

“Are you able to connect to the wireless Internet? I’d like to see what’s happening on Earth.”

Chen Chen asked.

“Regrettably, Sir Commander, the Earth’s Internet has been disabled. I’m unable to do so,” X-112 explained.

“No surprise there. Is Earth in trouble?”

Chen Chen raised his eyebrows. He already long had suspicions that the situation on Earth was similar to the desolated scenes on the Elysium. Otherwise, it would not explain how no one had come onboard this structure for so long.

“In that case, help me check the main control system’s daily log.”

“Understood. The system’s daily log has been extracted... Today is 23 March 2180 and the time now is 3:30 PM. The last update on the daily log was made on 20 July 2165 at 11:59 PM.”

Chen Chen now had a clearer picture of the situation. The timeline of the movie was set in 2154, which meant that 11 years after the event of the movie in 2165. Everyone who was on the Elysium had suddenly left.

As no one was commandeering the structure, the daily log was only updated until that day. Then, the entire Elysium went into hibernation mode till Chen Chen arrived in the 2180 when the system was reactivated after Chen Chen blasted the door open.

In other words, everything happened on the eventful date of 20 July 2165.

Chen Chen then asked, “X-112, did the original residents of Elysium leave this place? Where did they go?”

“Negative. The original residents of Elysium have never left.”

X-112 answered, “Their position is at 2111 Basin...”

Then, an image was displayed on the glass screen featuring a large crater on the Elysium. White objects were piling up in the crater.

The Elysium had emulated the geographical landscapes of the Earth when it was built. There were mountain valleys, rivers, and even basins that were incorporated into the structure.

However, when Chen Chen had a clear sight as to what those white objects were, he felt chills down his spine.

Those white objects were skeletal remains of probably tens of thousands of people amassing in the basin...

“This is the aerial view of 2111 Basin.”

X-112 explained further, “Upon verifying the citizenship marks that were in everyone’s bodies, I can conclude that all of Elysium’s residents are in there.”

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes and took a deep breath. “Is this a massacre?”

“It’s not.”

X-112 began playing a video recording.

Chen Chen saw that the video was a CCTV recording of the leisure room in this tower. The timestamp on the upper right corner indicated that it was 20 July 2165 at 11:58 PM.

In the video, three staff on the night shift, comprising of two men and a woman, were seen drinking their coffees in the leisure room. Although there were no sound recordings, Chen Chen could tell that they were having an enjoyable conversation. There was nothing strange about the recording yet.

Then, as soon as the time reached 11:59 PM, one of the staff who was gesturing wildly suddenly froze as he slowly lowered his hands down. He then stood up and almost mechanistically began walking out the door.

The other two staff were behaving in similar manners. It was like the trio had suddenly been controlled by a machine. They walked out of the leisure hall and made their way to the main hallway.

“Follow them,” Chen Chen instructed.

Then, the recording switched scenes and Chen Chen could observe that many others had joined the trio in their marches.

“Play it at three times the normal speed,” Chen Chen instructed again.

Then, as the video played faster, the crowd soon completely walked out of the screen before the video was replaced by the recording of another camera that was situated further in front of the previous camera.

At last, Chen Chen saw up to a thousand people exiting the government tower and walked unanimously in the same direction.

However, the recording did not end there as there were surveillance cameras placed throughout public areas in the Elysium. It was there where Chen Chen witnessed a magnificent phenomenon.

Like herds of ships, tens of thousands of people walked across the surveillance cameras. Among the huge crowd were middle-aged men in their business suits, beautifully dressed young women, housewives in pajamas who seemed to have just woken up, and even naked men who still had bubbles foaming on their heads...

In short, these people had mechanical facial expressions that were void of any emotions. There was no resistance nor reluctance in their actions. It was as if someone was controlling them remotely as the crowd behaved similarly to a group of walking corpses and puppets.

“Is their destination 2111 Basin?”

Chen Chen asked.

“Yes.”

X-112 played the continued fast-forwarding the video while it continued tracing the movement of the government staff. When the video resumed its normal speed, the timestamp indicated that it was already noontime the next day.

At last, tens of thousands of people could be seen walking into 2111 Basin. Chen Chen noticed that once a person stepped foot onto the basin, it felt like they had accomplished their final mission and allowed the latecomers to push them forward, squeezing them into a deeper spot of the basin...

What a creepy sight...

chapters.title: Chapter 224: Sculptures
chapters.body:
Chapter 224: Sculptures

Chapter 224: Sculptures

Half an hour later, an aircraft coated in green camouflage raced across Elysium’s sky and appeared over 2111 Basin.

This was a military aircraft. It would be an exaggeration to say that it was a spacecraft because it did not have the ability to navigate in outer space. It could only fly briefly at high altitudes and outer space, with a maximum travel distance of 36,000 kilometers.

This was the distance between Elysium and the surface of the earth.

Nonetheless, this was sufficient for those living on Elysium. Currently, the four jets of the aircraft shifted to point downward. Following that, the aircraft was suspended at a high altitude before it descended little by little.

At last, the military camouflaged aircraft stopped at the edge of the basin. The gull-wing doors were slowly raised.

Chen Chen strode out of the aircraft, wearing the PK Armor.

X-112 had already tested Elysium’s environment. Other than the standard bacteria, it was as clean as his own toilet. The air composition posed no problems as well, with just a slightly higher percentage of oxygen.

Even so, Chen Chen did not remove his mask since he had learned that there was an unknown threat in this dimension.

After exiting the aircraft, Chen Chen looked toward 2111 Basin.

Strictly speaking, this basin had the topography of a valley surrounded by mountains on all sides. However, at this time, the valley was full of corpses that stretched as far as the eye could see. These corpses stacked up like a mountain peak, reaching dozens of meters in height.

These corpses had wasted away into a heap of bones, which had become nourishment for the thick vines that covered them.

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes as he observed the endless swathe of bones. “Have you found the reason? Why did they act in that manner?”

“I couldn’t find any reason.”

X-112’s voice came from the communications system of the aircraft behind him. “It seems that the Elysium government has imposed certain restrictions on artificial intelligence. The main AI here doesn’t have the ability to think and reason and I can only speculate based on the clues it already knows.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Then let me sum it up. On 20 July 2165 at 23:59, all the citizens of Elysium who lived here simultaneously lost control over their personality and consciousness for unknown reasons.

“The indication that these people had lost their personality and consciousness was that they stopped any work they were doing, turned around, left their houses, and walked toward 2111 Basin.

“Upon arriving, they seemed to have completed their mission. They simply stood here, motionless, until they died...”

Chen Chen looked up and asked, “Have I made any errors in my summary?”

“No, Sir Commander,” X-112 replied.

“Very well. Then do you think that all of this resembles a program?”

Chen Chen paced back and forth. “If you think of humans as robots, human instincts such as eating and sleeping can be understood as a kind of program. That abnormal occurrence was due to a hacker’s attack. Wouldn’t that explain it easily?

“As the hacker broke into the mainframe, a special command was implanted in everyone at the same time. The only request of the command was for everyone to stop everything they were doing and come to the 2111 Basin.

“Furthermore, this kind of command possessed the highest authority that cannot be disobeyed. It even exceeded the survival instinct of human beings. There was no room for disobedience. Therefore, what happened, happened...”

At this point, Chen Chen frowned. “However, the one behind the implanted order did not give them a second order, so everyone could only wait indefinitely. However, because humans and machines are different, in just a few days, this group of people died due to a lack of energy intake.”

“I understand what you mean.”

X-112 responded, “You think that this was a sort of cyber attack?”

“Yes, what happened in Elysium was like being hacked. Apart from that scenario, I don’t know what else can explain all this.”

After speaking, Chen Chen walked to those bones and gently picked up a yellow skull. He noticed the operation marks on the skull and smiled immediately. “If my guess is correct, the brains of those in Elysium have been implanted with something like a smart chip, right?”

“It’s true that there was something like that.”

X-112 answered, “But only about one-third of the people have been implanted with brain chips. Still, these chips were designed to facilitate interactions between machines and humans, and didn’t have the function of manipulating thoughts.”

Chen Chen’s hand froze when he heard this. Following that, he picked up another skull and looked at it. Indeed, this skull was smooth and shiny, without any marks.

When he saw this, Chen Chen’s expression stiffened. “In that case, my hypothesis is incorrect?”

After thinking about it, Chen Chen asked again, “Are you sure that the citizens across the entire Elysium stopped their movements in just an instant?”

“According to the surveillance videos I’ve investigated, it can be determined that it happened to everyone at 23:59,” X-112 replied.

“Then, let me try another explanation.”

Chen Chen thought for a while and added. “Are you sure that all citizens of Elysium suffered from abnormal behavior on 20 July 2165 at 23:59? Or was it a successive occurrence such as some occurring at 59 minutes 01 seconds, some at 59 minutes 02 seconds, and so on?”

X-112 paused and then continued. “I’ve adjusted and compared the times in the videos and found no abnormalities. The citizens in all of Elysium had acted strangely at the same time.”

“What about the Earth?”

Chen Chen asked, “When that abnormality occurred in Elysium, were they in contact with Earth at that time?”

“According to the log report, the same incident happened on Earth at that time.”

Meanwhile, the 3D projection on the armed aircraft’s screen displayed images from various cameras on Earth.

From the images, one could see that when the phenomenon happened in Elysium, the same thing had befallen the humans on Earth at almost the same time.

“Does this mean that the humans on Earth are probably all dead? It’s not surprising that even after so many years, Earth hasn’t sent anyone to investigate this...”

Chen Chen’s pupils contracted as he ordered, “Break down and compare the videos from Earth and the videos from Elysium according to the number of frames!”

As X-112 occupied the main control computer of Elysium, this level of computing was not a problem. Soon, X-112 replied again, “Sir Commander, the answer has already been obtained.

“According to the frame-by-frame analysis of the surveillance videos with a 480 frame rate, the time when humans on Earth suffered the abnormality was 0.12 seconds after the humans in Elysium, and the time when humans on the other side of Earth acted abnormally was 0.3 seconds after the humans in Elysium.”

Upon hearing this, Chen Chen suddenly revealed an expression of realization. After all, one should know that the distance between Elysium and Earth was 36,000 kilometers and the speed of light was about 300,000 kilometers per second, which meant that light traveled from Elysium to the Earth’s surface in about 0.12 seconds.

This number was exactly the same as the time difference calculated by X-112.

“Travelling at the speed of light?”

Chen Chen looked thoughtful. “So, that’s it...”

X-112 continued. “According to my investigations, I found that the closer one was to 2111 Basin, the sooner this happened.”

“Understood.”

Chen Chen could not help but look at the ground beneath his feet and said in deep thought, “X-112, send me a construction robot to dig up this place. Even if I have to dig through the crust, I have to know what’s inside!”

“Sir Commander, according to the safety regulations, if you stay in Elysium’s world, the level of risk will rise to more than 10%. Please return to reality first.”

However, X-112 did not immediately agree to Chen Chen’s order and instead made a suggestion.

Chen Chen was stunned. “Has the risk risen to 10%? How did you calculate that?”

“Because I put together your reasoning, Sir Commander. The demise of the humans in Elysium was probably related to 2111 Basin.”

X-112 replied, “The excavation of 2111 Basin is likely to trigger that event again. According to my estimation, once the event is triggered again, your chances of dying are extremely high.”

“Very well. Your reasoning isn’t wrong.”

Chen Chen nodded, tossed away the skull in his hand, and turned to the aircraft. “In this case, send me a copy of all data on the Medpod 3000 and provide a decoding method based on the data. I’ll leave two Black Knights here. One of them will be deployed to you and the other is responsible for guarding the portal. All you have to do is restore Elysium’s power system and control the construction machinery here to dig through 2111 Basin.”

“Understood. Just leave this to me,” X-112 answered.

Thus, after that, Chen Chen was sent back to the government building and was guided by X-112 to a 3D printing room. Soon, Chen Chen was witnessing the printing room swiftly print out a USB drive.

It was a white USB drive.

“This is all the scientific and technological information that exists in Elysium’s dimension, including exoskeleton technology, robots, pulse rifles, close-range aircraft, the Medpod 3000, and so on.”

X-112 explained.

“Well done indeed!” Chen Chen put away the USB flash drive. Not even he could conceal his delighted surprise.

This time, obtaining all information on the technology of an entire civilization in one fell swoop could be said to be Chen Chen’s greatest harvest so far.

Following that, Chen Chen was sent back to the villa where the USB drive’s passage was located. Without any hesitation, Chen Chen returned to reality immediately.

After being disinfected, Chen Chen exited the disinfection room and removed the PK Armor with a contemplative look.

“Did something happen inside?”

Little X asked.

“Yes, I found something very interesting in the world of Elysium.”

Chen Chen was pensive. “If my guess is correct, it’s better to open as few portals as possible from now on...”

After thinking for a while, Chen Chen handed the USB flash drive to a Black Knight and ordered, “X, download the data in this USB flash drive and at the same time, organize a list of the data for me.”

“Is this information from Elysium’s dimension? Understood.”

“One more thing.”

Chen Chen gave it some thought and added again. “If the two Black Knights on the other side of the passage return, remember to isolate them. If you find anything wrong, report it to me as soon as possible.”

“Got it!”

Although Little X did not fully understand, it still replied quickly.

After he had settled everything, Chen Chen turned and left the substation, and stayed at the lounge of the nuclear power plant.

Two days went by.

Just when Chen Chen was about to leave the nuclear power plant temporarily until there was news from across the portal, Little X finally brought some news.

“Sir Godfather, a Black Knight has returned from the passage with a video file.”

“I’ll be right over.”

Chen Chen immediately returned to the substation upon hearing this. In a room next door, Chen Chen saw the video file that was mentioned.

It was a USB drive, similar to the one Chen Chen had brought back.

After thinking about it, Chen Chen inserted the USB drive into the computer. The computer screen flashed and a laboratory full of cold glossy walls appeared on the screen.

The camera first panned around, seeming to display the various equipment in the laboratory. According to Chen Chen’s memory, if he had not guessed wrongly, this should be a materials laboratory. The apparatuses here were used for experiments with materials.

However, the final image on the screen was fixed on three sculptures of human faces.

These three human face sculptures were about thirty centimeters long and twenty centimeters wide. They were almost the same size as ordinary human heads, but their shape was extremely peculiar.

In terms of style, these sculptures were like shoddy products made by beginners, without any refinement or beauty to speak of. Not only that, the lines that made up the sculptures’ patterns were also extremely simple, and could even be described as minimalistic.

The eyes of the first sculpture from the left were diamond-shaped and seemed to be closed tightly while its ears were pointed and long, standing up straight like a rabbit’s.

The eyes of the middle sculpture were opened but it seemed to only have a pair of eye sockets that sunk deep into the face as if the person in the sculpture did not have eyeballs.

As for the sculpture at the end, both its ears and eyes were normal. However, its mouth was wide opened and it looked like a black hole. Chen Chen felt an inexplicable chill just by looking at it through the video.

“Sir Commander, I’m X-112. What you see now are the three sculptures I dug out of 2111 Basin, which were the only objects found there.

“I checked all records regarding these three sculptures. The earliest record of them was their discovery in 2025 in the world of Elysium.”

The childish voice of X-112 was the same as that of Little X. The only difference was that it was more unflustered. At this point, Chen Chen was listening to it saying, “In 2025, the three sculptures were first uncovered in Elysium’s dimension by the wealthy North American collector, Gross. He held a ball especially for these three sculptures and exhibited them proudly at the ball.

“The collector claimed that the sculptures were dug out of a gold mine and were at least several million years old, but no one believed him.

“Subsequently, Gross, who was doubted by everyone, took the three sculptures to an expert for appraisal.

“However, after tomographic and isotope identification using the relevant instruments, the final result was that these three sculptures had only been carved in the past year. Namely, they were created recently. All at once, Gross became a laughing stock in the collectors’ circle.

“Following that, over a hundred years later, with the establishment of Elysium, the descendants of Gross moved here from Earth. It can be confirmed, from the records of transported items at the time, that these three sculptures were indeed brought over by descendants of Gross.”

X-112 continued. “These are the only records of these three sculptures, but in my inspection, I’ve also found something truly strange...”

chapters.title: Chapter 225: List Of Technology
chapters.body:
Chapter 225: List Of Technology

Chapter 225: List of Technology

“I used the electron spin resonance method to test the three sculptures again.”

X-112 said, “According to my calculations, they’re just ordinary stone sculptures made of calcium carbonate and silica. But no matter how I used ultrasonic or electromagnetic waves, I couldn’t scan the internal structure of the sculptures. It’s as if their insides are made of lead blocks.

“Since I can’t discern their internal structure, I simply measured when they were engraved and used the radiation-induced effect as a test method to determine their age. At last, I determined that their engraved age was still within the past one year.”

Chen Chen was astounded when he heard this. In this case, did it not mean that these three sculptures could rejuvenate themselves? Were the sculptures alive?

X-112 continued to report. “But the problem is that these three sculptures were discovered underground in 2111 Basin. They have been buried for at least 15 years. However, Elysium only retains 26,280 hours of its surveillance records, which is equivalent to three years. I’ve checked all the monitored records but I can’t find when the three sculptures were buried here, which means that the sculptures have been buried for at least 18 years.”

As he watched the video sent by X-112, Chen Chen displayed a thoughtful look.

These three stone sculptures reminded him of the maxim of the “three wise monkeys”.

The three wise monkeys comprised three monkeys squatting on the ground, one covering his eyes, one covering his mouth, and the third covering his ears.

Legend had it that the three wise monkeys originated from the Analects: “See no evil, speak no evil, and hear no evil.” The implicit meaning was “Don’t see what shouldn’t be seen, don’t hear what shouldn’t be heard, and don’t say what shouldn’t be said”.

However, the three sculptures on the screen were different compared to the adorable, innocent appearance of the three wise monkeys.

The three sculptures were much simpler and cruder than the three monkeys in terms of lines and aesthetics.

Nonetheless, it was because they were too primitive and crude, that they induced an eerie sensation. Whether it was the black gaping eyes or that bottomless abyss of a mouth, it provoked the deepest fears of the human heart.

It was just like 2111 Basin. No matter how one looked at it, the demise of the humans in Elysium was related to these three sculptures...

However, Chen Chen could not imagine how these three sculptures accomplished this. Why did they suddenly activate in 2165 and wipe out all of humankind?

What did the Gross family discover and why did they bury them in 2111 Basin?

Chen Chen carefully pondered the information he had already obtained, but the information was still too scarce to allow further deductions.

After thinking for a while, Chen Chen simply took out a recorder and said into the microphone, “X-112, I’ve received your message. Please follow my instructions.

“First, I permit you to use any means to disassemble the three sculptures and also direct destruction if necessary.

“Second, collect all information on the centuries-long history of the Gross family and send it to me.

“Third, command an aircraft to explore Earth and report all the results of the exploration along with the information on the Gross family to me.”

After some thought, Chen Chen added. “Finally, if there’s no progress in the research on the sculptures, give up on Elysium’s dimension and re-transport your program back to the hard disk. The Black Knight will bring it back to reality.”

After saying all this, Chen Chen handed the recorder to the Black Knight beside him.

Next, this recorder would be thrown into the passage by the Black Knight and transmitted to X-112 on the other side.

After doing all this, Chen Chen felt an involuntary weight in his chest.

He had not felt this when he first used the USB drive, but as he used it with increasing frequency, Chen Chen started to feel a growing sense of unease.

True, it seemed that the USB flash drive only recognized films relating to science and technology, and only movies that conformed to the logic of reality could be stored in the USB drive. However, it was because of this rule that the three horror films in the USB drive worried Chen Chen to no end.

This was because no one knew what these three horror films represented...

At first, Chen Chen had thought that as long as these three horror films were not played, there would be no problems, but the film Elysium had completely overturned Chen Chen’s expectations.

At this point, Chen Chen had only opened the portal to three movies —

Chronicle, Resident Evil, and Elysium.

The corresponding universes of the three movies made Chen Chen think of a sinister implication...

This was because the three dimensions he visited had all suffered from the collapse of human civilization...

Resident Evil was fine since it was a movie about the apocalypse in the first place, but what about Chronicle and Elysium?

A coincidence...

Or an inevitability?

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes. Although he did not want to admit it, at this moment, he was already walking on the edge between life and death. Furthermore, this path was unavoidable.

To ascertain whether all of this was just a coincidence or an inevitability, Chen Chen must open more channels and travel to more dimensions to prove all of this.

However, this sort of behavior was extremely dangerous.

Just like the human civilization in the world of Elysium — although the technology had stagnated due to the severe social polarization, it still surpassed reality by more than a century. Even so, this civilization had quietly perished in one ordinary night...

Even a toad would jump twice before it died, yet an entire civilization of a dimension did not even have the chance to cry out.

The cause of their demise was even more absurd. It turned out to be the doing of three sculptures, whose basic principles were still unknown.

Chen Chen had doubts, for the first time, about the worldview he had firmly subscribed to.

“Is there really something beyond science in this world?”

Chen Chen murmured in a low voice. However, he then sneered, popping an NZT-48 pill into his mouth.

All at once, Chen Chen’s mind was steadied and his once-scattered thoughts settled down.

Countless thoughts flashed across his mind. Chen Chen still remembered that Kelvin, a famous physicist who left an indelible mark upon history, once predicted that airplanes heavier than air would never fly. He also thought that X-rays were a senseless cheap trick, and that radio was useless...

Furthermore, Rutherford, who discovered the atomic structure, believed that humans would never be able to create an atomic bomb.

The great philosopher Comte once predicted that science could never determine the composition of stars because, in that era, it was impossible for humans to travel to other planets to perform a survey.

It turned out that within a few years, scientists could prove that the sun was composed of hydrogen by analyzing the spectrum of sunlight and there was no need to fly over there at all...

As another example, Nobel Prize winner Michaelson once said that there was nothing new to be discovered in physics. Subsequently, four years later, quantum mechanics was born and had now become a main discipline of physics, thus illustrating that it was possible for the laws of physics to change.

These examples all proved without exception that it was not religion and theology that limited the development of science, but the arrogance of using what was known to explain the unknown.

Therefore, was it true that the demise of humankind in Elysium’s dimension did not conform to science... Or was it just not in line with contemporary scientific knowledge?

Chen Chen shook his head and stopped thinking about this.

After Chen Chen finished reading the message X-112 had sent from across the channel, he received an update from Little X again.

“Sir Godfather, I’ve sorted out the information you brought back from the Elysium dimension.”

After Little X spoke, a Black Knight came in lugging a stack of A4 papers that was as thick as a Chinese dictionary.

“This is the technical data from Elysium’s dimension?”

Chen Chen took the information and flipped through absentmindedly.

“No, that’s just the table of contents,” Little X answered.

“...”

Chen Chen just flipped through it casually, but his eyes suddenly lit up.

As this stack of data included almost every technology that human civilization could ever come up with, he just had to flip to a page to come across a new method to manufacture an alloy called “high-displacement metal”.
