However, the discovery of a normal-temperature superconductor would render low-temperature facilities worthless, saving the user the cost of maintaining these facilities as well as the capital required for constructing such facilities.

That was only one of the many reasons. A normal-temperature superconductor would also make the replication of quantum entanglement much simpler, paving the way toward the invention of a quantum calculator.

What more, it would also pave the way for new technologies that could manipulate nuclear fusion...

The discovery of such material was enough to trigger the Fourth Industrial Revolution in human civilization.

chapters.title: Chapter 240: Huge Technical Advancements
chapters.body:
Chapter 240: Huge Technical Advancements

Chapter 240: Huge Technical Advancements

To Chen Chen, the discovery of the normal-temperature superconductor would solve many technical challenges that he faced.

For instance, the electromagnetic levitation that was effortlessly produced by the normal-temperature superconductor would allow the frictionless bearing of the T-800 to perpetually be in pristine condition, indicating a huge upgrade for Chen Chen’s robot.

Take another instance, the construction of an electromagnetic weapon, a weapon that utilized zero-resistance magnetic field generated by a superconductor. Previously, superconductors could only be stored under low temperatures. Its strict environmental requirement had rendered the material extremely inflexible and it was unsuitable to be used to construct a small, convenient electromagnetic weapon.

However, the discovery of a normal-temperature superconductor meant that a light electromagnetic weapon was now possible, perhaps even reaching the size of one’s palm. Meanwhile, the special property of the superconductor would remove any concern of the gun’s coil burning out due to high electrical resistance.

In the Elysium movie, it was precisely the discovery of a normal-temperature superconductor that led to the invention of various electromagnetic weapons such as the Kusal Klaus submachine gun, Kusal Crow Air Scavenger, Ascari Kaman railgun, and Ascari electromagnetic gun.

With this normal-temperature superconductor, Chen Chen now had the ambition to research a nuclear fusion reactor and a Large Hadron Collider. It was quite possible that the world’s first nuclear fusion reactor would be created in Chen Chen’s hands...

The ceramic chemical factory that produced the superconductor was merely one of Chen Chen’s many factories that operated with black technology. Opposite this factory was a fuel cell factory.

The factory had combined the techniques that were derived from the Elysium dimension and the technologies from the Avengers to produce a highly-efficient hydrogen fuel cell.

Other electronics factories and machinery factories had their research objectives too. Some were tasked with researching high displacement metals while others were tasked with researching inertial dampers. From the outside, these factories seemed just like any other ordinary building. However, these factories were all monitored closely by Little X while drones and closed-circuit cameras were placed throughout the industrial area. Fully-armored Black Knights were also stationed secretly in each corner to protect the facilities at all times.

However, there were only limited Black Knights to go around. Hence, Chen Chen had long-established another factory to research a drone that was featured in the Elysium dimension.

It was a surveillance drone that was called a “UAV” and it took after the shape of a flying disc. It was equipped with noise cancellation technology and only needed a low amount of energies to power itself. The drone was featured only in the opening scene of the movie and its name could only be found in the movie’s settings record.

The production of a movie typically began with developing a comprehensive worldview of the movie’s settings. For example, in the Avengers series, official features such as frictionless bearing, inertial dampers, and a battery that could last for more than 120 years could be found on its Wikipedia page.

To be able to mass-produce this surveillance drone, Chen Chen had gone on to retrieve two such drones from the Elysium as reference models. Unfortunately, due to certain technical limitations, it would still be some time before Chen Chen could produce an exact replica.

Factories that were tasked with classified research were usually built toward the back of the industrial area. Chen Chen had also discreetly built a line of defense, in which all secret factories were stationed behind it. On the other side of the line was the open area that included electronics assembly factories that housed more than one hundred thousand workers.

The electronic assembly factories were built for two purposes. The first was to allow the other secret factories to hide in plain sight, the second was to provide employment opportunities to the local residents so that Blacklight Biotechnology’s influence could continue to grow in Namibia.

Ever since the Blacklight virus attacked Namibia, the country’s population was down to less than two million people. The employment of more than one hundred thousand workers was equivalent to employing ten percent of the country’s population.

This became an important bargaining chip in every negotiation with the Namibian government. Should the Namibian government attempt to forcefully drive the corporation out of the country, they had to consider the consequences heavily.

An attack against Blacklight Biotechnology would not only bring severe economic impact but also render ten percent of the country’s population immediately jobless.

After departing from the ceramic chemical factory that produced the yttrium barium copper oxide composite, Chen Chen sat in his car.

Cheng Cao, who had also just joined Chen Chen in his car, asked, “Boss, would you like to take a look at the fuel cell factory?”

“There’s no need for that. Let’s head back to the Spire Experimental Base.”

Chen Chen gestured to Cheng Cao. He had already received a message from Little X which informed him about the latest update from the fuel cell factory in his daily Eco Science City report. As of now, the professors who were in charge of the fuel cell factory were already heading to the Spire Experimental Base where they would submit a detailed report to Chen Chen.

Soon, Chen Chen had arrived back at the Spire Experimental Base where he was greeted by Professor Joseph.

Half a year ago while Chen Chen was expanding his company into the heavy industry, he had invited a large group of engineering professors to interview, whom he ended up hiring a few after lengthy considerations.

These professors had also been implanted with God chips and were granted access into God’s Forbidden Zone. Through this, they were given exposure to the most sophisticated technologies as well as had their lifespan potentially extended.

It was a win-win situation.

“Mr. Chen, we bring good news. We’ve followed the model you provided and have successfully created a new version of a fuel cell.”

Then, he placed several cylindrical objects that were glowing in silver-white light on the table. The cylindrical objects varied in thickness.

Chen Chen received those objects and asked, “What are the properties of these hydrogen cells?”

“Hydrogen cells that are 1.5 cm in diameter and 10 cm in lengths could release an electrical potential that reaches up to 200V whereas those with a 3 cm diameter and 20 cm length could reach up to 400V. The electrical potential of these fuel cells could be prorated via their sizes and their thermal efficiencies are at 80%, meeting your requirement.”

Professor Joseph answered.

“Not bad at all!”

Chen Chen was overjoyed. These hydrogen cells were a form of power generation device that produced electrical energy through electrochemical reactions between its fuel and oxidant. Theoretically, such fuel cells could achieve a thermal efficiency rate that was close to 100%.

The technique and model Chen Chen provided was a hydrogen fuel cell from the T-800. The battery had a thermal efficiency of 95%. Although Joseph had yet to produce a fuel cell that could reach that level of efficiency, it was to be expected. Part of the reason was due to a lack of material.

One of the most important structures of a fuel cell was its electrolyte diaphragm. Its main function was to separate the oxidant and the reducing agent of the fuel cell as well as to conduct the cell’s ions. The thinner the electrolyte diaphragm, the better. As Chen Chen could not reach the technical level of that in the Avengers, it was not surprising that his fuel cell could not reach that level of efficiency.

Besides that, to fit the fuel cell into his weapon, Chen Chen had to eliminate the hydrogen collector to reduce the fuel cell’s volume. This hydrogen collector was an important element in the Avenger.

However, to Chen Chen, the fuel cell could always be changed manually. Hence, the collector did not play too big of a role for his purpose. At most, the elimination of the fuel cell would only reduce its endurance power.

Now that Chen Chen possessed a normal-temperature superconductor and a hydrogen fuel cell that could support an enormous amount of electrical voltage, there was only one essential material left – a high displacement metal.

Once these three materials were ready, Chen Chen could then use the superconductor material as his coil, the hydrogen fuel cells as an electrical source, and the high displacement metal as a rifle, to mass-produce the destructive electromagnetic weapons that were featured in the Elysium.

chapters.title: Chapter 241: New Combat Suit Model
chapters.body:
Chapter 241: New Combat Suit Model

Chapter 241: New Combat Suit Model

As time passed by, it was soon the end of 2023.

This year, the Earth Federation had gone through multiple disasters. The onslaught of the Blacklight virus began back in May while the first vaccine was developed by Blacklight Biotechnology in July. This was then followed by a series of clinical trials and then mass production and the pandemic was finally under control in October.

It was also at the beginning of October when Blacklight Biotechnology announced three special anticancer medicines. These medicines were subsequently introduced to the drug inspection committees of multiple continents and thus began another round of tedious clinical trials.

Unlike the African Continent, other continents had stricter requirements toward their medications during the clinical trial. There were at least three periods of phases in those trials, which meant that Blacklight Biotechnology needed to wait at least three years before their medicines could be sold on the counter.

Regardless, these were still miracle drugs that were capable of curing cancer. They had managed to reduce the expected waiting period to three years after receiving support from multiple financial groups. If it were any other common medications, the waiting period would typically require at least ten years.

This was also the year where Blacklight Biotechnology revealed its true potential.

It began with the construction of the world-renowned Eco Science City, followed by its acquisition of the largest security company in Africa, Tara Private Security Company. Following that, the corporation played an important role during the Blacklight virus pandemic, being the first entity to discover the virus as well as the first to research an effective vaccine.

Toward the year-end, Blacklight Biotechnology had continued its victory streak by announcing three special anticancer medicines at once.

This year, Blacklight Biotechnology had brought many surprises to the world.

After much international coverage, Blacklight Biotechnology’s reputation had reached its highest point. Before the dust of the pandemic could even settle in Africa, thousands of tycoons arrived at Eco Science City in their private jets with the intention of undergoing age-reversal therapies in their institution.

The rise of Blacklight Biotechnology had reached an unstoppable pace.

After three months of research and development in the Spire Experimental Base, the second batch of T-version Black Knights had also been launched.

These Black Knights had been through several rounds of genetic tunings by Hannibal Caesar and Li Lei’s team. The Black Knight’s heights had been successfully limited to a range from 1.7 meters to 1.8 meters and their sizes were also limited to that of an average human being.

After their size compressions, the Black Knights’ strengths had been reduced from a range of 9000-10000N to 2000-2500N, which was the equivalent of carrying up to 250 kilograms on a single hand. Despite the strength reduction, it was more than enough for the Black Knight’s functions.

In terms of speed, their sprint speeds were reduced from an average of 90 kilometers per hour down to 40 kilometers per hour, whereas its endurance was increased, being able to sprint at its maximum speed for approximately 800 meters.

This implied that the newly produced T-version Black Knights had the sprinting speed of Usain Bolt as well as the endurance of a professional marathoner.

There was no longer any need to even mention their recovery ability.

It could be said that every Black Knight contained an element of Alice as well as a weakened version of Captain America. They were relentless, emotionless, and did not fear death. Their sole purpose was to accomplish all tasks that were assigned by Blacklight Biotechnology.

To accomplish their missions, they were ready to do just anything...

Not only that, there were now fifty female Black Knights in this batch – 10 African variations, 20 Caucasian variations, and 20 Asian variations.

Almost as if the T-virus had fundamentally improved their genetic compositions, the postures of these females were outstanding, which Chen Chen was mildly unhappy about.

After all, if these Black Knights were sent on an undercover mission, their attractive appearances would make it difficult for them to blend into the crowd.

The moment these Black Knights were born, Chen Chen had already retrieved a hundred STEM chips. Now that Little X had developed much more, she now possessed an impressive comprehension of the inner workings of these STEM chips. Little X was now capable of hacking one STEM chip per minute, changing all of its intelligence stats to God.

Not only that, Chen Chen went on to retrieve ten sets of GS Combat Suits and ten cloaking devices and distributed them equally to five male Asian variations and five female Asian variations. These Black Knights were then sent to Mainland China to provide additional protection to Chen Chen’s parents.

Of course, these Black Knights would stay hidden in the dark. Chen Chen’s parents would not even notice their existence.

With that, Chen Chen had solved the inconveniences that came with a fully male bodyguard team.

The rest of the Black Knights were enlisted into a Black Knights Force, where they would be considered as official resources of Tara Strategic Resource Ltd. and tasked to patrol around Eco Science City and Blacklight Industrial Park. If a country in the African Continent was willing to pay, the Black Knights would also be deployed to bring peace to that nation.

The strong combat abilities of the Black Knights Force had brought many positive reviews from many countries for Tara Strategic Resource Ltd.

Following more and more advancements that came with the insights from Elysium, Chen Chen’s hidden strength was also expanding massively. A nano grade carbon fiber manufacturing plant had recently discovered a 100 nanometers grade fiber technique, which allowed Chen Chen to finally break the bottleneck of the GS Combat Suit...

Chen Chen was now sitting in his office as he observed several patrol Black Knights escorting the staff who were in charge of the nano grade carbon fiber manufacturing plant into the premises. When they arrived, the staff then presented a piece of pitch-black tight suit in front of Chen Chen.

Chen Chen was mildly disturbed when the suit was displayed before him.

This combat suit seemed to bear no difference at all compared to the GS Combat Suit. Having the same shade of matt color, it also bore the same full-body cover design.

The person-in-charge noticed Chen Chen’s reaction and immediately became nervous. He hastily asked, “Mr. Chen, are you unhappy about this nanocarbon fiber combat suit?”

“No, I’m not.”

Chen Chen looked at the person and said, “Please tell me how the suit works.”

“Sure, sure!”

The person-in-charge wiped off the cold sweats that were pooling on his forehead as he smiled awkwardly. “Sorry, it’s pretty hot today... Coughs, this combat suit was designed after the GS Combat Suit that you’ve provided. After six full days of research from our material experts, we’ve managed to come up with a simplified version of it.

“Why do we call it a simplified version? It’s because there’s some technology involved that we just can’t crack using existing techniques.”

The person continued. “This combat suit is similar to the GS Combat Suit. There are five layers of structures. The outermost layer is made out of the nanocarbon fiber that we replicate using a unique process. It can be said to be the strongest existing fiber in this world as of now. It has excellent impact resistance and can withstand a temperature of up to 1300 degrees celsius. It also has high heat insulation properties, which means that the wearer of this armor will suffer no damage at all even if he’s sprayed directly with fire in a short amount of time.

“The second layer is made out of ultra-high molecular weight polyethylene fiber. Then comes the middle layer. Regrettably, we’re unable to replicate the liquid armor that’s within, so we used shear thickening fluid as a substitute instead.”

The person-in-charge laughed bitterly. “In terms of defensive ability, the shear thickening fluid isn’t inferior against liquid armor. After absorbing large amounts of kinetic energy, it’s capable of distributing it across the layer and turning into a fixed dense state. However, it still couldn’t alter its state via the electric charges that are naturally emitted from the human body.”

Chen Chen nodded and asked, “In other words, does this mean that this combat suit only has the bulletproof ability for now?”

“Yes.”

The person-in-charge nodded. “Without liquid armor, this combat suit cannot enhance the human body like the GS Combat Suit. It’s just a bulletproof vest for now.”

Chen Chen felt mildly disappointed but still proceeded to say, “It must’ve been tough work. You can leave this sample here. Let the experts know that they can continue researching on this subject and that all of you soon will receive your fair share of rewards.”

“Sure! Thank you, Mr. Chen!”

The person-in-charge nodded hastily in gratitude and proceeded to exit Chen Chen’s office.

It was only after the person left that Chen Chen’s face turned visibly solemn.

He clapped his hand lightly and a Black Knight immediately entered the room.

“Put that on.”

Chen Chen pointed toward the black combat suit and said.

Without any hint of hesitation, the Black Knight proceeded to remove his clothing and put on the black combat suit that covered his entire body.

It was only when the suit was on the Black Knight that Chen Chen finally noticed the differences.

The GS Combat Suit had many button-like structures on it. These were precautionary structures that were built onto the suit. When the suit suffered from heavy damage, the button would be released to release liquid armor, saving the wearer from an incredibly deadly situation.

For this new combat suit, as its defensive ability could not rival against the GS Combat Suit, there was no button built on it.

Although the GS Combat Suit was still superior to this combat suit, Chen Chen felt relieved that it was still in a different league than a military bulletproof vest. In one of the experiments, a plastic dummy equipping the suit only suffered minor damage despite taking on 1000J’s impact of AK bullets.

Chen Chen’s disturbed reaction had nothing to do with the quality of the combat suit. It had to do with the design of the combat suit, which matched exactly with an image in Chen Chen’s memories.

Now that the Black Knight had put on the suit, Chen Chen blurred his own vision and felt a sense of panic.

As the Black Knight put on another layer of clothing outside the combat suit, it now appeared to be a calm man dressed in a black-collared jacket, a pair of black gloves, and a pair of black combat boots.

Such fashion was the exact one the antagonist in one of Chen Chen’s movies had used...

Chen Chen recalled that the movie was Deadly Crisis: A Shocking Scheme. It was rated by Hollywood’s critics as the worst movie of the year. Its Rotten Tomatoes rating had only reached thirty-two percent while its global box office was a mere total of 3.4 million USD.

From the antagonist’s soulless facial expression to his black tight suit, the resemblance of the character with this Black Knight was uncanny.

“Does this not suggest that my hypothesis is becoming closer and closer to reality...”

Chen Chen thought silently.

He could still remember that the movie’s timeline was on 30 May 2025, which was approximately a year and a half from now. It suggested that a year and a half later, whatever happened in the movie may very well take place in real life.

If Chen Chen previously only had fifty percent confidence in his own hypothesis, he now had eighty percent confidence.

The only unsolved puzzle was only how the movie’s plot was going to play out. After all, the characters in the movie may also exist in real life.

Then, Chen Chen suddenly recalled something and spoke toward the webcam, “X, did anything strange happen in Osmond Biotech Ltd. recently?”

Osmond Biotech Ltd. was the name of a company that invented the AD-001 medication in the movie. It was also the company which the main character worked in. In reality, there was also a real company bearing the same name.

“Not at all. However, the company has recently begun its Series B financing and has obtained fundings from several companies, aggregating a total of 50 million US dollars,” Little X answered.

“Funding, is it?”

Chen Chen felt an idea sparked in his head and said, “Get Old Qian in here right now.”

“Yes, sir!”

Little X answered. A wave of silence then resumed in the room.

After approximately twenty minutes had passed, a knock came on Chen Chen’s office door.

“Please come in.”

Chen Chen looked up and saw a mildly exhausted Qian Wenhuan entering his room. He laughed lightheartedly and said, “Old Qian, what’re you running so fast for?”

“Well, you asked for me, so I thought there must be some urgent issues that need my attention.”

Qian Wenhuan smiled and wiped his golden-laced glasses. “Boss, what is it that you ask me for?”

“Make some arrangements with the Human Resource Department and prepare to send a few employees out to the North American Continent.”

Chen Chen said sternly, “...and purchase the share of a company called Osmond Biotech Ltd. and purchase as much as you can.”

“Okay.”

Qian Wenhuan was initially shocked. However, he hastily nodded his head and said, “Osmond Biotech Ltd. right? I’ll remember that name. Is there anything else you’d like to instruct me to?”

“That’s all for now.”

Chen Chen shook his head and felt a pinch of admiration for Qian Wenhuan’s decisive attitude. Then, he thought of another thing and elaborated on his previous instruction. “Oh, right. It’s okay if you’re unable to purchase any shares directly. The company is currently undergoing B Series financing. You may take a look at the venture capital companies that are purchasing these shares. If you can’t get your hands on any of the shares, then purchase these venture capital companies.”

“....” Qian Wenhuan nodded and said, “Alright. What’s the price range that you’re looking to invest in?”

Chen Chen gave it some thought and said, “Around three billion US dollars. If spending is too high, then the cost would outweigh the benefit. If these venture capital companies are mostly small-medium enterprises, then purchase as many as you can. However, if it’s a mega-conglomerate, then forget about it. After all, we can’t afford to spend on big companies like these.”

“Understood. I’ll bring back good news.”

Qian Wenhuan received his instruction and turned to leave.

After seeing Qian Wenhuan out, Chen Chen then went into a contemplating gesture as he murmured to himself.

“Osmond Biotech Ltd....”

“Well, I’d loved to see what kind of surprises await from you.”

chapters.title: Chapter 242: Bet-On Agreement
chapters.body:
Chapter 242: Bet-On Agreement

Chapter 242: Bet-on Agreement

In the end, Qian Wenhuan had made arrangements for Anthony and Jennifer to lead a group of expert negotiators and depart for Los Angeles.

Anthony and Jennifer were both Swiss. Just like Qian Wenhuan, they were employees for Preston Company. One of them was a business negotiator whereas the other was a legal consultant and they were both sent to Africa after Chen Chen had acquired their company.

In the beginning, the duo was attracted by the attractive salary the company offered and had intended to resign after working for a couple of years. However, they did not expect Blacklight Biotechnology to expand so smoothly in Africa and the duo decided to settle down longer.

Once the group landed in Los Angeles, the group spent a night resting up before heading toward Osmond Biotech Ltd. the next day.

Jennifer was wearing a white-collared OL costume. As she exited a rented multipurpose vehicle, she frowned at what she saw.

Their destination was close to a rural area and the entire location just seemed deserted. In front of her was an office building that seemed old and almost ruined. It was evident that the building had at least fifteen years of history.

“Is this Osmond Biotech Ltd.’s head office?”

Jennifer shook her head and said, “It looks just like any other ordinary company. I’ve no idea why would boss want us to purchase this business.”

Anthony shrugged and said, “Nevermind, these aren’t something we should care about. Besides, it looks like they didn’t even bother to greet us. I guess we should just go ahead and enter.”

Then, the duo led the group into the office building.

“We’re the employees of Blacklight Biotechnology. I’m Anthony, from the Business Development Department and this is Jennifer from the Legal Department. We have an appointment with the representatives of Osmond Biotech Ltd. on the 23rd floor.”

Inside the office building, Anthony was answering the security guard’s questions patiently.

“Please hold on a moment.”

The security guard took up his phone and dialed Osmond Biotech Ltd.’s number.

After two minutes had passed, the guard finally put down the phone and said, “It’s settled now. Please head upstairs. The people of Osmond Biotech Ltd. are expecting you.”

“Thank you.”

The group went into an elevator and soon found themselves reaching the 23rd floor.

Upon exiting the elevator, the group was immediately greeted by a blond-haired woman.

The blond-haired woman had an attractive figure, dressed in what seemed like a typical secretary would wear. However, her reaction was not enthusiastic at all. “You must be the people from Blacklight Biotechnology. Please follow me.”

Then, she hastily turned around and walked toward the meeting room.

Her attitude was unpleasant enough to raise a few more eyebrows.

When the group reached the meeting room, she merely instructed the group to wait a bit longer before she left them cluelessly in the room.

“This doesn’t feel right.”

Jennifer took a look at her surroundings and said, “This company didn’t seem welcoming at all. Do they not know that we’re here to provide fundings?”

“It must be that the funding round has been concluded.”

Anthony pondered and said, “We’re late to the party.”

Just when the group was whispering to each other, a middle-aged man dressed in a business suit entered the room. “My apologies, everyone. I just came here from another meeting and had to make you wait for a little.”

Then, he extended his hand toward Anthony and said, “My name is Parker, the founder of Osmond Biotech Ltd. I also currently serve as one of the company’s board directors and its CEO.”

Anthony and the group stood and took turns to shake hands as well. “We’re the managers of the Blacklight Biotechnology Business Development Department. Beside me is Jennifer, who’s from the Legal Department. It’s a pleasure meeting you.”

After a round of introduction, both sides took their seats with Parker initiating the discussion. “Blacklight Biotechnology’s reputation has been soaring for the past two years. May I ask what’s the purpose of your visit today? Is there something that we can collaborate on?”

“I’ve heard that your company is undergoing a B Series financing. Is that true?”

Anthony asked, “According to our understanding, your company has been developing at a rapid pace in the past two years. While I don’t know what your current business strategies are, I do feel that you may want a strategic investor like us.”

Then, Anthony snapped his finger as his subordinate immediately handed a report over to him. He then pushed the agreement across the table to Parker. “Mr. Parker, being in the same industry, I believe I don’t have to talk about Blacklight Biotechnology any further. Please take a look at this agreement.”

Parker nodded and received the agreement. After flipping over a few pages, he said, “Having Blacklight Biotechnology as our strategic investor is definitely a decent option. Regrettably, our B Series financing has been concluded and our board members have been elected. I’m afraid we can’t accept this enticing offer.”

“Mr. Parker, do you not want to at least think about it?”

Anthony frowned and said, “After B Series financing, I trust that your company has a new goal to expand toward. This is where our company can be of great help.”

Parker shook his head and said, “Our company will soon be announcing our next step, which is to take out Alzheimer’s disease.”

Parker let out a peal of light laughter and continued. “I’m aware that Blacklight Biotechnology has a program that’s called the Alzheimer’s Disease Reversal Treatment that’s capable of suppressing the symptoms of Alzheimer’s disease. However, our goal isn’t just to control the disease, we’d like to cure it.”

Anthony and Jennifer looked at each other as a sudden revelation became apparent. It was not surprising they were so unwelcoming of their visit. Could it be that Osmond Biotech Ltd. thought the people from Blacklight Biotechnology had caught wind of their plans and assumed that they were here to thwart it?

Anthony then pondered a bit further and things started to make sense. The only reason why they could afford to be so snobbish was that they did not only have a plan, the cure was already here!

Then, Anthony could not help but shook his head. He thought that the management must have known about the cure and had sent him to make the investment.

However, it looked like Anthony was about to fail his task.

Hence, Anthony followed the usual procedure to persuade Parker further. It was until Parker rejected once more that Anthony stood up and said, “If that’s the case, we’ll not insist. We shall make a move.”

From aside, Jennifer then passed a name card to Parker and said, “If you decide to change your mind or have a C Series financing, perhaps you can consider our company’s proposition.”

“For sure. Farewell, everyone.”

Then, Parker proceeded to send Anthony and his group off. Once the elevator door was closed, Parker then let out a worrisome expression. “D*mn it, have many people known about our research on that drug already? How’s this possible...”

...

“Yes, Director Qian. They’ve declined our offer...

“As far as we know, they seemed to be researching an Alzheimer’s disease cure and were completely indifferent toward our proposal.

“Alright, got it. I’ll take a look at the venture capital companies.”

Outside the office building, Anthony gave Qian Wenhuan a phone call to provide the latest status update. Then, his next destination was to visit the venture capital companies that had invested in Osmond Biotech Ltd.

There were six venture capital companies that had invested in Osmond Biotech Ltd. Six separate financial conglomerates owned these companies and they owned a total of eighty percent stake in Osmond Biotech Ltd.

One of the companies, Munster Capital, had purchased a fifty percent stake in the company.

Unsurprisingly, it would be the next destination Anthony was heading to.

“During their negotiation, Munster Capital and Osmond Biotech Ltd. had both signed a bet-on agreement.”

Jennifer was frowning as she read the report in the car. “The agreement’s condition was that Munster Capital would invest one billion US dollars worth of capital into Osmond Biotech Ltd. for a fifty percent equity stake. However, if Osmond Biotech Ltd. had failed to produce an effective cure for Alzheimer’s disease by 31 December 2025, Munster Capital reserves the rights to demand Osmond Biotech Ltd. to repurchase its share. The repurchasing amount shall be the total of the capital invested plus an additional imposition of eight percent annual interest.”

Anthony was visibly at unease as he muttered, “Is Osmond Biotech Ltd. crazy? Many top pharmaceutical companies have failed to produce a cure for Alzheimer’s disease. Signing this agreement isn’t going to end well for them.”

“Unless...”

Anthony thought for a while and said, “Unless this company already has a massive breakthrough in their research development, which explains their confidence!”

“If that’s truly the case, I’m afraid it won’t be as easy to acquire their shares anymore.”

Jennifer was worried too. “After all, if they’re able to truly come up with a cure, it’ll be a revolution in the medical field. The impact of this would surpass the responses we received when we announced the Alzheimer’s Disease Reversal Treatment.”

“Agreed. I’ll make sure to include this hypothesis in our report.”

Anthony was tense. As an employee of Blacklight Biotechnology, he understood the gravity of the situation. Once this company successfully produced an effective cure, it would reduce the “Alzheimer’s Disease Reversal Treatment” to mere trash. It would bring massive damage to Blacklight Biotechnology.

The best way to mitigate that harm was to acquire the company and sell it as one of the corporation’s products.

Of course, Anthony would not be the only one having that idea. It was to be expected that these venture capital funds would have the same intentions too.

With a heavy heart, Anthony and his group found themselves at the entrance of a tower where Munster Capital was based. This time, none of the main stakeholders even came to greet Anthony. It was only a department manager who came to greet them.

It was evident that they had no interest in whatever proposition Anthony had. They were only here out of politeness.

Anthony could sense their true intention and proceed to inform the manager that they would visit another day before leaving the premises.

“There’s another investor whose name is Clifford. He’s the second-largest shareholder in Osmond Biotech Ltd. and is also Parker’s business partner.”

Jennifer continued reading the materials as she prepared for the final sprint. “The report indicates that Clifford had earlier invested three million US dollars into the company, acquiring a forty percent stake in the company. However, after a round of angel crowdfunding and AB series fundings, he’s now left with a stake of seven percent. It’s not much, but if we can get it, at least we’ll have something to show for our company.”

“Let me have a look.”

Anthony went to the computer and saw a man who looked like a tycoon. “Very well, let’s go meet him now.”

Following the information provided by Blacklight Biotechnology, Anthony and Jennifer soon found themselves in front of Clifford’s home only to realize that he was preparing to head out.

He was an elderly person well past his sixties and had white hair and a beard growing on his face.

“Are you all from Blacklight Biotechnology?”

As Anthony appeared at the gate, Clifford’s eyes immediately brightened up as he greeted them affectionately. “I’ve been wanting to see you all for a while. I’ve recently just decided to visit Eco Science City early next year. I wouldn’t have thought I’d bumped into you all so soon.”

“Well then, do you have some time to discuss an issue relating to your stakes in Osmond Biotech Ltd.?” Jeniffer said hastily, “It won’t take up too much time.”

Hearing their proposal, Clifford then seemed to be in a conundrum. “I’m sorry, I was just about to head out to a good friend’s party. Why not both of you follow me and we can discuss the details over at the event?”

“Are you sure?” Anthony felt that it was slightly inappropriate.

“Nothing inappropriate about that. The host is an affectionate gentleman who would welcome even uninvited guests. As long as I’m there, your presence won’t bring any trouble at all to the host...”

Then, Clifford invited the duo into his car whereas the rest of the negotiation group returned to their hotel rooms.

Soon, Anthony and Jennifer found themselves in a manor at Beverly Hills.

Although they were no strangers to glamorous scenes, Jennifer still found herself awe-struck as she observed the glittering decorations of her surroundings that immediately transformed the manor into an elegant white palace.

There were luxury cars parked across the manor, each owned by a successful individual who was then personally escorted into the compound. Anthony had also received the same treatment.

“Old friend Clifford, long time no see!”

“Old friend!”

Near the entrance into the manor, a middle-aged man in his fifties gave Clifford a light hug. Then, he gave a curious look behind Clifford and asked, “Who are these people?”

“These are high-level representatives from Blacklight Biotechnology, they’ve come here to check out what the fuss is all about. You better not disappoint!”

The two men seemed to be close friends as Clifford was able to banter seamlessly with that person.

“The same Blacklight Biotechnology that came up with the Alzheimer’s Disease Reversal Treatment and special anticancer medicines?” The man’s eye brightened up as he took a closer look at the duo.

“Yes. I’m Anthony, a manager from Blacklight Biotechnology’s Business Development Department. This is Jennifer, she’s the manager of the Legal Department. It’s an honor to meet you.”

Anthony spoke and behaved like a true gentleman.

“My friends, welcome to the party. You all have come just at the right time.”

The middle-aged man let out a burst of hearty laughter and said, “We have a few exciting scenes to show you all. I guarantee you’ll have the shock of your lifetime!”

Then, the man passionately welcomed the guests into his property.

chapters.title: Chapter 243: An Upper-Class Party
chapters.body:
Chapter 243: An Upper-Class Party

Chapter 243: An Upper-Class Party

Once they settled down into the glamorous party, Anthony and Jeniffer had finally snapped out of the awe-struck feeling that they harbored since coming into this place.

The experience that they had just been through felt like a dream to them.

Although they were well-compensated working in Blacklight Biotechnology, it was still a huge gap away from becoming a true tycoon. For Jennifer and Anthony, it was their first time witnessing such an opulent occasion.

“By the way, Mr. Clifford,” Jeniffer asked softly once the middle-aged man had left, “is that the organizer of this party?”

“Yes, his name’s Gross. You can address him as Mr. Gross.”

Clifford nodded. When he noticed a group approaching him, he preemptively said to his companions, “You both can go take a look around. Once I finish catching up with some old friends, we can talk about business later.”

“Don’t mind us.”

Anthony and Jennifer nodded and hastily walked away.

As the party did not call for any dress codes, the duo did not seem to stand out too much despite being in their work clothes.

Once they walked toward a quiet corner, Jeniffer’s eyes were beaming with envy as she said, “Is this how the upper-class live their lives? Why do I never see Mr. Chen live like this?”

“Mr. Chen is wealthy only fairly recently. His personality and culture wouldn’t resonate with what we’ve observed here. These are tycoons who’ve been wealthy since decades ago, perhaps even up to a century.”

Anthony lamented, “Besides, Mr. Chen is still a Chinese after all, his culture is fundamentally different. As far as I know, they don’t typically host parties like these at night.”

“What if we’re comparing influence?”

Jeniffer asked softly, “Would these tycoons be as influential as Mr. Chen?”

“A normal tycoon would never be as influential as Mr. Chen.”

Anthony was determined. “Although Mr. Chen was only wealthy recently, he had already amassed capitals that could rival an entire healthcare industry. This isn’t something a normal tycoon can pull off.

“However, if we’re talking about the top tycoons, then things might be different. After all, their capitals that have been amassed for centuries could probably be compared against Blacklight Biotechnology’s properties. It’s fifty-fifty at this point...”

“Are you overestimating Mr. Chen?”

Jennifer was shocked. “Although I concede that Mr. Chen’s a remarkable individual, these Northern American and European tycoons are capable of influencing major government policies.”

“Indeed, but don’t forget that Mr. Chen also has support from dozens of wealthy families and financial conglomerates,” Anthony said, “besides, do you think our company’s as simple as it looks?”

“You mean...” Jennifer asked cluelessly.

“You’ve worked in Blacklight Biotechnology for a while now. I’m sure you would’ve noticed.”

Anthony spoke softly, “For instance, arms smuggling. This has already been an open secret for a while now. There are hundreds of fully armed Black Security Service personnel as well as the Black Knights that were owned under Tara Private Security Company. These armed forces even have armored cars and military helicopters at their disposal...”

“Well, if you put it that way, our company seems much more powerful now.”

Jennifer nodded and felt a wave of excitement.

Just when the duo was chatting away and sipping champagne, the middle-aged man suddenly went on stage and clapped his hands.

The music system of the hall was instantly turned off as all of the guests unanimously channeled their attention toward the person.

Even Anthony and Jennifer had stopped their conversation.

“Ladies and gentlemen.”

Gross seemed to have put on a layer of foundation on his face. His cheekbone seemed to be protruded obviously from his cheeks as his face appeared to be extremely pale under the glare of the spotlight.

Gross continued smiling and said, “Aside from bringing you a source of entertainment, I’ve hosted tonight’s party to show you an extremely important object. Now, would the lovely guests standing near the stairs entrance please take a few steps backward?”

Following his gesture, the few guests seemed to retreat subconsciously, forming an empty space near the staircase.

“As you all know, aside from being an entrepreneur, I’m also a collector who loves collecting precious artifacts. This time, my construction team has found this in a gold mine...”

Following his announcement, three showcases gradually rose from the ground. The guests were completely awe-struck by this scene as they gasped in shock. No one would have thought that there would be such platforms in this area.

“Come, let’s take a look.”

Anthony and Jennifer walked curiously toward the crowd as they saw the showcases that were still ascending from the ground.

After all, an object that was sought after by Gross would be one of invaluable price. Even if they could not get their hands on it, the duo thought there was no harm in learning something new.

The showcases were no different from those in museums, both were covered by a transparent glass case that was specially handled to become bulletproof.

As the platform continued raising, the crowd could finally see what was contained in these showcases. Those were three stone statues!

Anthony observed that these statues were completely carved out of stones. These statues seemed to have stood the test of time as their edges and boundaries were slowly blurring away. The statues seemed to belong in a group, each featuring a human head that had a peculiar expression on it.

The first statue was a human head that had its eyes tightly shut. It had long pointy ears that seemed to grow out of its temporal lobe, bearing resemblance to a person growing a bunny ear.

The second statue had its eyes wide opened and a deep eye socket, seemingly dragging its eyes into its skin. Under the shine of the spotlights, the eyes sockets seemed to become a deep abyss.

The third statue had eyes and ears that were fairly normal. However, its mouth stretched open so widely that it elongated its face by one-third of its normal length. The inside of the mouth was also pitch-black, resembling a black hole that could absorb any traces of lights.

Strange was the only word that Anthony could use to describe his first impression of these statues.

Unsure of whether it was an optical illusion, Anthony felt like the lights had dimmed significantly in the party the moment these statues were featured.

“Oh my god, look at that ancient texture and the traces that were left ages ago. How unbelievable...”

After a short silence, someone in the crowd abruptly marveled out loud. After all, if it was something Gross had brought out, no one would have second-guessed the authenticity of these items.

“Mr. Gross, is this an artifact of the Mayan civilization?”

“I think these were made during the times of Ancient Greece!”

“I don’t think these fit the styles of Ancient Greece. In my opinion, they should be statues that were carved by the Sumeru civilization.”

The crowd soon engaged in exciting discussions. In reality, everyone was merely supporting Mr. Gross as they attempted to appear interested.

“These statues are ugly...”

Jeniffer said softly, “Anthony, are you able to recognize which era these statues belong to?”

“Nope, but there are so many experts here, one of them ought to have the answer.” Anthony shook his head.

chapters.title: Chapter 244: Turned Down Again
chapters.body:
Chapter 244: Turned Down Again

Chapter 244: Turned Down Again

“Mr. Gross, I really can’t tell which era these sculptures are from.”

After a while, everyone still had not reached a unified consensus, so one of them choked back his impatience and glanced at Gross on the steps. “Mr. Gross, please clarify.”

“I don’t know their year of production either.”

Only then did Gross stopped beaming smugly as he proclaimed, “But what I want to tell everyone is that I’ve unearthed these three sculptures in a gold mine in the South American Continent.”

“A gold mine?” Everyone was instantly bewildered.

“Yes, they were dug up from a gold mine.”

Gross laughed and said, “I know it’s hard to believe. Even when I received these three sculptures, I didn’t believe it at all until I rushed to the gold mine and found the location of the excavation, where I saw the cavity where these sculptures had been. Then, I believed everything.

“I think everyone should know what this means.”

Gross slowly became serious. “No traces of artificial excavation were found near these sculptures, which means that they were probably buried in gold mines due to geological changes. However, the last geological change that can be traced back to hundreds of thousands of years ago. This means that these sculptures probably have a history going back hundreds of thousands of years or even millions of years!”

“That, that’s impossible...”

“The Stone Age can be traced back to three million years ago, but how could the ancient apes at that time have the technology to make such complex stone sculptures? It’s impossible...”

“Still, they do look like the totems of some civilization...”

“Maybe they were God’s gifts to some primitive people!”

Everyone started debating again.

“I say, old man, no wonder you’ve looked unwell recently, and your body has gotten thinner than before. Is it because of these three sculptures?” Clifford chuckled and stopped the bickering.

“Yes, for some reason, lately my head has been aching frequently.”

Gross spread out his hands helplessly. “Still, my personal physician has examined it and said that it’s just a nervous breakdown, nothing else.”

At this point, a gray-haired old man with a pair of very thick glasses came forward. “Gross, if possible, I’d like to inspect these three sculptures up close. After all, if your miners didn’t lie, these three sculptures may be the biggest discovery of this century.”

“Of course, Professor Griffin.”

This gray-haired old man seemed to enjoy such a high status that even Gross was acting deferential.

After Gross was done showing off, the dinner party continued, and everyone gradually turned their attention away from the three sculptures. After all, most people did not have any favorable impressions of these three weird-looking sculptures. In comparison to those, they were more concerned about taking advantage of this banquet to discuss a few transactions or take the opportunity to find an attractive bed partner.

“My apologies for making you wait.”

Just as Anthony and Jennifer were on their fourth glass of champagne, Clifford finally came over. He raised his glass to them and grinned. “It’s noisy here. Why don’t we go out to talk?”

“Sure.”

Anthony glanced behind him before leading the way to the garden outside.

The garden was only illuminated by a few rows of dim lights. It was quite dark but only because their eyes had not gotten used to this level of darkness.

In the gloom, they could make out a few figures on the chairs conversing in low voices. Anthony did not mind them. He went to a corner before turning around.

“Mr. Clifford, Jennifer and I are here this time on behalf of Blacklight Biotechnology. We hope to talk about the shares of Osmond Biotech Ltd. that you own.”

Anthony lowered his voice. “Of course, you can make a few requests. If we can’t grant them, we could still consult with our superiors.”

“So, that’s it...”

Clifford pondered for a while. “I don’t know much about this company. It’s just because this company was first founded by a down-and-out friend. After he went bankrupt, he had wanted to make a comeback. So I funded him three million dollars, which isn’t much anyway. I thought that would be the final time I’m helping him.

“However, now it seems like his company has become very much sought-after.”

Clifford displayed a crafty look. “So before I give you an answer, I’d like to ask you two to tell me — why do you want the shares in my hands?”

“This...”

Anthony and Jennifer shifted their expressions when they heard these words. Their behavior had fully aroused this man’s curiosity. It would not be easy to get Clifford to transfer the shares.

“This is a decision issued by our company. We’re only the operatives and we aren’t quite clear about the reasons.”

Anthony spoke and noticed Clifford’s disbelieving look, so he could only add. “Well, in our opinion, it should be because Osmond is researching a drug that can completely cure Alzheimer’s disease. Moreover, they must have made good progress in this research, so everyone else is starting to become restless.”

“You’re very honest and didn’t deceive me with some lies.”

Clifford nodded and said, pleased, “Nonetheless, in this case, I can’t transfer my shares to you. After all, I believe you also understand that, if there’s really a drug that can completely cure Alzheimer’s disease, the value of the shares I own might increase by more than a hundred or thousand times in less than ten years.”

“Even so, that’s merely one possibility.”

Jennifer hurriedly said, “What if this drug can’t be developed? After all, you know as well how many pharmaceutical companies in the world have suffered grievous losses in this area. The difficulty of treating Alzheimer’s disease is no lesser than that of treating cancer. It might even be harder. After all, cancer can be cured while Alzheimer’s disease has never been cured until a year ago!”

Hearing this, Clifford nodded and smiled. “Ms. Jennifer, what you said makes sense.”

“It’s not just that.”

Anthony chimed in. “Even if the drug is developed, what are the chances it could pass Phase III clinical trials and go on the market?

“Let me give you an example. From 1998 to 2022, in terms of drug development for Alzheimer’s disease, a total of 165 drugs have been developed around the world, but only 6 drugs have passed clinical trials. The rest have failed. The passing rate is only 3.36%. Surely you understand what kind of figure this is...

“In addition, a clinical trial period of more than ten years has to be taken into account. Even if this new drug gets on the market, it’ll probably be ten years later.”

Clifford smiled again when he heard all this and said carelessly, “Mr. Anthony, what you said is very reasonable too, but I think your company must be so anxious to acquire shares in Osmond surely because of some unique aspect about this new drug?

“Not just that.” Clifford continued with a smile. “It’s only three million dollars, after all. I don’t really care about this amount of money, but after listening to your persuasion, I now plan to continue investing in the upcoming Series C financing round. For that, I have to thank you both.”

“...”

Anthony and Jennifer were completely dumbfounded.

chapters.title: Chapter 245: Reply
chapters.body:
Chapter 245: Reply

Chapter 245: Reply

In the operating room, Chen Chen took out a loud, buzzing bone saw and slowly sliced open the chest of a pale-skinned giant lying on the operating table in front of him.

Blood gushed out like a fountain, splashing across Chen Chen’s mask in a brilliant, startling red.

The peculiar thing was that the giant who was being dissected had a face that was ninety percent similar to Chen Chen’s. If one added eyebrows and hair, it would almost be an enlarged version of Chen Chen.

This giant was the tyrant that resulted from Chen Chen cloning his own fertilized egg, which was injected with T-virus and hatched in the life incubator.

The firm skin and muscles that were as tough as rubber tires could not be cut with a scalpel at all, so Chen used an electric bone saw to forcefully cut open the tyrant’s chest.

Following that, an assisting Black Knight stepped forward and swiftly cut out a piece of sample tissue from the tyrant.

Skin, liver, intestines, kidneys, stomach, heart, and lungs...

A sample was cut from every internal organ. These samples were then put on a plate and sent to the workbench on the side.

The bone marrow puncture needle was useless in extracting the brain tissue. Chen Chen had to use the bone saw again to cut the tyrant’s skull bit by bit and extract a part of the brain tissue inside.

As Chen Chen needed infallible experimental data, he had cloned himself with a complete brain, only without forming any consciousness.

Even with a piece of the brain cut off, the life force of this tyrant was still extremely robust. Not only was he a long way from dying, but his wounds were also constantly healing.

Thus, Chen Chen had to inject a syringe filled with cyanide into the tyrant to end the life of this unconscious monster.

Next, Chen Chen took out another clone that had not been injected with T-virus. This clone looked only about ten years old because it had not been injected with T-virus. It was exactly what Chen Chen had looked like when he was a child. Still, it would serve its purpose as a comparison.

At the moment, Chen Chen had four sample clones that had been injected with T-virus and four sample clones that had not been injected with T-virus.

Just when Chen Chen was about to use the samples of internal organs from both sides for comparative analysis, Little X’s voice suddenly came out of the speaker.

“Sir Godfather, Qian Wenhuan sent a message that the negotiation team we sent has reported failure.”

“How did they fail?”

Chen Chen did not look surprised at all. He just walked to the cutting table and asked calmly while slicing off sections from the viscera samples.

“When our people arrived, Osmond had completed the Series B round of financing and the founder of the company acted in a hostile manner toward us.”

“Hostile?”

Chen Chen’s hands froze. “Are they developing drugs to treat Alzheimer’s disease?”

“To think that you got it immediately.”

Little X replied, “According to the report by Anthony, who was in charge of this matter, this was brought up indeed in the discussion with the other party.”

Hearing this, Chen Chen squinted and showed a thoughtful expression. “If we really want to prevent them from developing the drug, there are hundreds of ways with the means we have at our disposal. Nevertheless, compared to that, I’m more interested in knowing how reality would play out the plot in the movie.

“Besides, in retrospect now, the ending of the movie seems very interesting.”

Chen Chen continued. “The protagonist finally arrived at the Los Angeles headquarters from New York, but when he handed over the newly developed drug to the high-level board of directors, he found that the board was also wearing the same clothing as the villains... So, we actually have two options now.”

“I know.”

Little X smiled. “The first one is to follow the plot of the movie and forcefully converge the company’s shares through coercion, assassination, and chip implantation. On the other hand, we could directly eliminate the company at its root or destroy all of their information, so that the plot in the movie would not take place.”

“Yes. To go ‘with’ or ‘against’ the flow — those are our two choices.”

Chen Chen passed the work he was doing to the Black Knight beside him. His face suddenly became increasingly solemn. “Sure enough, there’s no such thing as a free lunch. As I get more and more involved with the USB flash drive, the price of using it also becomes more apparent. Although it seems that these costs are incredibly covert, I feel that once I make a wrong move, it would trigger a ripple of changes, like dominoes...”

Little X was silent for a moment. Although it could be described as the strongest artificial intelligence in reality, when faced with this kind of problem, the logical thinking ability of the computer was still inferior to the human brain.

“Let’s play safe for the first encounter.”

After thinking about it, Chen Chen asked, “Who holds the most shares of Osmond now?”

“That would be Munster Capital. They’ve invested 1 billion USD and now occupy 50% of Osmond’s shares. They’ve also signed a gambling contract,” Little X replied. “Behind this venture capital company is the Munster family, a newly emerging, small financial group with great potential.”

Chen Chen snapped his fingers upon hearing this. “Notify Brando and ask him to send a few members of the B.S.S. force, equipped with memory erasers and cloaking devices, to bring me the one at the helm of this financial group. Little X, you’re in charge of manipulating the data of international flights in North America. I want to see him in three days.”

Since Brando and the rest had formally joined God’s Forbidden Zone, Chen Chen had handed over the command of the B.S.S unit to him. They were responsible for the security of the Eco Science City.

“You’re not using Black Knights?” Little X asked.

“The Black Knights are still not as adaptable to situations, so I’m not too confident in them.”

Chen Chen said, “The Black Knights are still suitable for some simple tasks such as assassinations, regular combat, and so on. However, this time, it’s a kidnapping target. They would have to go through the international airport and many levels of inspections. A Black Knight would be too easily exposed.”

“Got it.”

Little X answered.

Chen Chen did not say anything else, but walked aside and continued to observe the Black Knight slicing the internal organs.

It was not that Chen Chen had not considered using violent means to kidnap the head of the Munster Financial Group, implant a chip in them, then force them to transfer the shares to his own name, before finally destroying the body.

Even so, this was way too aggressive. A major move like the transfer of shares would attract everyone’s attention. At that point, a simple investigation would show that it was the doing of Blacklight Biotechnology.

Although capitalists were all lawless fanatics, there were still rules in this industry. If Chen Chen dared to break such rules in the open, of course, these financial groups would not sit idly by.

After all, if Chen Chen could take someone’s property by force today, he could easily seize someone else’s property tomorrow. No one would tolerate a ticking bomb by their side.

Therefore, even if Chen Chen intended to kidnap his opponent in the first step, he would not kill the guy, let alone implant the God chip. After all, the malpractice of the chip was too significant and easily exposed.

Once exposed, Chen Chen who used such an evil device would immediately be labeled as a public enemy, and he would never rise to the top.

chapters.title: Chapter 246: Arrest
chapters.body:
Chapter 246: Arrest

Chapter 246: Arrest

At this time, at Los Angeles International Airport, Anthony and Jennifer sat down in the VIP terminal. They remained silent for a long time.

Qian Wenhuan had already issued an order for the two of them and the negotiating team to return directly, without having to stay on in Los Angeles.

Even so, they were returning after having failed in their mission. At the very least, they were naturally ashamed. As for the worst-case scenario, the company’s senior management might question both of their capabilities, which could even affect their careers.

Nonetheless, there was no use thinking about these things. What was done was done. No one wanted to sell their shares to Blacklight Biotechnology.

“Are you both Mr. Anthony and Ms. Jennifer?”

Just when the two of them were feeling downcast, four or five men wearing black clothes and sunglasses suddenly surrounded them, looking at them condescendingly as they subtly blocked off all escape routes.

“I am. And may I ask who you are...”

Anthony looked up blankly and asked.

“We’re with the Federal Bureau of Investigation. There’s an investigation that we need your cooperation with.”

The man in black at their head flashed his ID and the other four immediately stepped forward. They seized Anthony and Jennifer by the arms and marched them out of the terminal.

“This is...”

Panic fluttered across Anthony’s face. “Wait, what happened? We didn’t violate federal law!”

“You’ll understand when you reach our destination.”

The FBI agent did not even explain anything to the two of them and hauled them away at once. The rest of the negotiating team was stunned.

“Did any of you attend the ball at Gross Manor?”

“No, we didn’t.”

Everyone quickly shook their heads.

“Please come with us first. We won’t take too much of your time.”

The man in black continued while gesturing for them to walk ahead.

They could only follow obediently.

Anthony and Jennifer were carted to the FBI office building. They were instantly astounded when they got out of the vehicle.

What they saw was that the entire hall of the Federal Bureau of Investigation was filled with various sculptures, ranging from bronze sculptures to stone sculptures. The large ones were more than ten meters high and the small ones were only ten centimeters tall.

Countless sculptures filled the hall and there was also a large number of trucks that were bringing in more sculptures.

“What’s going on?”

Anthony mumbled to himself.

After they passed through the hall of sculptures, the two were taken into a makeshift interrogation room. This interrogation room was modified out of an office. The FBI, for some reason, could not even provide a formal interrogation room.

There were people dressed in black coming in and out as well as a large number of people in uniforms. Currently, a man rushed into the interrogation room while drinking water and said hello to the men in black who were detaining the two. Those men immediately withdrew.

All at once, only Anthony and Jennifer remained in the interrogation room, along with the man drinking water.

After drinking the water in the cup in loud gulps, the man exhaled and turned to look at the two of them. “Do you want some?”

“No, it’s fine. Thank you.”

Anthony still looked dazed. “Excuse me, what has happened and why are we arrested? Surely we didn’t violate the laws of North America, let alone the laws of the Earth Federation.”

“I apologize.”

The man nodded. “First, let me introduce myself. You can call me Investigator Geraldson. Yes, neither of you violated any laws, but everything happened so suddenly. We could only do this to make sure you cooperate.”

After that, the man grew stern. “From now onward, please answer each of my questions seriously. Any falsehoods, once discovered, will likely lead you to be arrested for crimes against humanity.”

“Crimes against humanity?”

Jennifer exclaimed, “Why is that?”

“This is what the Earth Federation has stipulated. We in North America are merely following orders.”

Geraldson spread his hands. “Okay, can the both of you tell me where you were last night?”

Upon hearing these questions, Anthony and Jennifer exchanged a glance and finally, Anthony smiled bitterly. “Do you need to ask that? You seem to have arrested us because we joined the dinner party last night, right?”

“Very well. So you did join the dinner party last night.”

Geraldson took out a pen recorder and recorded what Anthony said. Then he asked again, “So at the party, did you see... Uh, three sculptures?”

“Yes, there were three oddly shaped sculptures.”

Anthony was no longer thinking of anything else — he might as well tell the truth. “One of them had prominent ears and the others had prominent eyes and mouth respectively. The three sculptures seemed to be very...”

Anthony paused here, searching for a description.

“Weird.” Jennifer filled in.

“Yes, the three sculptures gave off a weird vibe, both in terms of their shape and lines.” Anthony nodded.

“Very well...”

Geraldson nodded. “Next question — have you noticed anything unusual about those three sculptures?”

“Unusual? In what way?”

“I don’t know either.” Geraldson shook his head. “For instance, if you got a weird feeling, that should count as something unusual, I guess.”

“We only took a look at the three sculptures from a distance,” Anthony said helplessly. “We only noticed each of their three prominent features, which were the ears, eyes, and mouth. I don’t remember anything else...”

“Plus, we didn’t touch those three sculptures. They were all sealed in the jewelry display. Even if there was an ancient virus or something on them, we wouldn’t have been in contact.”

Jennifer chimed in.

Anthony was shocked as he recalled the horror of being dominated by the Blacklight virus. He quickly asked, “Is there really some kind of ancient virus on the three sculptures?”

Geraldson shook his head, “No. To be honest, we aren’t sure why those at the top have given us these orders... Forget it, just pretend I didn’t say anything. I’ll continue with my questions.”

With that, Geraldson continued to ask, “Please explain to me the origins of those three sculptures, including everything you heard, saw, and analyzed. Tell me everything.”

Geraldson’s words caused a flutter in Anthony’s expression. The investigator’s words seemed to imply something, but now was not the time to think about all that, after all.

Thus, Anthony once again narrated the origins of the three sculptures, how they were dug out from a gold mine in South America by a man named Gross. At the same time, he revealed to the federal investigator before him that it was a man named Clifford who had invited them to the showing.

“Very good.”

Geraldson listened to all of this but remained completely unmoved. He had already examined the entire affair thoroughly. At this point, he was just verifying the facts.

chapters.title: Chapter 247: General Knowledge Can Be Horrifying
chapters.body:
Chapter 247: General Knowledge Can Be Horrifying

Chapter 247: General Knowledge Can Be Horrifying

One day later, Chen Chen was woken up in bed.

“Sir Godfather, an anomaly has been found in North America.”

Little X’s voice came from the speaker.

Chen Chen rubbed his eyes, looked at the phone, and found that it was three o’clock in the morning. Only then did he sigh and asked feebly, “What anomaly...”

“Since last night, police stations throughout North America and the Federal Bureau of Investigation have unanimously launched an investigation into the sculptures.”

Little X’s voice was rather somber. “Not only that, but also none of the negotiating team, who was supposed to return to Walvis Bay International Airport in the early morning, have returned. I checked their mobile phones by satellite positioning and found that they are actually located within the FBI building in Los Angeles.”

“And?”

At first, Chen Chen was a little irritated but as Little X went on, his expression slowly turned calm. He asked, “Did you find anything on the Internet?”

“I’ve hacked into the network of the police station and the FBI, and learned that the Earth Federation United Front had sent a mandatory mission to search all of North America and seize all sculptures with any abnormalities.”

“United Front, sculptures...”

Chen Chen’s pupils contracted as if he had realized something.

From what Chen Chen remembered, the Earth Federation United Front seemed to be a department more mysterious than the FBI. It was rare to hear about the deeds of this department. Ninety percent of people had never even heard of this department before.

Of those who had heard of this department, ninety percent did not know what the role of this department was.

Based on its name, it should be a battlefront that united the entire Earth Federation. Still, this was truly thought-provoking. Was the Earth Federation United Front meant to fight any particular existences?

No one knew the answer.

However, in the command given, Chen Chen could pick out two key phrases, namely, “United Front” and “sculptures”. After discarding the former, the latter was the one that left a deeper impression on Chen Chen.

The shadows of the three stone sculptures flashed across his mind at once.

“Coincidence? Or...”

Chen Chen sat up in bed, concentrating his thoughts in the dark. After a long while, he asked again, “Did you find out anything in the end?”

“No.”

Little X replied, “I’ve investigated deep into the Federation’s official website, but couldn’t find the network entrance of the Federation United Front. However, I found several encrypted letters. It’s just that I’m afraid the Earth Federation will be immediately alerted once these letters are cracked.”

“Forget it then.”

Chen Chen stood up and turned toward the washroom. “The time in North America is UTC-8, whereas we’re now in UTC+1, which means we’re about eight hours[1] ahead of North America. North America should be going into nighttime now, right?”

“Yes, it’s seven in the evening over there.”

Little X replied, “I’ve cracked the network of the FBI in North America and discovered that the entire North America has gathered more than 100,000 sculptures at this point, all of which are peculiarly shaped or had a strange past. Even priceless artworks such as The Thinker and Mona Lisa are being intensively guarded...

“At present, the Federal Bureau of Investigation and the police have selected 1,300 suspected anomalous sculptures from these sculptures and issued over ten thousand letters requesting cooperation in the investigation. In the Los Angeles Federal Bureau of Investigation, one such letter for Anthony and Jennifer was also found...”

However, halfway through all this, Little X stopped abruptly, then said in surprise, “Sir Godfather, I’ve suddenly made an important discovery...”

“Don’t tell me that you’ve found the sculptures that were in Elysium.” Chen Chen’s expression sharpened.

“I’m sorry to say it, but yes.”

Little X lowered its voice and said in a somewhat surprised tone, “Among the Los Angeles FBI’s information, I’ve come across the photos and data of the three sculptures. Not only that, but I’ve also found information concerning Gross...”

Meanwhile, the monitor in the bedroom turned on automatically and a picture of the three sculptures appeared.

Sure enough, the three sculptures in the photo were the same as the ones Chen Chen had seen in Elysium’s dimension. The eyes, ears, and mouth were highlighted respectively, just like the saying in the Analects —”See no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil”.

Seeing that the sculptures were identical to the ones he remembered, Chen Chen narrowed his eyes.

As for Gross, that was also the name of the family in Elysium and also the name of the first owner of the three catastrophic sculptures...

It was as if reality and film were overlapping at this instance...

Chen Chen walked into the bathroom, washed up quickly, then stared at himself in the mirror as he wiped his face with a towel. “In other words, those three sculptures are now in the FBI branch in Los Angeles?”

“At the moment, yes.”

Chen Chen walked out of the bathroom. With a gentle flick of his right hand, an NZT-48 pill flew into his mouth. Following that, Chen Chen closed his eyes softly as countless deductions and facts began to surge through his brain...

“The Gross family, the three sculptures, the Federal Bureau of Investigation, the Federation United Front...”

Chen Chen mumbled. Endless theories and clues were automatically integrated and organized. Within a few seconds, Chen Chen had considered many things. “Now it seems that the Federation is looking for these three sculptures? However, they didn’t directly single out the ones owned by Gross, but rather, initially selected more than a thousand out of hundreds of thousands of sculptures?”

“Yes,” Little X replied, “in the information from the Federation United Front, there’s no clear objective, only two clear elements — North America and sculptures. I’m also sure that no other continent has received a similar mission.”

“In that case, the Earth Federation seems to have learned of something through some channel.”

Chen Chen wore a solemn look. “However, due to some reason, the information received by the Earth Federation isn’t comprehensive, so the Federation could only expand their scope and conduct a large-scale investigation throughout North America... Therefore, the Earth Federation seems to have a certain channel through which they knew about the danger of the three sculptures and the fact that these sculptures are in North America?”

“For the moment, that’s the case,” Little X responded.

Chen Chen suddenly sighed. “This is information that even we don’t know. If I hadn’t been to Elysium’s world, I’d have never thought of the dangers posed by a few ordinary sculptures...”

With that said, Chen Chen walked to the computer and began to look through the information that Little X had retrieved from the FBI. As expected, the origins, appearance, and owner of these three sculptures were the same as in Elysium’s dimension. They were all dug out of a gold mine by a person named Gross.

Moreover, precisely because these three sculptures were very special in appearance and origin, they were also collected by the Federation and selected as a candidate in the group of more than a thousand “suspected anomalies”.

After this, the sculptures with “suspected anomalies” would be transported to the Earth Federation headquarters in New York for subsequent processing.

After reading all this, Chen Chen finally displayed a woeful expression. “As I thought, it’s not just once.”

“Ah, what’s not just once?” Little X asked curiously.

“I mean that the unusual response of the Earth Federation has occurred more than once.”

Chen Chen sighed. “Didn’t you realize? I’ve had doubts ever since the Blacklight virus. Why was it that, when the Blacklight virus spread in Africa, countries around the world responded perfectly, almost scoring full marks? I’ve always thought it was because of what happened three years ago that provided the relevant experience, but there are still many things that don’t make sense.

“However, if you consider that along with the Earth Federation’s behavior this time around, it can all be explained.”

Chen Chen uttered each word slowly, “I suspect that the Earth Federation has something that lets them predict crises...”

“Something that predicts crises?” Little X tried saying this out softly.

“I say that it’s ‘something’ because I don’t know if it’s a person or an item or something like a scientific instrument.”

Chen Chen explained, “Still, according to my deductions, its existence can be ascertained. Even so, it appears to have a flaw — it’s unable to accurately pinpoint what the crisis will be.

“In other words, there are generalities and errors in its communication with the Earth Federation, which resulted in the responsive plans of various countries when the Blacklight virus struck last time and now the large-scale search for sculptures...

“I’ve even thought of something terrifying.”

Chen Chen suddenly lowered his voice and said, “In 1991, the Soviet Union disintegrated and the Earth Federation was established less than a year later. In the beginning, only the five core members joined, along with dozens of their smaller allies but as the Earth Federation became stronger and stronger, eventually, the other countries couldn’t withstand the pressure. They all joined this community of shared human fate...

“This was all only briefly mentioned in the textbooks. They only stated that, at that time, history was made, as that was the first great integration in human history. But now, in retrospect, there are fishy aspects to it that are truly unsettling...”

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes. “And one of the most fundamental questions is — why should humans be united in the absence of foreign enemies?”

“Huh?”

Little X seemed to realize something suddenly and exclaimed in a whisper.

“Sometimes, general knowledge is a horrifying thing.”

A smile tugged at the corner of Chen Chen’s lips. “A question like this is ridiculous, right? It’s like asking why one plus one equals two. Even a child can answer — because of humanity’s shared communal fate, the Soviet Union was disintegrated and human governments were federalized, establishing the Earth Federation.

“However, if you remove this ‘general knowledge’, you’ll find the hidden darkness beneath. Such as, why did the Soviet Union suddenly collapse? Was it only because its economy couldn’t support it? Why was the human federation established? In all this, are there external causes that ordinary people can’t know about?”

Chen Chen continued. “History textbooks only tell us that the strong countries integrated the weak countries and the weak countries depended on the strong countries...
