Adrian nodded and closed the file on the AD-001 drug and proceeded to open another file with details concerning the cancer targeted drug. A mix of surprise and shock appeared on his face. “This is completely different from the anti-cancer drug your group announced, this is a fourth variant of the anti-cancer drug!”

“That’s right.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Although Blacklight only publicly announced three anti-cancer drugs, we have six variants of anti-cancer drugs that can completely cure cancer. There are also over ten such anti-cancer drugs currently under development. The reason we haven’t announced them to the public is that these are currently restricted to the members of God’s Forbidden Zone only.”

“Members restricted?”

“It means that due to some of the techniques employed being too unconventional, we’ve decided to restrict it to a small group for the time being to avoid inciting a large societal shift.”

Chen Chen further explained, “By small group, I’m referring to the participating members of God’s Forbidden Zone. We develop new drugs with the help of the investment of each financial group. After commencing development, they’ll be made available for these members at first notice. This is the methodology of God’s Forbidden Zone.”

There was finally a mild look of intrigue on Adrian’s face upon hearing Chen Chen’s remark. “So what exactly prompted you to approach me?”

“There are two reasons why I’ve come here.”

Chen Chen narrated, “One. To invite Sir Adrian to join God’s Forbidden Zone. And two, I’d like to purchase all of the Osmond shares from your hands.”

“If I were to join, will I have access to all of the non-publicly available drugs listed on the website including AD-001?” Adrian’s eyes lit up.

“Yes. Only with your current status, you don’t have the privilege to be a part of the ‘Disciple’s Council’ in God’s Forbidden Zone, you will soon be though. However, if it pleases you, you may choose to be enlisted as an Alpha member, which is the highest-ranking member next to the Disciple Council. The only difference is that you will not have as much authority as the Disciple Council and don’t have access to free medical treatment.”

“Money won’t be a problem, I just want to get my hands on this AD-001.”

Adrian quickly responded by shaking his head.

“That won’t be a problem,” Chen Chen answered, “as long as you’re willing to transfer the shares of Osmond Biotech to us.”

“Are you worried about the off chance of Osmond developing another variant of a targeted drug for Alzheimer’s disease?” Adrian finally had an epiphany when he heard this. “Which is to say that Osmond’s research trajectory is correct after all!”

“Correct.”

Chen Chen nodded. “So, Sir Adrian, have you come to a decision yet?”

Adrian appeared visibly conflicted at this point. “I’m willing to join God’s Forbidden Zone but... what if I refuse to give away my shares in Osmond?”

Chen Chen smiled and turned to look at the cold corpse lying on the rug without a word.

Adrian immediately understood what Chen Chen was suggesting with this gesture. He let out a brief sigh and nodded. “I understand, I’ll have to consider this for a while...”

“No problem.” Chen Chen gave him a nod and got up politely. “Please give me an answer as soon as possible.”

With that, Chen Chen opened the door leading out of the study and left the room, disappearing at the end of the long and winding corridor.

Watching Chen Chen’s slowly vanishing figure, there was a look of uncertainty on Adrian’s face, he slowly descended into the crevices of his mind.

chapters.title: Chapter 257: Success
chapters.body:
Chapter 257: Success

Chapter 257: Success

In the end, Chen Chen purchased all of the shares from Munster for a fair sum of 1.2 billion.

Munster previously bought the shares at the price of 1 billion. A net profit of 200 million was considered rather generous already.

Although he was slightly upset, Adrian was well aware that this was the best possible outcome.

Though there was the possibility of Osmond developing a cure for Alzheimer’s disease in the future, it was all but speculation. Even if it were to succeed, it may be a matter of years or even decades.

If he wanted to cure his father’s disease as soon as possible, God’s Forbidden Zone was his best bet.

Besides, there was no end to the mysteriousness of the young man who came to him, one who even seemed to possess supernatural powers. If he was unwilling to hand out his shares, it may very well be an invitation to a great disaster.

The poker card which seemed to kill using an invisible force left a heavy impression on Adrian.

To date, Blacklight Biotechnology officially held 50% of Osmond’s shares. This piece of information quickly went viral, causing many people to speculate all sorts of likely scenarios that could come out of it.

Chen Chen also made an official announcement regarding Alzheimer’s disease targeted drug AD-001 in the inner circle of God’s Forbidden Zone.

Originally, Chen Chen was going to announce this in 2025 after experiencing the events as told in the movie. However, the situation was developing faster than he had expected. With Osmond slowly gaining momentum, he had to do something before it was too late.

Besides, this announcement was not exactly breaking news in the inner circle of God’s Forbidden Zone. Chen Chen had already made offhand mentions about it to the members of the Disciple Council as early as September last year.

At the time, the information Chen Chen revealed to them was: “In three years at most, a new line of targeted drugs will be produced to terminate the disease of the century.”. Although it had only been one year since, they should at least be mentally prepared for it.

Following the announcement, some of the financial groups who believed that Chen Chen was just all bark no bite this entire time began to properly reevaluate the technological prowess of God’s Forbidden Zone. Meanwhile, the ones who had been cautiously optimistic had become entirely convinced.

At the current moment, specialized drugs had been developed to alleviate the two major diseases targeted at the elderly, which were cancer and Alzheimer’s disease. The “Mankind Anti-aging Project” was gaining proper traction. The 30 years of lifespan granted by the age reversal treatment was also well insured.

Chen Chen quickly returned to Namibia without lingering for too long in North America. He still had another research project on his hands and could not afford to waste too much time.

Returning to the Spire Experimental Base, Chen Chen headed toward his secret laboratory while listening to Little X’s reports.

“So, you’re saying that during this period, you’ve decoded the entire genetic map of the tyrant clone?”

After getting sterilized and opening the door to the laboratory, Chen Chen was immediately greeted with the sight of a group of Black Knights, each occupied with a task.

“Yes, after making thorough comparisons, I’ve found that the genetic similarity between your tyrant clone and your ontology is 99.9%. Only 30 strands of genes showed signs of mutations.”

Little X explained, “What we’ll need to do now is to rewrite the genetics of the T-virus until we can produce a variant of T-virus that can transcribe the 30 genes in your body to a genetic structure similar to that of the tyrant clone. At that point, you’ll be able to safely inject yourself with the T-virus without any side effects, consequently attaining physical qualities like that of Alice in the movie.”

“You’ve done a great job.”

Chen Chen nodded with satisfaction. He had to admit that Little X was starting to become more independent. There may not be much by way of innovation but so long as Little X had data for reference from real life, she could transform them into something else of her own.

Lu Xun once said that the world did not come with paved roads, the roads were formed as a consequence of people treading its grounds.

Though Little X may not know to pave roads of her own, she knew how to use the roads paved by others. She could then tread the same grounds far better than the first ones who did it.

The next task of rewriting the genetic sequence of the T-virus was truly a work of endurance as Little X would have to try each path one by one. Chen Chen had found out the answer to the question and now had to work his way backward to acquire the formula to generate the answer.

When it came to dealing with genetics, an enormous amount of manpower and time would be needed to find the correct formula.

Naturally, Chen Chen did not need to burden himself with these tasks. He had Little X manning the operation and the Black Knights putting in the hard work without complaints. All he needed to do was to sit and wait patiently for the results.

After taking a lap around the laboratory, Chen Chen decided to take his leave.

“Sir Godfather, we currently have a good number of normal-temperature superconductors in production, would this be a good time to start working on building particle accelerators?”

Little X asked again.

“Let’s see how things go first.”

Chen Chen hesitated for a moment and responded, “As it is, we haven’t met the research requirements for particle physics. This is a sizable project on par with the Spire Experimental Base project. There’s a small chance of us hiding it from the Namibian government whether we’re talking about the materials required or the construction itself.”

“But if we don’t have a particle accelerator, we won’t be able to build isotopes or expert materials like high-offset metals,” Little X informed regretfully.

“There’s nothing we can do about it.”

Chen Chen nodded. “We’ve been gaining too much capital momentum lately, any more and we might find ourselves labeled a public enemy. For the moment, it’s best to lay low and wait for a year or two to allow our influence to spread across the Earth Federation. At that point, we can switch gears into development to properly accomplish our goals.”

“Understood,” Little X answered.

“Right, are the unmanned drones on the industrial zone’s end completed yet?”

Chen Chen asked, “I sent the spy drone from UAV to them as well as plenty of ultra-high precision machine tools, have they not produced any prototypes yet?”

“Concerning the drones, there are still some technological barriers yet to be overcome.”

Little X answered, “But the weapons expert Professor Charles yielded some results in just two days. They have designed the first batch of electromagnetic weapons by referring to the blueprints from Elysium.”

Chen Chen lit up at this revelation at last. “In that case, have Charles send me several prototypes.”

Following Chen Chen’s orders, several weapons closely resembling the “Cousar Crowe Rifle” from the movie were promptly delivered to him.

At the same time, Chen Chen had arrived at the shooting range in the Spire Experimental Base.

“These are the first batch of mid-range weapons manufactured according to the blueprints you provided.”

Little X’s voice came from the Bluetooth earphones. “Due to technological and time limitations, the heavy-range weapons are still under development. They are forecasted to be completed within a year.”

This did not elicit a response from Chen Chen. He silently picked up the weapon from the metal crate.

Based on appearance alone, the rifle was extremely peculiar. An ink-black and heavy body, no protruding stock or barrel structure, on a whole, it appeared like an enlarged version of AUG.

chapters.title: Chapter 258: Pulse Rifle
chapters.body:
Chapter 258: Pulse Rifle

Chapter 258: Pulse Rifle

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

Inside the movie, the Cousar Crowe Pulse Rifle was a portable weapon specifically designed for targeting humans and large vehicles.

This was an easy to control and lightly-built gun. Its relatively short range was compensated with immense power. The rifle also came with a set of anti-propulsion piston muzzle system, greatly reducing its recoil.

It was coated with a layer of pure black nitrate oxide paint, a single-point burst with a fully-automatic switch rifle that was not much larger than the common rifle. The sleeve of the gun was built extremely thick, giving it a powerful and sturdy feel.

There were two barrels at the front of its body. One of them was a gun barrel while the other barrel served as a pipeline to host a fuel cell. A hydrogen fuel cell with a diameter of 3 centimeters and a length of 20 centimeters was slotted inside it. Its instantaneous discharge voltage could reach up to 400V.

The barrel was inlaid with a ring layer of superconducting material. There was no need for any form of rifling installation as the bullets were propelled using electromagnetic force in accordance with the principle of Lorentz force, which spun the bullet at extreme speed using electric pressure.

Additionally, the bullets were crafted using magnetic metal. Based on the diamagnetic principle of superconducting materials, the propelled bullets should not make any form of contact with the gun barrel itself, thus essentially improving the lifespan of the firearm.

This form of electromagnetic rifle used the same gunpowder as the weapons which Chen Chen previously had Professor Charles design, which were powered by the Galaxy pendant. The traditional method of cartridge cased gunpowder was substituted with a modern solution, which allowed the magazine to be loaded with more bullets.

The electromagnetic rifle in discussion used a 5 x 20-millimeter shuttle-shaped hollow bullet. A regular magazine the size of a palm could hold up to 200 rounds.

Apart from the hollow rounds, there were also several other ammunition such as the spiral-shaped armor-piercing rounds and explosive rounds to choose from.

Apart from several core components such as the barrel, most of the gun was manufactured using engineered plastic, greatly reducing the weight of the gun. The gun was 54 centimeters long and 23 centimeters tall, weighing only 4.5 kilograms.

There was also a built-in biometric authentication system which prevented opponents from effectively using them.

Beholding the midnight-black, sturdy weapon designed for killing, Chen Chen switched it to single-burst mode. With his right hand, he set the butt of the gun on his right shoulder and supported the front of the gun with his left hand. He aimed at a target a hundred meters out with a three-point single-line motion aiming method.

After that, he gently held down the trigger.

Following a swift “swoosh”, a large hole of 3 centimeters in diameter was punctured into the steel plank a hundred meters away!

“This sound...”

A spark surfaced in Chen Chen’s eyes. As opposed to the usual popping sounds similar to that of a firecracker emitted by traditional gunpowder weaponry, this sound was closer to a short whistle caused by the friction produced in the air by an extremely fast-moving object, only mixed with a hint of the spark of electric current.

The decibel of a traditional firearm firing was extremely significant. Even when firing in a bustling city, the sound of gunshots could still be heard half a kilometer away. However, the noise produced by this weaponry barely reached 50 decibels. This was drastically lower than the usual decibel frequency produced by traditional gunpowder firearms which bordered around 145 to 160 decibels.

On top of that, thanks to the “anti-propulsion piston muzzle system” installation, Chen Chen only felt a tiny amount of recoil which was just like the recoil of a pistol.

“The reason that regular firearms are loud is caused by the difference of air pressure on its inside and the surroundings. The moment the bullet exits the barrel, it causes a sudden decrease of chamber pressure which produces a thunderous noise.”

Little X explained, “Since the new variant of the rifle doesn’t require chamber pressure, there’s only the sound of bullets slicing through the air.”

Chen Chen nodded, his eyes flashing with amusement. In his time in Namibia, he had tried his hands with a good amount of firearms, but this was the first time he had such a comfortable experience with a gun.

This was the legendary sci-fi gun as they called it. Perfect, perfect...

After that, Chen Chen experimented with the shooting speed. The Cousar Crowe Pulse Rifle could be shifted into either semi-automatic or fully-automatic modes. After switching into the fully-automatic mode, Chen Chen pulled the trigger. He instantly felt the gun coming to life in his hands.

After a wave of gentle vibration barely lasting ten seconds, the rifle produced a clicking sound, indicating that its bullets had been emptied.

“Such shooting speed is double that of an ordinary machine gun...”

Chen Chen touched the barrel of the gun and only felt mild heat on its surface. Needless to say, this was the diamagnetic effect of the superconductor at play. With this implementation, the metal should not experience any wear and tear even after firing tens of thousands of rounds.

Upon testing, it was concluded that this gun could fire more than 1,200 rounds per minute, blowing the regular 17 shots per second of common guns out of the water.

In the end, the firearms statistics as tested by Chen Chen was: 545mm long, weighed 4545g after loading, aiming baseline 203mm, barrel diameter 8mm, magazine capacity 200 rounds, initial speed 1200m/s, kinetic energy 2500J, and effective range of 600m.

Chen Chen was admittedly floored by such ridiculous statistics. Even a bullet shot from a regular rifle could not reach velocity up to 800m/s as its velocity should be hovering around 1000J and effective range merely 400 meters. Even a bullet shot from a sniper rifle could not achieve the bullet speed as displayed by this rifle.

As far as its destructiveness was concerned, though it may not reach the heights of high-caliber sniper rifles, it still held up well enough on its own. With the help of armor-piercing rounds, it had no trouble piercing through 50 millimeters thick steel panels.

“Not bad at all.”

After concluding the tests, Chen Chen let out a sigh. “No muzzle friction, comparable to a silencer, low recoil, destructiveness close to a sniper rifle... With all these features perfectly incorporated together, it belongs to a class of its own. With the Black Knights fully fitted with these weapons, who would dare challenge us on the battlefield?”

“These are only the middle to low range electromagnetic weapons only.”

Little X explained, “The superior firepower belongs to the high range electromagnetic weapons such as the electromagnetic railguns. Using an enormous amount of electric pressure, it compresses hundreds of kilograms of shells and accelerates them to first cosmic velocity. The resulting blast can instantly flatten a mountain or obliterate an entire city in an instant.”

Chen Chen broke into a smile. “We sure can manufacture such weapons, but then how do we hit the city? These aren’t anti-aircraft weaponry, not unless we mount these electromagnetic cannons on top of satellites and fire from above.”

“That’s true.”

Little X pondered on the subject. “Which is to say that the most viable option on Earth are still missiles?”

“Yes. For instance, intercontinental missiles that can pass through the atmosphere and then dive at several times the speed of sound are conventional weapons of warfare on Earth.”

Chen Chen nodded. “So, unless we have missiles and established air dominance, we cannot let the Earth Federation find out what we’re doing. Otherwise, what good is a superior military force if we don’t have an established air dominance? We’re just live targets.”

“According to the information extracted from the Elysium universe, there’s a mini aircraft called the ‘Bugatti-Folge Luxury Transporter’. It can pass through the atmosphere layer and travels back and forth between the Earth and Elysium. It’s only the size of a car.”

Little X considered for a moment. “Can you try to accumulate enough energy to extract it?”

It was then when Chen Chen suddenly recalled that John Carlyle, the misfortunate bloke in the movie who was the CEO of Armadyne, seemed to have boarded the aircraft.

Inside the movie, after the protagonist shot down the aircraft John Carlyle was in, John’s memory was taken away by the protagonist before his death. He was probably the upper-class member who died the most embarrassing death.

After giving it some more thought, Chen Chen shook his head and shot down Little X’s theory. “This aircraft doesn’t have any built-in invisibility feature. My list of requirements for an aircraft includes optical, infrared, and radar invisibility capabilities. Besides, simply having one or two of such aircraft won’t change things in the grand scheme. What I need is an aircraft that can be infinitely reproduced.”

“Which means that at the end of the day, it’s still an issue of industrial development.”

Chen Chen expressed, “If memory serves me correctly, most of these aircraft use nuclear fusion engines to traverse space. Although we have our hands on information concerning nuclear fusion engines, we still have a long way to go before we can reproduce them.”

“So, for our next step, we will still have to tackle the issue of nuclear fusion...”

chapters.title: Chapter 259: Nuclear Fusion Device
chapters.body:
Chapter 259: Nuclear Fusion Device

Chapter 259: Nuclear Fusion Device

The three forms of nuclear reactions known to mankind were nuclear fission, nuclear fusion, and nuclear decay.

Among these, nuclear fission and nuclear fusion provided energy for humans. According to Einstein’s mass-energy equation, E=mc^2, as long as there was mass attenuation after the nuclear reaction, it meant that a certain amount of energy had been released.

Nuclear fission was the production of energy through mass loss caused by the fission of heavy atoms.

On the contrary, with nuclear fusion, the aggregation of light atomic nuclei produced heavier atomic nuclei and mass attenuation would occur, thereby generating energy.

Nuclear fusion was better than nuclear fission not only because nuclear fusion released a larger amount of energy, but also, more importantly, the raw materials for nuclear fusion could be extracted from seawater. Deuterium was abundant in the Earth’s seawater, amounting to 40 zillion tons. If all of it was used in fusion reactions, the energy released would last humans tens of billions of years.

The common raw material for nuclear fission — uranium-235 — had scarce reserves and the resulting nuclear waste would affect geology for hundreds of years. Therefore, nuclear fission could not compare to nuclear fusion, both in terms of reserves and cleanliness.

Therefore, nuclear fusion energy was an infinite, clean, and safe new energy that countless people yearned for.

Of course, nuclear fusion did not refer only to deuterium-tritium fusion. As long as an element was lighter than iron in the periodic table, it could be used for nuclear fusion. The reason humans chose only tritium and deuterium as fusion materials was that their conditions for nuclear fusion were the simplest.

Compared with other elements, the hydrogen isotope deuterium and the tritium nucleus both only had one proton. The fusion reaction between tritium and deuterium just needed to overcome the repulsive force between the two protons, while other elements would have to overcome the repulsive force between a bunch of protons. The difficulty was not the same.

Now that Chen Chen had determined his next step to conquer nuclear fusion, he returned to his office and dug out the nuclear fusion reactor technology from the world of Elysium.

Still, after a few glances, Chen Chen frowned. He had always specialized in medicine and biology. Although he had some understanding of nuclear physics, it was superficial, a little better than lay people at most.

Thus, Chen Chen looked up and said, “X, put together immediately a document that will allow me to quickly understand nuclear physics.”

As he spoke, he took out the chewing gum tin box, opened it gently, and an NZT-48 pill instantly flew into his mouth.

After that, Chen Chen’s expression turned sharp and clear.

At the same time, Little X had swiftly compiled information on nuclear physics from the basics to advanced stuff. Chen Chen flipped through the technology document Little X had provided while corroborating it against the data regarding Elysium’s nuclear reactor.

This time, Chen Chen could understand almost everything.

“I didn’t think that there would be more than one type of nuclear fusion reactor technology used in Elysium’s dimension.”

Chen Chen smiled and turned to the second drawing. “It’s an inertial confinement nuclear fusion reactor and a magnetic confinement nuclear fusion reactor? How incredible...”

“The ones mainly used across the continents of the Earth Federation are mostly these two types too.”

Little X explained, “But if these technologies are subdivided, they can be divided into multiple branches. There are even jokes of that sort circulating on the Internet...”

“What jokes?” Chen Chen could not refrain from asking.

“They would divide all kinds of fusion technology into the nine camps of DnD’s alignment chart in a straightforward and intuitive manner. Each camp is even represented by an anthropomorphic statement.”

Little X explained, “For example, a stellarator that represents ‘lawful good’ would state: My heart and my actions are as calm as stars and everything I do is steady.

“As for the neutral good magnetic confinement fusion, it’s anthropomorphic statement is: there’s no magnetism to exude, no helium-3 to combust, because I don’t want to be a reactor anymore!

“And the chaotic good tokamak: Plasma current, I’ll tell you again — don’t you cause any more trouble!”

“In addition, there’s the spherical tokamak representing lawful neutral, the field-reversed configuration tokamak representing true neutral, the magnetized target fusion representing chaotic neutral, and so on...”

As Little X went on, its voice became increasingly excited. “The funniest one is the laser fusion technology that represents chaotic evil — its anthropomorphic statement is: I’m about to f*cking shoot my load!”

“...”

Chen Chen looked up, startled. “X, did I hear correctly — you’re cracking a dirty joke?”

“I, I didn’t...”

Little X started to stammer. “How is that a dirty joke? Humans are dirty jokes. I’m an intelligent lifeform... Are dirty jokes even possible with intelligent lifeforms? I’m just referring to a meme, the meme of nuclear physics!”

Chen Chen shook his head and did not press the matter. He just took note that Little X was becoming increasingly human. Meanwhile, he said, “Now I understand why the development of nuclear fusion is more difficult than nuclear fission. It’s because the physical principles of the two reactions are different.

“The mass of nuclear fission nuclei is relatively large and it’s unstable, so fission reactions can be induced under normal temperature and pressure. However, nuclear fusion is different, for it must overcome the strong electrostatic repulsion between the nuclei. Without enough energy or special environmental conditions to break past the critical point of this repulsive force, one wouldn’t be able to fuse atomic nuclei together.

“So, the key is to create a high-pressure, high-temperature environment?”

“Yup.”

Little X replied, “The most common nuclear fusion reaction in the universe is the Sun. Because of its own mass, the Sun forms an ultra-high pressure of 200 billion atmospheres at its core, plus a temperature of 15 million degrees. Thus it can fuse hydrogen atoms into helium atoms.

“However, if humans want to achieve the same nuclear fusion on earth, the ultra-high pressure conditions would be completely impossible to achieve. Therefore, humans’ current nuclear fusion devices need to perform at high temperatures, requiring a temperature of more than 100 million degrees compared to the Sun’s core temperature of 15 million degrees.

“The simplest, most direct method is to use a large number of energized coils to form a ring along the coils, generating a strong magnetic field that produces a magnetically confined nuclear fusion reactor. This device that can generate a sufficiently strong ring-shaped magnetic field is called a ‘tokamak device’.

“The other option, which is the inertial confinement nuclear fusion reactor, uses a gaseous mixture of deuterium and tritium, measured to the milligram, to fill a small ball a few millimeters in diameter. A laser beam heats it up uniformly from the outside. The spherical surface absorbs the energy and expands outward, producing a reaction force where the gas inside is compressed and highly pressurized, accompanied by a sharp rise in temperature. When the temperature reaches the required ignition point, the gas in the ball will explode and produce a lot of heat!”

chapters.title: Chapter 260: Laser Components
chapters.body:
Chapter 260: Laser Components

Chapter 260: Laser Components

“This process of this explosion takes a very short time, only a few picoseconds. As long as three or four such explosions occur every second and continue without break, the energy released will be equivalent to a million-kilowatt power station!”

Upon hearing Little X’s explanation, Chen Chen nodded immediately. “I know a little about the tokamak device, but I remember that the tokamak has a huge flaw. The other one is the inertial confinement fusion reactor. Does the Earth Federation have similar devices?”

“Yes, the tokamak device that represents magnetic confinement does have a big flaw because the core of the tokamak device is where the magnetic field appears. To generate a magnetic field, you need to use a coil. With a coil, there’ll be an electrical lead and with an electrical lead, there’ll be resistance.’

Little X replied, “The more practical a tokamak device is, the stronger the magnetic field has to be and the greater the current that must be passed through the lead. Thus, the resistance of the wire comes into play. The resistance will reduce the efficiency of the coil and limit the intensity of the current so that the tokamak device can’t generate an adequate magnetic field.

“As such, superconducting technology is very important. Today’s Earth Federation has made low-temperature superconductors into coils so that the problem with both the volume and the loss of current can be solved. Therefore, the combination of this superconductor and the tokamak device is called the super tokamak device.

“Today, there are only three continents in the Earth Federation that have a super tokamak, namely the North American Continent, Mainland China, and the European Continent. Of these, there are five in North America, two in China, and one in Europe.

“As for inertial confinement fusion reactors, this equipment is available in many countries and continents all over the world, but the one that is truly famous is the National Ignition Facility Project in North America or NIF for short.

“This device focuses 2 million joules of energy through 192 laser beams onto a deuterium-tritium pellet with a diameter of 3 millimeters. Each laser beam discharges for about three-billionths of a second, generating a high temperature of 100 million degrees. The pressure exceeds 100 billion atmospheres, which triggers nuclear fusion.”

“I got it.”

Chen Chen snapped his fingers. “In other words, magnetic confinement nuclear fusion uses a strong magnetic field to confine deuterium and tritium in a magnetic container, heating it to hundreds of millions of degrees Celsius to achieve fusion reactions. On the other hand, inertial confinement uses ultra-high intensity lasers, irradiating deuterium and tritium in very short bursts to produce the fusion reaction.”

After understanding this, Chen Chen looked at the nuclear fusion reactor technology from Elysium and suddenly saw the light at the end of the tunnel.

In simple terms, the focus of each of the two technologies, magnetic confinement and inertial confinement, was not the same. The magnetically confined super tokamak device had a good nuclear fusion reaction effect and did not require repeated ignition, but was disadvantaged in that it was large and rather inflexible. Moreover, the cost of electricity required to maintain a strong magnetic field was not low.

Therefore, the super tokamak device was used as a nuclear power plant reactor in Elysium’s world.

The advantage of inertial confinement was that the equipment could be made to be very small and one would have more control over the ignition fire. Even so, the disadvantage was that it had to consume a lot of energy to generate lasers for continuous ignition.

Therefore, the inertial confinement device was used as a spacecraft engine in Elysium’s world.

Chen Chen reviewed these two technologies and compared them with the nuclear fusion devices of the countries in the Earth Federation. He found that the biggest factor. Other than conceptual innovation, that allowed these two types of nuclear fusion devices to be produced in Elysium’s world was the normal-temperature superconductor.

The normal-temperature superconductor eliminated the need for a liquid helium cooling system in the super tokamak device, thus greatly reducing its complexity and cost. Coupled with the stability of high-displacement metal, a mature nuclear fusion device was naturally a matter of course.

The same was true for the inertial confinement device. The normal-temperature superconductor could increase the energy released by the laser ignition device several times over, thereby augmenting the laser’s energy, even forming a laser effect called “ultra-high field”. This would instantly produce an output of 200×1015 watts, which was 100,000 times larger than the total global electric energy output, yet took only less than one trillionth of a second.

With this energy, the fuel formed by deuterium and tritium could reach the critical point of nuclear fusion at once. One would not require something as complicated and huge as the NIF device, whose efficiency was not high enough anyway.

Even so, the only caveat was that if one wished to create a mature super tokamak, one still needed to use materials like high-displacement metal. Chen Chen was not capable of producing this high-displacement metal for the time being. To do so, he would need the help of particle accelerators.

Chen Chen could only flip to the page “High-Displacement Metal” and pore over its contents.

The so-called high-displacement metal was a kind of aluminum. However, it was not normal aluminum, but an isotope of aluminum.

Generally speaking, metals such as aluminum did not produce isotopes, but with the help of particle accelerators, artificial interference and manufacturing became possible.

For example, the high-displacement metal made of metallic aluminum was essentially an aluminum element with a high number of excess neutrons. One would first rely on superconductivity at room temperature to form a strong magnetic field, then used a particle accelerator to fire iron atoms through the strong magnetic region.

As the magnetic field in this region had reached an energy level that could break apart an atom, the iron atoms would disintegrate when passing through the magnetic field. The electrons would fly to one side of the magnetic field, while the protons would fly to the other side, leaving only the neutron which continued to fly forward, unaffected, while maintaining its flight trajectory.

After this, as long as these neutrons were collected by a resonance machine and electrons were attached to the surface of the neutrons with a strong electric combiner, virtual particles could be produced.

Due to the virtual particles being negatively charged, one only had to use the particle accelerator again to accelerate the virtual particles and accelerate them to a subluminal state, so that they could be used to bombard aluminum atoms.

As they had attained subluminal speed with immense kinetic energy, these virtual particles could overcome the repulsion force and forcefully penetrate the aluminum atom. In this way, the aluminum atom would be filled with a large number of neutrons, causing the element structure to shift. This was why it was called high-displacement metal.

Due to the high imbalance of neutron content, this material was extremely insensitive to energy conduction and completely nullified thermal energy weapons and thermal wave weapons. Coupled with super metallic strength and durability, it was far better than any alloy. The datasheet even named it the toughest substance within the solar system.

Since it was impossible to manufacture a super tokamak device, Chen Chen could only step back for now and build the other type — the inertial confinement device reactor.

To build this kind of reactor, Chen Chen would have to purchase many high-precision laser equipment components, then modify them to create a device that could emit ultra-high field lasers.

As he considered this, Chen Chen instinctively stroked his chin.

It looked like the time had come for those financial groups to be put to work. Had he not united this bunch of people precisely because of the influence they possessed?

chapters.title: Chapter 261: Chinese New Year
chapters.body:
Chapter 261: Chinese New Year

Chapter 261: Chinese New Year

The perfect super tokamak device required high-displacement metal because the energy generated by fusion needed to be absorbed by an inner wall called a “cladding”.

The “cladding”, also known as the “first wall of the fusion reactor”, was the inner wall directly exposed to the fusion reaction. What it had to face was plasma with a high temperature of tens of millions of degrees or even hundreds of millions of degrees, and a large amount of neutron radiation. The strength of the material depended on the arrangement of atoms. Under such extreme conditions, after the atoms were continuously bombarded, the performance of the material would soon deteriorate.

Therefore, this kind of inner wall would be manufactured in an extremely complicated manner. For example, in the “International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor” project, the thickness of the tokamak device’s cladding was between 80 to 100 centimeters, but faced with high-energy neutrons, this thickness was still not enough.

Therefore, the emergence of outlandish materials such as high-displacement metals was necessary.

Nonetheless, now Chen Chen did not have the capability to make high-displacement metals, so he could only go for the next best thing and come up with a way to build an inertial fusion reactor instead.

Inertial confinement fusion was also called laser fusion. It required very high laser power as well as high-precision optical instruments to control the lasers’ aim. The requirements for the symmetry of implosion were very demanding. In the entire world, only the NIF project in the North American Continent could operate 193 laser devices simultaneously.

In Elysium’s dimension, this could be done with just 48 lasers, but on the condition that the laser transmitters were in a supersymmetric state.

Of course, Chen Chen would not count on locating the components for these laser transmitters in Namibia. He asked Little X to look around and directly customized a large number of optical components and control chips from various high-tech companies in North America. Then, he ordered a large number of lenses and optical components from Europe.

Some of these suppliers even served ASML Holding in the Netherlands. The components of the EUV lithography machines were produced by these suppliers.

As for other parts that he could not acquire, Chen Chen would have to rely on those financial groups.

Thus, at the beginning of the new year, 2024, Chen Chen returned to Handu and spent the Chinese New Year with his parents.

According to the tradition of Mainland China, Chinese New Year was a time to visit one’s elders and the first day, in particular, was spent visiting one’s paternal elders.

Chen Chen’s father had two brothers and one sister. Among them, Chen Chen’s father was the youngest, which meant that Chen Chen had two uncles and one aunt, who also had a few kids of their own.

The eldest of these cousins was the son of Chen Chen’s first uncle. He had turned 30 this year and was thus independent, having established his own family. Chen Chen heard that he worked in a bank. The youngest of the cousins was the youngest daughter of Chen Chen’s second uncle. She was the same age as Chen Chen and was in her senior year, about to graduate in half a year.

In truth, Chen Chen was not used to this kind of annual family gathering. When he was young, Chen Chen’s father was assigned to the frontier area because he was admitted into the military academy. Chen Chen grew up in the frontier from birth, up until he was eight or nine years old.

Chen Chen’s father was not promoted in the army even after a long time. Thus, after nearly ten years as a battalion commander, he chose to transfer to his hometown of Handu and worked as an ordinary civil servant on a basic level.

However, who could have imagined that he would work this job for the rest of his life?

After his father moved them back to Handu, only then did Chen Chen begin to meet his parents’ relatives. Chen Chen’s life was not bad, namely, it was the life of an ordinary working family. Every annual family gathering was rather socially conventional.

Chen Chen’s two paternal uncles were pretty much the same. Only Chen Chen’s paternal aunt rose above the pack and became deputy chief of the Electric Power Bureau. She had always been the core of the annual family gatherings.

However, over the past two years, as more news about Blacklight Biotechnology reached them, the relatives gradually understood what was happening.

Perhaps they did not know much else, but at the very least, they knew that Blacklight Biotechnology was a giant multinational corporation and what multinational corporation would not be worth hundreds of millions?

Following that, as Chen Chen’s parents purchased a villa in an upscale area, the other members became increasingly friendlier to Chen Chen’s family. Maybe Chen Chen’s parents could now hold their heads up higher.

“Little Chen is 22 this year, right? He’s truly growing up.”

At the dinner table, Chen Chen’s paternal aunt sighed. “I still remember when Little Chen was this tiny thing a few years ago. To think that he had grown so much in the blink of an eye...”

“Oh right, surely it’s time to talk about finding a partner. Little Chen, have you taken a liking to any girl over here?”

The wife of his second uncle quickly clarified, “Although foreign women aren’t too bad, with genes you can never tell how they’ll mutate. For instance, our neighbor married a large, foreign woman, who ended up giving birth to a little black baby. Our people’s genes are still more stable...”

“?”

Chen Chen raised his head as if he just heard them talking.

“Little Chen is still young, right?”

Chen Chen’s father felt rather awkward. “Now he still has to focus on his career. We can afford to wait three to five years.”

“Hey, Fourth Brother, well-said!”

Chen Chen’s uncle gave a thumbs up. “After all, a man must be ambitious in all aspects. Wonderful, wonderful!”

Everyone roared with laughter.

“By the way, Little Chen, now that you’ve started a company, there should be a lot of socializing involved, huh?”

Chen Chen’s uncle raised a glass. “And you’re not a kid anymore. Don’t leave me hanging — have a drink with me?”

“I’m not a drinker.” Chen Chen waved his hand.

“Hey, Little Chen, you’re being rather prim and proper.”

Chen Chen’s uncle wore a stiff expression as he said unhappily, “If you don’t want to drink, just say that. You’re a big boss now. Who would believe that you don’t drink?”

“Uncle, I’m really not a drinker.”

Chen Chen smiled softly. He was not putting on airs. This was the truth.

The movie had already shown that, after taking NZT-48, the male protagonist went to the bar to fool around while the drug was still in effect. As a result, after drinking a few alcoholic drinks, he fell into a fragmented state of mind and had no memory of anything that happened in the next ten hours.

To someone like Chen Chen who tried to control everything, this kind of situation where he was out of control was completely unacceptable. Thus, since the day he obtained NZT-48, he had never taken a drop of alcohol.

“Little Chen, today’s a holiday anyway. Why don’t you have a drink or two with your uncle?”

On one side, Chen Chen’s father tried to smooth things over while subtly hinting at Chen Chen.

Chen Chen’s father had always been a traditional person who adhered to conventions. He did not wish to see Chen Chen disobeying his elders.

Seeing this, Chen Chen smiled slightly, poured all of his Pepsi into an empty glass, then said to his uncle, “Uncle, a toast to you.”

With that, he downed the soda in one big gulp.

At this, Chen Chen’s uncle did not say much, only drank his own wine resentfully before sitting down. The atmosphere around the table was a little gloomy for a while.

After the meal, Chen Chen sat on the sofa and played with his phone out of boredom. This was how Chen Chen had always acted — an introverted person of few words. If no one took the initiative to strike up a conversation, Chen Chen could go a whole day without uttering a word.

However, if in the past, Chen Chen was merely a little low-key in gatherings with such a personality. Now, this was no longer the case. Although everyone seemed to be talking animatedly on the surface, in fact, everyone’s attention was focused, intentionally or unconsciously, on Chen Chen.

After just sitting on the sofa for a while, Chen Chen felt everyone was watching him secretly. His cousin Chen Zheng, the son of his first uncle, was also rather fidgety as he repeatedly glanced over at Chen Chen.

After hesitating for a long time, Chen Zheng finally plucked up the courage, walked wordlessly to Chen Chen, and tapped Chen Chen on the shoulder.

“Little Chen, come here for a moment. I have something to tell you!”

Chen Zheng said in a low voice.

Chen Chen nodded, put away his phone, stood up, and followed Chen Zheng out of the living room.

When Chen Chen stood up, since he was the center of the gathering, almost everyone looked over subconsciously. Upon noticing everyone’s reaction, Chen Zheng, who was walking in front, blushed even more and pretended to have seen nothing.

Chen Chen’s second aunt even secretly pinched his second uncle on the arm.

Chen Zheng only breathed a sigh of relief after he reached the yard, away from everyone. He took out a pack of cigarettes, drew out two cigarettes, and handed one to Chen Chen.

Chen Chen waved his hand to indicate that he did not smoke.

Seeing this, Chen Zheng was a little embarrassed. “You’ve founded a company, but you don’t even smoke?”

“Carbon monoxide kills human brain cells.” Chen Chen pointed to his head, then said, “Cousin, please say what you want to say.”

“Cough. In that case, I’ll be frank.”

Chen Zheng coughed softly. “Here’s the thing, Cousin. I’ve become tired of working in the bank recently. Seeing that you’ve done so well now, I also thought of starting a company and investing in a project. Over the years I’ve saved about 200,000. I just need a little more, so I was wondering if you could lend me a little...”

By this point, Chen Zheng’s face was thoroughly flushed red.

Chen Chen did not agree immediately, but asked, “What project is this? Does your wife know?”

“I want to start an advertising company. My wife disagrees with it, so I came to you.”

Chen Zheng shook his head and smiled bitterly. “In her opinion, starting a company isn’t as stable as working in a bank, after all. But I know that if I continue to work there, that’ll be it for the rest of my life. I really don’t want to end up that way...”

After he heard this, Chen Chen thought for a while. “Why don’t I invest one million RMB, all as shares, and occupy 70% of the company’s shares. Cousin, you’ll be responsible for managing the company and you’ll own 30% of the shares. As for your funds, don’t use them. I hear that your wife’s pregnant, so it’s better to keep that money for emergencies.”

“Really?”

Chen Zheng was overjoyed upon hearing this. “Thank you, Cousin, I didn’t expect you to be this generous!”

Chen Chen waved his hand. “It’s fine. I’ll be staying in Handu for the time being these few days. Hit me up after you’ve found a lawyer and drawn up a contract.”

With that said, Chen Chen returned to the room.

When Chen Chen reached the sofa again, he sighed inwardly.

He knew in his heart that after he made his fortune, this kind of thing was indeed inevitable. As early as one or two years ago, various relatives had taken a fair sum of money from Chen Chen’s parents under the pretext of borrowing money.

Although Chen Chen would not feel distressed even if the “borrowed” money was a thousand times more than it was, this sort of thing was still pretty uncomfortable.

Even so, Chen Zheng was different. Like the old Chen Chen, Chen Zheng belonged to that breed of honest, quiet men who rarely asked for help. As far as Chen Chen knew, among all his relatives, it was only his first uncle’s family that had not borrowed money from Chen Chen’s parents.

Now that Chen Zheng had made a request, Chen Chen was naturally willing to give him a chance.

However, even though Chen Zheng had described it as “borrowing”, in reality, he had no ability to repay Chen Chen. If this ended up as a loss, surely Chen Chen could not make his cousin put up his home as collateral. In any case, Chen Chen’s parents would not allow Chen Chen to do so.

Therefore, Chen Chen simply joined Chen Zheng’s entrepreneurship in the form of a shareholding system. Whether they ended up making a profit or losing money, Chen Chen would barely feel it.

Furthermore, if they ended up making a loss, their partnership would naturally end there. Even if Chen Zheng wanted to borrow more money, Chen Chen would have a reason to turn him down and shut him up.

After Chen Chen had sat back down on the sofa for a while, his second uncle, urged impatiently by his wife, finally came over with an apologetic smile on his face. “Little Chen, come with me. I have something to tell you...”

Chen Chen did not look up, only swiping his phone as he said, “Second Uncle, I’m a little tired today. Just say what you need to say.”

Chen Chen had learned from the bodyguard assigned to his parents that his second uncle had already “borrowed” more than 800,000 RMB from Chen Chen’s father, to the point that even a people-pleaser like Chen Chen’s father was unwilling to lend him any more money...

“Er...”

His second uncle looked embarrassed instantly, turning to glance at his wife before he steeled his nerves with a grin. “Here’s the thing — money’s rather tight for me these days. Is it possible for you to lend me some?”

“Second Uncle, I recall that you’ve borrowed quite a sum from my father. You’re still borrowing more?”

Chen Chen pretended to be puzzled. “You know, it’s not easy for me out there, working all alone. At first, I wanted to save up to get married, but I didn’t expect all my money to be loaned to everyone. I’ve not even seen an IOU at this point. I’m in a difficult position too.”

“Little Chen, we won’t believe that you have no money!”

Finally, Chen Chen’s second aunt who had been secretly commandeering her husband could not help speaking up. “Who doesn’t know that Blacklight Biotechnology is a multinational corporation? Everyone on the Internet knows that the boss of Blacklight Biotechnology is worth tens of billions. Plus, your family lives in a villa worth millions and drives a luxury car worth over a million. We just want to borrow such a small sum and we’re close relatives, after all. Do you need to be so stingy?”

The moment she opened her mouth, out spewed a torrent of vitriol.

The atmosphere swiftly turned cold and silent for a while. There was a touch of embarrassment on everyone’s faces.

Chen Chen simply ignored her and stood up promptly. “Dad, Mom, let’s go.”

“Oh, okay, okay...”

Chen Chen’s father sighed as if waking up from a dream, then bade farewell to everyone. He dragged Chen Chen’s mother with him and they walked out.

“You guys...”

The second aunt was stupefied. She could only stare blankly but ultimately did not know what to say.

After leaving his grandmother’s place, Chen Chen drove his parents back. Chen Chen’s father sighed frequently along the way as if he still did not understand.

“Why is Second Brother, who was such a good man, suddenly acting in this manner?” Chen Chen’s father could not help blurting out this question.

“Perhaps that’s just life...”

Chen Chen answered quietly.

chapters.title: Chapter 262: Murderous Intent
chapters.body:
Chapter 262: Murderous Intent

Chapter 262: Murderous Intent

On the seventh day of Chinese New Year, Chen Chen got ready to take a train to Shangdu. He was going to visit the head office and pop in to see Professor Wang Xi while he was there.

Chen Chen bade farewell to his parents and walked out of the door.

However, just after leaving the house, Chen Chen received a message from Little X via his Bluetooth headset.

“Sir Godfather, the T-Knights of the defense team have discovered two international spies outside your door who are trying to get in touch with you!”

“Spies?”

Chen Chen inquired softly, “Where are they from?”

“The East China Sea Continent.”

Little X replied, “I can trace their identities through their communication records. They both seem to be members of the Handu Chamber of Commerce in Mainland China, but they’ve been frequently in touch with the contact person of the secret department in the East China Sea Continent. It can be determined that they’re spies from the East China Sea.

“But don’t worry, these two people don’t mean you any harm. Their mission seems to be to persuade you.”

Chen Chen nodded.

When he walked out of the community gate, Chen Chen saw two men in neat suits standing outside the gate. When the two of them saw Chen Chen, their eyes suddenly lit up and they smiled enthusiastically. “Mr. Chen Chen, we’ve been waiting for you for a long time!”

“I don’t seem to know you two. You are...”

Chen Chen pretended to look mystified.

“I apologize. We’re employees of Huaguang Credit Company!”

The man at their head smiled and said, “My name is Wei Changmin and my colleague next to me is named Li Ren. We at Huaguang Credit want to discuss some company affairs with you. Do you have some time?”

As he spoke, the man took out a business card and handed it to Chen Chen, looking extremely humble.

“I don’t have much time these days. Please head back.”

Chen Chen waved his hand.

“Mr. Chen Chen, it’ll only take a while. We won’t hold you up for too long...”

The man named Wei Changmin tried to persuade Chen Chen.

Since Chen Chen knew their identities, he could not be bothered with them. He immediately walked past them and left in a taxi.

As they watched the taxi pulling away, the two of them dropped their smiles. The man named Li Ren could not help frowning. “Wu Tengjun, sure enough, we failed.”

“It’s to be expected.”

The other man waved one hand and solemnly said, “He’s able to become the world’s first genius, who has developed the Blacklight virus vaccine and has multiple patents in the field of cutting-edge medicine. Of course, such a formidable man has his pride but we needn’t be too discouraged. We must trust that faith can move mountains!”

“Wu Tengjun, you’re right!”

After this brief exchange, the two men turned to leave.

Of course, these two people did not know that they had already been spotted when they approached the community where Chen Chen’s parents lived. Their identities had also been exposed long ago.

Back then, for safety reasons, Chen Chen had sent five special forces soldiers under Cheng Cao to protect his parents. He gave each of them a GS Combat Suit, a cloaking device, and a memory eraser.

Of these five people, two were acting as chauffeurs in Chen Chen’s parents’ home while the remaining three were secretly providing protection in shifts.

At the end of last year, Chen Chen once again sent a Black Knight force of five men and five women, expanding the secret security team to fifteen members.

Chen Chen had even bought the houses of his neighbors without his parents knowing. Now, these villas looked just ordinary residences on the surface, but they had long been managed stringently by Chen Chen. Any strangers would have nowhere to hide in this area.

Soon, Chen Chen reached the train station.

As it was the New Year, it was the peak period for returning home at this time. There was a turbulent flow of people in the station. At first glance, the crowd was dense with almost no end in sight.

Chen Chen chose the train because there was a direct route from Handu to Shangdu, traversing more than 2,000 kilometers in just five hours. Although it would take longer, it was more flexible and safer than flying.

However, just as Chen Chen had squeezed into the crowd of hundreds and entered the station, Little X suddenly notified him, “Sir Godfather, your second uncle has arrived at your parents’ house...”

Chen Chen frowned. “Why are they there?”

“It appears that they’ve come to borrow money and are arguing with your parents...” Little X said feebly.

“Ask Lil Wang and the rest to watch closely. Don’t let my parents get hurt,” Chen Chen exhorted.

Lil Wang was one of Chen Chen’s bodyguards assigned to his parents. By now, he had been with Chen Chen’s parents for nearly three years and had fully won their trust.

However, before Chen Chen had finished speaking, Little X suddenly cried out, “Oh no, Sir Godfather, your mother has been pushed by your second aunt and has fallen to the ground and passed out!”

At this time, Chen Chen was entering the ticket gate. After hearing these words, his expression suddenly shifted and he turned back and squeezed his way out.

“Is my mother badly hurt? What about Lil Wang and the rest, are they just sitting around being useless trash?”

Chen Chen asked malevolently as he rushed out of the station.

“She has no wounds. The bodyguards are taking your parents to the hospital...”

It was the first time Little X was hearing Chen Chen sound so anxious and it quickly explained, “Sorry, during the quarrel, the two bodyguards were sent away by your father. He said that the bodyguards shouldn’t interfere with family matters. Your father has done this each time he quarreled with your second uncle...”

“That b*stard!”

Chen Chen’s face turned even darker and he did not know if he was cursing his second uncle or someone else. He quickly stopped a taxi and hurried to the hospital to which his mother was taken.

Half an hour later, when Chen Chen walked into the ward, he immediately saw his mother on the bed, still conscious, and several people around the bed.

“Mom, are you okay?”

Chen Chen breathed a sigh of relief when he saw that there was nothing wrong with his mother except for her pale complexion.

“I’m fine, just feeling a little weak.”

Chen Chen’s mother shook her head and said with gratification, “Little Chen, hadn’t you gone to Shangdu? Why did you come back?”

“Lil Wang informed me.”

Chen Chen glanced coldly at his second uncle and his wife who were standing aside.

“It’s a misunderstanding, just a misunderstanding!”

Chen Chen’s father hastened to explain, “Just now, your second uncle and aunt visited us. Out of the blue, your mom suddenly fainted. Thank goodness it was nothing. Little Chen, just take care of your own business. Your second uncle and aunt are here with me to take care of...”

“Take care?”

Chen Chen glared steadily at them. “Pushing someone over is taking care of them?”

“Your mother pushed me too.”

His second aunt suddenly screeched, “She also pushed me then. We both collided. Who would know that your mother would be this feeble? Why am I the one to blame?”

“Okay, okay. Let’s not talk about it.” Chen Chen’s father tried to patch things over.

“Dad, you’re still on their side?”

Chen Chen took a deep breath and pointed at the two of them. “Both of you get out right now. If you don’t get out of here within ten seconds, I won’t...”

“Slap!”

However, before he could finish, Chen Chen felt a stinging pain on his cheek. It was his father who had slapped him.

“Watch how you talk to your aunt!”

Chen Chen’s father scolded angrily.

Chen Chen was taken aback for a moment, then his expression turned completely gloomy. He sat down on the empty hospital bed next to him, wordlessly cracking his neck.

The entire place fell silent for a while.

When Chen Chen’s father noticed Chen Chen’s behavior, he felt a pang of remorse for some reason. He turned and said to the other two, “As for the money, I’ll think about it. You may leave now!”

The aunt gave a cold snort at this and walked out of the ward with Chen Chen’s second uncle.

When the two had left, Chen Chen’s father sighed and sat beside Chen Chen, advising him earnestly, “Little Chen, although you’ve made it big outside, those two are still your second uncle and aunt. No matter what, you must maintain the most basic respect for your elders, got it?”

“That’s about to change...”

When Chen Chen heard this, an inexplicable smile tugged at the corner of his mouth as he mumbled.

“What did you say?”

Chen Chen’s father did not quite catch his words.

“Nothing.”

Chen Chen cracked his neck again, stood up, and exited the ward.

“Little Chen, Little Chen! This kid...”

No matter how his father yelled behind him, Chen Chen paid him no mind.

chapters.title: Chapter 263: Car Accident
chapters.body:
Chapter 263: Car Accident

Chapter 263: Car Accident

“Crash!”

Chen Chen shoved the glass door of the hospital building with one hand. At once, the slab of glass was swung open by an immense force to its maximum angle. Then, being unable to withstand that force, it erupted like blood from a wound!

“Ahh!”

Everyone in the hospital’s lobby could not help exclaiming in shock.

Even so, Chen Chen, the perpetrator of all this, did not pay them any attention. He strode out of the hospital and disappeared in the crowd...

At the same moment that Chen Chen left, a white car was driving at a steady pace on the ring road in Handu.

“How unfortunate!”

Inside the vehicle, a middle-aged woman with heavy makeup kept yelling and cursing, “He’s just gotten unbelievably lucky in his business and made some money. How dare he act so arrogantly!

“How infuriating, he’s become this snobbish after earning money. Nothing good ever comes out of that household!”

As she spoke, the middle-aged woman turned to look at the middle-aged man in the driver’s seat and kept questioning, “By the way, didn’t you often say that your brother was very attached to you when you were kids? That he’ll always listen to you? So why can’t you even get a little money from him? Are you still a man?”

“Alright, alright, just shut up about it!”

The middle-aged man in the driver’s seat looked like he had a headache. “We can just go to them and ask for money again, right? We’ll just keep on bugging them — won’t they eventually give in?”

“Why should I shut up? Why should I suffer in silence?”

However, the middle-aged woman yelled even louder, “Why should I shut up, why? Why should I be insulted like that? I’m getting more furious as I think about it. That little sh*thead dared to talk to us like that? How badly brought up he is! Only an uncultured family like you Chens can bring up such an uncultured child!

“They’re loaded. I’ve even heard that the company started by that f*cking kid earns more than 100 million a year. He only gives us a few hundred thousand at a time — are we just beggars that he’s chasing away?!”

Throughout the entire journey, the middle-aged woman kept deriding Chen Chen. She was Chen Chen’s second aunt by marriage and the driver was naturally Chen Chen’s second uncle.

“Alright, alright, stop talking about it. Can you let my ears rest a little?”

Chen Chen’s second uncle said helplessly.

“Oh, now you’re putting on an act?”

The second aunt suddenly sneered. “Where did the old you go? Back when you tricked me into bed, you weren’t like this. I’d thought that you’d make something of yourself. And now look, a little sh*thead has more money than you. You’ve lived your whole life as a pitiful mongrel!”

“I said, shut...”

“Boom!”

Chen Chen’s second uncle started shouting in response but before he could even finish the phrase “shut up”, they were struck by an extreme impact and the entire car flipped and spun in the air like a sheet of paper!

At this moment, the couple only felt that the world was spinning around. Their minds were blank and also their bodies seemed to have fallen apart as they were shaken violently!

The entire car kept rolling over the ground, one tumble after another, and soon lost its original shape. It had been reduced to a hunk of deformed copper and iron.

They did not know how much time had passed. It felt like a few seconds and it felt like an eternity. Chen Chen’s second aunt suddenly recovered her senses, only to find that she was bound tightly to her seat. She was also upside-down — evidently, the car was overturned...

“Hey, are you okay...”

She looked reflexively at her husband and saw that he had been reduced to a bloody pulp. Most of his head had been torn off by something harsh and blunt. A solitary tongue lolled out, exposed to the air...

“Agh ah ah ahh arghh!!!”

She unleashed an ear splitting scream.

Nonetheless, this scream was drowned out by the roar of an engine.

Hearing the rumbling of the approaching engine, the second aunt turned her head subconsciously and looked around, only to see a heavy truck with a dented front barreling toward her...

After that, she lost all consciousness.

...

“The T-Knights have completed the mission.”

Little X’s notification came from the Bluetooth headset. “The target has been destroyed and the survival rate is 0%.”

At this moment, Chen Chen was sitting on a bench in the hospital garden, staring at the phone in his hand.

A video was playing on the screen of the mobile phone, which seemed to be taken from the perspective of a traffic surveillance camera.

In the upper right corner of the video, a white car was approaching from a distance.

Nonetheless, before it could drive any further, a heavy truck suddenly emerged from behind, crashing into the back of the white car at a speed of more than 100 kilometers per hour!

The white car was suddenly like a loose sheet of paper that was carried off into the wind!

The white car was sent flying over a dozen meters, changed its shape in the constant tumbling, and finally fell completely upside-down in the middle of the road.

From this perspective, Chen Chen could even see someone in the vehicle struggling slowly as if they had made it out alive.

However, just then, the truck that had stopped suddenly reversed for a while, before it accelerated forward again and hit the white car ruthlessly!

“Bang!”

This time, the white car, riddled with holes, finally could not stand it anymore and exploded into a ball of flames.

Those inside were dead beyond dead.

After watching the video on the phone, Chen Chen finally looked happier. Following that, he turned on the dialer and pressed a three-digit number.

When the call was connected, Chen Chen said:

“Hello, is this the public security bureau? I met two suspicious people this morning, whom I suspect are foreign spies. They also threatened me, saying that they would do something to my family. I was afraid that my family would be harmed, so I prepared to call the police...”

After reporting to the police, Chen Chen stood up and prepared to go to the public security bureau to give his statement. Still, as he got up, he seemed to have thought of something. “X, ask Lil Wang to tell my parents that I’ll be taking them to Namibia for a while.”

“Are you going to use the Medpod 3000 on your parents?” Little X instantly understood.

“Yes. My mother’s body isn’t in an ideal shape. It must be caused by too much fatigue in her early years.”

Chen Chen nodded and looked up at the sky. “I know they don’t want to leave Mainland China or even their hometown to live in an unfamiliar country. I don’t insist on that either. I only want to give them the strictest protection and the best medical care.”

“But if you let your parents use the Medpod 3000, wouldn’t the secret be revealed?”

Little X asked curiously.

“Of course, I won’t let the secret be revealed.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “It’s not a big deal to make an exception this time and use the memory eraser on my parents. I’m not as pedantic as my father. That which they should know, they’ll know sooner or later. That which they shouldn’t know... I’ll never let them know.”

After all this, Chen Chen put away his phone, turned around, and left the hospital.

chapters.title: Chapter 264: Statement
chapters.body:
Chapter 264: Statement

Chapter 264: Statement

“You’re saying that there were two suspicious-looking people, who may be spies, trying to contact you?”

“Yes. They said they were from Huaguang Credit Company and wanted to talk to me. After I declined, they used my relatives to threaten me...”

In the office of the officer on duty at the police station, Chen Chen was sitting on one side of the table with a cup of tea in front of him while the police officer was sitting on the other side.

At this time, upon hearing Chen Chen’s remarks, the policeman could not help displaying a look of confusion. “Uh... Sorry, I haven’t asked who you are...”

“I’m the chairman of Blacklight Biotechnology. My name is Chen Chen.”

“Blacklight Biotechnology?”

The police officer paused. “This name is so familiar...”

“It’s Blacklight Biotechnology from Shangdu.”

“So it’s you! It’s an honor, a true honor...”

The police officer was startled, quickly stood up, and shook hands with Chen Chen. After that, he did not doubt Chen Chen any longer and began to record Chen Chen’s statement.

However, after a short while, the officer recording Chen Chen’s statement was suddenly summoned by another police officer, who seemed rather furtive.

About five minutes later, the police officer who was taking Chen Chen’s statement returned to the office with a hint of anguish in his expression. “Mr. Chen, we have verified something just now, something very unfortunate. Please don’t be frightened when I tell you.”

Chen Chen suddenly turned serious when he heard these words. “I’m a philanthropist. I will not be afraid. Please tell me.”

“Here’s what happened.”

The police sat back down across the desk and said, “We’ve just received a report. There was a car accident on the southern section of Bibo Road. A small white Zhaka car was hit by a heavy truck in the rear, causing the car to roll over. There was also a second collision. We’ve suspected this is a malice murder case and we were investigating the deceased. However, according to the license plate information, the owner of this car is suspected to be your relative, Chen Xiang.”

“What!”

Chen Chen widened his eyes when he heard the words and stood up abruptly. “That’s my second uncle! Oh no, are my parents in danger?”

“Mr. Chen, calm down!”

The police officer quickly soothed him. “Please calm down. We’ll investigate this matter carefully. I wonder if you have the contact information of Chen Xiang’s immediate relatives? We need someone to confirm the identity of the deceased.”

“Yes, my second uncle has a son and a daughter. My parents should have their numbers. I’ll ask my parents now...”

The police officer nodded.

After a few hours of making his statement, Chen Chen left the police station and returned to the hospital.

As soon as Chen Chen returned to the ward, Chen Chen’s father asked anxiously, “What’s going on? Little Chen, what happened to your second uncle and aunt?”

“It’s just an accident, nothing serious. Perhaps the police are even mistaken?”

Chen Chen frowned. “Just take care of Mom now. We’ll wait for an update from the police.”

“That...”

Chen Chen’s father only felt flustered. “Could it be that the police made a mistake and it was another car that had an accident?”

“That must be it.”

Chen Chen nodded and comforted his father. “Dad, don’t worry. God looks out for those who are good. They must be fine.”

“But... But why can’t I reach them on the phone?”

Chen Chen’s father did not relax after Chen Chen’s words. Instead, he became even more frantic as he paced around the room.

“Maybe both of them turned off their phones. After all, there’s so much pressure in modern society. It’s common to turn off your phone when there’s nothing to do to find your peace of mind.”

Chen Chen slowly walked to his mother’s bed and peeled an orange for her. “Mom, are you feeling better now?”

“I’m completely fine. Just take care of your dad now. Look at how he’s panicking...” Chen Chen’s mother could not stand it.

“What’s the big deal?”

Chen Chen’s lips twitched and he suddenly beamed. “Oh right, didn’t Second Aunt want to borrow a million from us for business? I’ll agree to that.”

“Ah, Little Chen, it’s not easy for you to make money either. You’d really agree?”

His mother asked worriedly.

“Sure, why wouldn’t I agree?”

Chen Chen chuckled. “Mom, I’m not that stingy. Plus, this money can be left to my cousin, Chen Shan. With this sum, she won’t have to worry about her future anymore.”

“My child, how can you say that...”

Chen Chen’s mother immediately understood what Chen Chen was implying and could not help but chastise him.

Chen Shan was the youngest daughter of Chen Chen’s second uncle. She was the same age as Chen Chen but was a little younger. When they were kids, she had also played with Chen Chen and she had not inherited her mother’s shrewish temper.

Then, as they grew up, the relationship between the two turned distant due to years of separation. Now Chen Chen had not seen his cousin for about four or five years.

Chen Chen’s second uncle also had an eldest son, who was 28 years old this year. Still, Chen Chen was not close to him, so naturally, he would not have any share of the money.

At this point, Chen Chen looked at his father, who was still fretting endlessly and shook his head.

Chen Chen’s loving, righteous father surely did not know that his second brother and sister-in-law had died long ago. Not only that, but they were also cremated on the spot. Their ashes were probably already scattered by the wind.

...

A few days later, Chen Chen’s father still had to come to terms with this cruel fact.

After attending the funeral, Chen Chen once again persuaded his parents to go to Namibia for a holiday.

Chen Chen’s parents hesitated for a while before they agreed.

After all, it was a city built by their own son. Now that they were invited, as parents, they had every reason to visit the place where their son was making a living. Anyway, they were not going to stay there permanently.

Thus, Chen Chen rested at home for a month, then returned to Namibia again and settled down with his parents.

To allow his parents to use the Medpod 3000, Chen Chen even deliberately disguised the Medpod 3000 as a CT machine and repaired his parents’ bodies under the pretext of a physical examination.

The restoration provided by the Medpod 3000 was much more advanced than any health-preservation technique owned by Chen Chen. In only one day, Chen Chen’s parents transformed from their 50s to their 30s at a speed that was almost visible to the naked eye.

Even so, on second thought, Chen Chen still used a memory eraser to erase a short period of memory in his parents, making them think that they had undergone the “Age-reversal Treatment Project”.

Nonetheless, Chen Chen’s parents were still shocked when they saw their younger faces. Chen Chen said that, if they wanted, he could perform this kind of surgery on his parents’ relatives for free, including Chen Chen’s paternal and maternal grandmothers.

Of course, Chen Chen’s parents were one hundred percent in favor of this.

After fulfilling his wish to treat his parents, Chen Chen finally felt relieved.

With the Medpod 3000’s restoration, Chen Chen’s parents’ bodies had reached their best condition, which was equivalent to extending the lifespan of an ordinary person by 20 years.

Of course, the effects of repairing the body once or twice with the Medpod 3000 were not very obvious. What was truly formidable about this machine was that it could perpetually preserve the human body in an optimal state of health.

Therefore, repairing the body once a month or even once a week, was the best form of maintenance. After all, as long as the body was kept in its best condition for a long time, one’s lifespan would naturally become longer.

For example, the wealthy in Elysium could easily live past 250 years.

chapters.title: Chapter 265: Sirolimus
chapters.body:
Chapter 265: Sirolimus

Chapter 265: Sirolimus

Over in Namibia, various orders for laser components required by Chen Chen had already been sent out but it would take a long time to receive the said orders for now. After all, Chen Chen needed high-precision laser lenses and other such components.

Each of these components was extremely valuable, so their production time was naturally extremely lengthy.

Chen Chen assigned bodyguards to accompany his parents in their travels around Namibia and he once again went to the Spire Experimental Base.

In the month that Chen Chen was absent, Little X had performed an injection experiment with the modified T-virus.

However, unfortunately, in that experiment, Chen Chen’s clone could not withstand this modified T-virus at all and directly became a zombie.

Presently, Chen Chen was looking into the isolated room. A fully rotting, aimlessly wandering zombie that looked almost identical to Chen Chen was in there. Chen Chen felt like crying and laughing at the same time.

“Sir Godfather, after this zombie was fused with the T-virus, we’ve checked his DNA and found three errors in total.”

Little X’s voice rang out, “Now that these three errors have been corrected and these corrections have been implemented in a virus numbered T-002, should I conduct a second experiment?”

“Go ahead.”

Chen Chen nodded. T-002 was the name Little X gave to this modified version of T-virus. The original full name of the T-virus was Tyrant-Virus. Chen Chen’s first genetically modified version of the T-virus was thus named T-001 accordingly.

T-001 was later reported to have failed. After improvement, it became the current T-002.

Just then, Chen Chen suddenly asked again, “By the way, how many of my clones are left?”

“Do you mean the clones that haven’t been injected with T-virus?”

Little X said, “Back then, you made a total of five cloned fertilized eggs that were fused with T-virus and five cloned fertilized eggs that were not. Now, there are four clones left that have been fused with T-virus and three normal clones that haven’t been injected with T-virus.”

“Three?”

Chen Chen pondered. “Make ten more normal clones. After all, the incubation of normal clones takes half a year. Incubating them in advance will prevent a future shortage of clones.”

“Understood.”

After Little X agreed, several Black Knight assistants immediately stepped forward, opened a life incubator, and took out a clone of Chen Chen.

After strapping this clone to a stretcher and connecting various life-sustaining equipment, a Black Knight took out the modified T-virus that had already been prepared and injected it directly into the clone.
