Since he managed to acquire what he needed relatively easy on this attempt, Chen Chen decided to stop exploring for now. Just as he was about to unplug the USB drive, his hand suddenly paused mid-motion.

“Black goo...”

Chen Chen muttered to himself, “The functionalities of the black goo were clearly defined in the movies Prometheus and Alien: Covenant. It’s a form of bioenergy in which a small amount of infection may cause genetic restructuring while infection in large doses may dehydrate and carbonize the body. Upon coming in contact with water after the carbonization, new DNA will be formed, thus giving birth to a new life form.

“The reason the Engineers race developed the black goo was to solve their struggles with continued reproduction.”

Based on the movie narration, the Engineers race had an extremely long lifespan up to 330,000 years. However, their lifespan proved to be detrimental to them as somewhere along the lines, the Engineers race had lost the ability to reproduce.

So, they began searching for their “God”, which was the alien-like life form shown in the murals in Prometheus. In truth, they were not aliens. Among the Engineers race, the alien-like God was referred to as “Deacon”. By drinking the blood of Deacon, the Engineers may find hope for the continued reproduction of their race.

There was a good reason behind the Engineers worshipping Deacon and even believing that the Deacon’s blood may restore their ability to reproduce.

In the extended cut of the movie, it was very subtly hinted that Deacon was the being that created the Engineers race. Later on, to restore their ability to reproduce, the Engineers began drinking the blood of Deacon. This was shown in the opening sequence of the movie.

In a ceremony resembling a sacrificial ritual, an Engineer having consumed the blood of Deacon was put to death, which the genetic of the dead was restructured to give birth to a new life form – humans.

The black goo shown in the later stages of the movie was merely artificial Deacon blood. After all, natural Deacon blood was scarce. Despite being an artificial product, it was still capable of restructuring genetics, its potential was unlimited...

The idea suddenly occurred to Chen Chen like a brilliant spark. Chen Chen narrowed his eyes suspiciously.

“Can it create new life?

“The only question is, will I be able to do it better than David did in the movie?”

Chen Chen murmured as the corner of his lips turned up into a faint smirk. He suddenly opened the USB drive again, slowly reaching into the display with his left hand...

chapters.title: Chapter 273: The True Nature Of Black Goo
chapters.body:
Chapter 273: The True Nature Of Black Goo

Chapter 273: The True Nature of Black Goo

Shortly after, a dark cylindrical jar 40 centimeters tall and 15 centimeters thick appeared before Chen Chen.

Chen Chen took several paces back when he saw this all-too-familiar object, he ordered, “X, send this item to the Spire Experimental Base, put it in the highest grade biosafety laboratory.”

“Understood, what do you plan to do with this object you instantiated?”

Little X asked curiously, “Based on what was seen in the movie, black goo seemed to only be able to create life forms like aliens.”

“Nothing major, it’s just that for someone dealing with biology research, a substance like black goo raises one’s interest.”

Shaking his head, Chen Chen expressed with a mild smile. “You won’t be able to understand such human emotions for now. Also, the primary usage of the black goo is certainly for the creation of aliens, however, this isn’t the only thing it’s capable of.”

“You mean to say ...” Little X asked with a mix of emotions.

“Based on what was seen in the movie, when the black goo infects its subject from the inside, it causes the cells to break down, which are then substituted with newly-formed DNA. In contrast, when it infects its subjects from the outside, it will begin to mutate rapidly and aggressively.”

Chen Chen continued his explanation. “Later on in Alien: Covenant, David explained the usage of black goo again. The nature of the black goo is like an invasive AI, it disrupts substances and provokes unique reactions in each cell. From it, it reconstructs a new life form, its potential is immeasurable...

“Do you understand now? The terror of the black goo doesn’t lie in its ability to create aliens, but in its ability to create a new life form. If a new advanced life form able to reproduce and evolve independently, this is a wonder unable to be replicated by any current technology.”

It was only after Chen Chen finished when he unplugged the USB drive and disinfected himself thoroughly before exiting the isolation room.

An hour later, when Chen Chen returned the USB drive to the control center and returned to the Spire Experimental Base, the Black Knights had already taken the jar of black goo to a level-4 biosafety laboratory.

After going through the processes of disinfection again, Chen Chen put on the positive pressure protective suit properly. He then proceeded to step into the highest-grade biosafety laboratory that had been sealed till now.

Based on international standards, biosafety laboratories were divided into four grades. Level-one laboratory had a defined specialization and was proven not to cause disease such as measles virus and mumps virus.

A level-two laboratory researched microbial substances related to some common human diseases such as influenza virus, with a moderate degree of risk.

The three-level biosafety level laboratory researched high-risk microbial substances such as Yersinia pestis, rabies virus, and HIV. Biosafety laboratories of this level should already be equipped with advanced protective facilities.

The highest grading was the level-four biosafety laboratory. This class specializes in extremely dangerous microorganisms such as Ebola. Even the slightest drop of water or puff of air produced from the laboratory had to be decontaminated thoroughly before allowed to be disposed of outside the laboratory.

Meanwhile, Chen Chen had trumped the list with an even higher, level-five biosafety laboratory. Protective features, rate of contamination, all of the related features of this laboratory were several times higher than that of a level-four biosafety lab. Raising aliens in this place should not be worrisome.

The reason that the level-five biosafety laboratory was never officially put into operation was that no substance had ever passed the need for a biosafety laboratory above level-four. Even the BE-006 Blacklight virus that came close to wiping out the entirety of Africa was not subjected to a level-five biosafety laboratory.

Today marked the day for the very first time the level-five biosafety laboratory that had been vacant all this time was put into operation. There was no ceremony whatsoever. Not a single staff member of the Spire Experimental Base was informed of this, just like how it was with the T-virus which Chen Chen was modifying in the secret laboratory.

Entering the laboratory, Chen Chen first did a visual sweep of the environment. The level-five biosafety laboratory was not located on the top floor of the Spire, but on the unknown-to-public bottom floor.

Chen Chen had an isolated space excavated deep below the Spire Experimental Base that could sever the connection with the Spire above at any time. Covering an area of 5,000 square meters, there were a total of 13 laboratories all with different functions, and two sets of living rooms with four rooms and a hall. The implemented security measures were dialed up to maximum.

At the present, only four Black Knights tasked with the research operation entered with Chen Chen. Each one of them carried an identical disinfected cage made for mice. There were four groups of mice in total that would soon become the first subject for the black goo.

There was no need for Chen Chen to micromanage as the Black Knights displayed discipline above that of professors and started the experiment following all standard protocols.

One of the Black Knights approached a biosafety cabinet. Beneath the thick glasses of the cabinet, the jar of black goo could be seen sitting idly.

The level-three biosafety cabinet was designed for usage in level-three and four biosafety laboratories. They were suitable for use in extremely high-risk biology research. The cabinet was completely enclosed. The only way for the researcher to interact with the substance was by using the pair of built-in gloves joined to the inside of the cabinet. This was the reason it was also named the glove box.

The black knights swiftly reached in with his hands padded with protective suit material and began fixing the jar of black goo inside the biosafety cabinet.

Based on an observation of David in the movie, it could be seen that the jar was easily opened. The Black Knight only barely twisted the lid before the jar opened.

“The jar seems to be made of silica, hard and brittle, but not dangerous.”

Little X provided a live narration of the angle of observation from the Black Knights’ eyes.

Chen Chen remained silent and stepped forward. He immediately met a jelly-like substance inside the jar. There were four popsicles in the clump of jelly.

“This jelly-like substance should be a buffer, helps buffer and shockproof its contents.”

Following Little X’s mention, the Black Knight promptly removed the four popsicles. The popsicles miraculously popped out immediately, their surface completely unstained.

“The buffer solution appears to consist of nanomaterial, they are very silky and don’t easily attach to the surface of objects.” Little X made further observations.

Chen Chen had a good look at the popsicle-like objects as well. It was referred to as popsicles because the shape of the object closely resembled a popsicle, with split sections in between. The popsicles took on a semi-transparent form with mild green liquid inside.

The only difference was that the outer shell of the popsicles was made of glass material. Apart from a large portion of its contents being the green liquid, there was a small residue of black substance at its bottom.

When the Black Knight gave it a gentle shake, the black substance immediately fused with the green liquid, transforming the popsicle into pure black color.

This was the true nature of the black goo.

chapters.title: Chapter 274: Prion?
chapters.body:
Chapter 274: Prion?

Chapter 274: Prion?

When Chen Chen saw this, his eyes grew solemn. In the movie, David had only added one small drop into a drink to infect a human adult, who had transformed into a humanoid monster within ten hours.

It was evident that the black goo was not a bacteria or a virus, but a very stable polymer compound. Otherwise, it would not be able to withstand the damaging effects of alcohol so easily.

At this time, the Black Knight was copying David’s actions in the movie. He broke off the tip of the popsicle, then inserted a syringe from one side and extracted a very small amount of liquid black goo from the popsicle.

Some of the Black Knights immediately placed a part of the black goo under the microscope for analysis and observation while others injected the fluid into a white mouse that had been prepared in advance.

Soon, Chen Chen saw that the mouse injected with black goo had begun to develop fever and convulsions.

In no time, this little white mouse started to shed its fur everywhere and the pink skin under the fur was exposed to the air. However, at this moment, the other white mice seemed to perceive this abnormality and they all surrounded the sick little white mouse.

Nonetheless, this heart-warming scene was immediately subverted. The sick mouse suddenly leaped up, lunged ferociously at one of the mice, and began tearing it apart with its teeth!

At once, the white mouse that was bitten was bleeding all over!

“After being infected with the black goo, one will lose all sense of reason and become very aggressive.”

Chen Chen observed silently. Quickly, the infected mouse easily killed the other mice in the cage. Chen Chen continued to note down. “After being infected with black goo, the host’s strength will suddenly increase and it can easily kill others of the same species, as was shown in the movie...”

After recording all these changes, Chen Chen noted that the body of the mouse in the cage was turning from bright red to grayish-white. At the same time, it began to consume the flesh and blood of its own kind, seemingly to accumulate energy for the next mutation.

Soon, this little white mouse’s body gradually grew a layer of stratum corneum, which began to blacken. Meanwhile, beneath the blackness, there were glimpses of crimson flesh.

In the end, a fine and dense layer of needle-like spikes came out of its body, replacing the original white fur. What was even stranger was that these spikes were still growing in and out endlessly. It was a chilling sight...

“This is a new species.”

Chen Chen was astounded. He summoned a Black Knight, who then stepped forward, put on thick protective gloves, and extended the needle into the cage. As the Black Knight gently held the mutated mouse, he extracted a blood sample.

After it was caught, the white mouse began to struggle violently while unleashing a strange piercing scream.

This sound was beyond the normal vocal range of a typical white mouse. It sounded harsh and savage. This mouse had undergone essential changes in both its genes and body structure.

Nonetheless, although this little white mouse had mutated so terrifyingly, the difference in size still existed. No matter how hard it struggled, it could not break free of the Black Knight’s grasp.

Still, before the blood of the mutated mice had been fully analyzed, the analysis of the black goo was completed.

When the composition of the black goo was determined, Chen Chen gasped in shock.

“Are you sure there’s no mistake?”

As Chen Chen stared at the test report in his hand, he displayed a befuddled look. “You mean, this black goo is a protein composed of more than 20 amino acids?”

“Yes, it’s a protein molecule that can infect a variety of carbon-based life.”

Little X replied, “If the facts weren’t right before our eyes, even I wouldn’t believe that there’s such a strange protein molecule in the world.”

“In other words, is this something similar to a prion?”

Chen Chen inhaled sharply. “Then how does it infect people? Is it the same as prions?”

“Yes, based on the blood test of the mice, we’ve gotten a rough answer.”

Little X said again, “But unlike other prions, this protein doesn’t sediment in the brain and damage the brain of organisms. Rather, it directly alters the human genetic chain.”

After hearing these words, Chen Chen understood it completely.

A prion was not a virus because in international textbooks, viruses were categorized as organisms. However, prions were not considered organisms but a system composed only of proteins, with traits of self-replication and infectivity.

Still, prions were filterable, infectious, and pathogenic, like conventional viruses, but they were much smaller than the smallest known conventional virus. The structure of their virus particles thus could not be observed under the electron microscope and they would not present an immune response.

As Chen Chen continued reading, he instantly understood why the black goo was a type of prion.

This was because when the prion protein of the black goo entered the host’s body, the normal PrP protein of the human body would be easily assimilated by the prion protein of the black goo. The proteins with normal configuration would take on the molecular structure of black goo, which increased exponentially and expanded rapidly.

Even so, if that was all, the black goo would at most be a new type of prion that was rapid and infective. However, what was more surprising was that the prion protein of black goo not only had the ability to assimilate proteins in the host’s body, but also the ability to disassemble nucleic acids in the host!

When the black goo’s prion protein encountered the host’s nucleic acid chain, it seemed to trigger a certain program to begin disassembly of the host’s DNA nucleic acid chain and recombine it into a new form.

After the nucleic acid chain was recombined, the black goo’s prion protein would also envelop this new nucleic acid chain to form a new virus structure.

At this point, the prions in the black goo had successfully evolved into a complete virus by plundering the DNA of organisms. This virus would work with the black goo to carry out a second transformation of the host’s body.

All of this had happened with that little white mouse’s body...

After reading the report in his hand, Chen Chen looked even more troubled. “It’s no wonder that the black goo can be extracted through the USB flash drive. I didn’t expect it to be a prion-like substance. But based on this report, I think that the prion protein of black goo is more like an artificial protein robot...”

“I thought so too.”

Little X echoed, “The black goo is hundreds and thousands of times more amazing than prions. It cannot be defined as a prion at all.

“After all, it’s the supreme creation of the Engineer civilization.”

Chen Chen nodded and said with emotion, “Still, at this point, I finally understand the precious value of the black goo. Compared to the black goo, the T-virus is like a cheap toy. Most organisms infected by the T-virus suffer from unstable disadvantages such as zombies. Only products that are infected at the stage of being fertilized eggs can become healthy mutant creatures.

“However, the black goo is different. It can be used directly on adult life forms. Any organism can directly mutate into a new species after coming into contact with it. What’s more remarkable is that this new species has the ability to reproduce. It’s completely possible to form a new population independently, unlike the T-virus that can only mutate on an individual basis without any way to continue.”

“Doesn’t that mean that the black goo can accelerate the evolution of life?”

Little X asked.

“No, there’s still a disparity between this and evolution.”

Here, Chen Chen paused and shook his head. “What is evolution? Only the transformation through constant adaptation to the environment can be called evolution. This isn’t the case at all with black goo. From the fate of the little white mouse, we know that the changes caused by this black goo are better understood as uncontrollable mutations.

“Moreover, the direction of this mutation is uncertain. It may be in a good direction or a bad one. Even humans are likely to lose the characteristics and intellect of high-level creatures after infection, just like in the movie...”

“Ah, if the black goo is so dangerous, then why do you still want to examine it?” Little X suddenly turned puzzled.

“Although the black goo is dangerous, there are two sides to everything.”

Chen Chen smiled. “After all, it’s a biological weapon that is dozens or even hundreds of times stronger than the T-virus. It’s useful. Besides... The Engineer race could live for more than 300,000 years and humans were created from the combination of Engineers and black goo. In that case, is it possible that we can uncover the secret of the Engineers’ longevity from the black goo?”

chapters.title: Chapter 275: New Way Of Thinking
chapters.body:
Chapter 275: New Way Of Thinking

Chapter 275: New Way of Thinking

The reason Chen Chen had extracted the black goo was ultimately to use it as a longevity technology.

Why was Chen Chen obsessed with longevity? In other words, why did human beings pursue longevity?

It was because this was an inevitable phenomenon.

As science and technology became more advanced, mankind was bound to be unwilling to live for only a brief one hundred years. Compared to the span of time measured out in hundreds of millions of years, a hundred years was like the twinkling of an eye, almost meaningless.

Furthermore, with the development of science and technology, the accumulation of knowledge became increasingly difficult. In ancient times, people could inherit the wealth of knowledge accumulated by civilization in just ten years of diligent study.

However, in modern times, a nine-year compulsory education could only build a mere foundation. Human knowledge had been divided into hundreds of branches. One person could only obtain achievements in a few branches in one lifetime.

The accumulation of civilization’s sum knowledge would only grow more abundant. Did it mean that in the future, a person would have to undergo compulsory education for decades?

By the time someone had painstakingly acquired the knowledge of a field, would they be near their deathbed then and be cremated and buried in just a few years?

Clearly, no one wanted this. If human civilization were to move forward, the need for longevity would become more urgent. This was a general trend that would not be shifted by anyone’s will.

According to what Chen Chen knew, there were probably three options for human beings to attain immortality.

Number one: keep their bodies and undergo genetic evolution.

It was true that the conventional means of human evolution employed reproduction as a way to make different groups evolve simultaneously. However, this did not mean that humans could not choose more efficient ways of evolution such as genes.

Humans could use genetic technology to strengthen themselves, putting humanity ahead of the pack. Evolution would occur in advance, thereby extending life.

This was the most ideal path.

Why so? As the human body was a sophisticated and complicated machine, it was composed of organic matter, not steel.

Perhaps this body was extremely fragile that a bullet or a sharp knife could terminate human life, but human beings also had the ability to multiply and evolve. They were capable of thought, consciousness, creativity, and personal agency.

All this was made possible by that fragile body.

The human body was a jewel born through a process of evolution that took millions of years, the most magnificent miracle of nature.

As long as human beings had complete bodies, there were unlimited possibilities.

Number two: Flesh was out, machines were in.

Although the potential of this path was not as great as the first option, it was a path that more readily promised longevity and even something close to immortality.

For example, the bionic technology that Chen Chen was zealously developing — replacing old organs was like replacing the parts of a machine. In this way, life could last longer.

If one were to be even more radical, one could also choose to keep the brain only and replace the entire body with machinery, so that the lifespan of human beings would be equal to the lifespan of the brain.

The lifespan of the brain alone was one hundred and fifty years. If this was combined with some special technology, the lifespan of the brain might be doubled a few times.

Nevertheless, although this method would easily allow people to live longer and further ensure the security of existence, it lacked part of the potential that all life should possess. Thus, unless civilization was at its end, Chen Chen would not regard this path as the main one.

The third and last path was the method of soul sublimation.

The rough idea of this path was: abandon the body and exist in the world in the form of a soul. Human beings would develop into a ubiquitous, yet incorporeal civilization.

It was just that modern science had not yet confirmed the existence of the soul, so this path was just an idea for now.

Chen Chen still remembered what Professor Wang Xi said: “We humans are still completely ignorant in the face of the boundless ocean of truth. Perhaps a distant, impossible dream like immortality is only the first step in the search for truth...”

It was this statement that sparked Chen Chen’s desire for longevity.

Chen Chen came back to the present. Now, he was staring at a humanoid monster covered in black armor in the isolation room, whose head was like a winter melon. Chen Chen’s expression was a probing one.

This monster had still been an ordinary human a day ago.

His name was Patton. He was once a murderer sentenced to life imprisonment. He was later purchased by Chen Chen and kept in the Delta-level experimental dormitory of the Spire Experimental Base.

Still, this Delta experiment subject started his journey mutation after being injected with five milliliters of black goo yesterday.

The experimental subject first showed pathological signs of flushing and fever, then hemorrhages all over the body, confusion, and finally a descent into a state of shock.

Within the body, the proteins of the experimental subject were rapidly converted into the prion protein of the black goo. At the same time, these prion proteins began to decompose the DNA of the experimental subject and combined with the DNA fragments, forming a retrovirus that only affected the experimental body itself.

After that, because of how the retrovirus functioned, the subject started to undergo violent physical mutations. During the mutation process, the subject developed an intense sense of hunger and any organic matter and protein were devoured as sustenance.

By this morning, the subject drifted between sleep and consciousness, then finally lost all rational thought and became an ugly monster with low intelligence and no humanity.

“The black goo promotes the forceful and rapid division of biological cells, greatly increases the mutation rate, produces all kinds of abnormal cells, and eventually produces a new organism...”

While he described the functions of the black goo, Chen Chen looked at the monster in the isolation room and sighed. “However, when the genes of the organism itself are altered in an instant, the combination of individual proteins, enzymes, and the DNA itself would cause tens of thousands of unpredictable results. Therefore, it’s no surprise no matter what the experimental subject becomes, but... This appearance is really ugly...

“However, after being infected by the black goo, the test subject exhibits extremely high energy metabolism efficiency and cell division rate, both unprecedented in the human body.”

Little X added. “Before the black goo was injected, the subject weighed 55 kg. After the black goo was injected, the subject spent five hours consuming food equal to his own weight. Only half of this amount was converted into useless urine and excrement that were discharged from the body. All the rest has been converted into the bodyweight of the subject.”

Chen Chen looked at the report in his hand. Sure enough, the bodyweight after the mutation had now reached 80 kg, which was 25 kg more than before.

“This is probably why the alien grew so fast.”

As he observed the enlarged test subject, Chen Chen pondered aloud. “In the movies, the aliens can often grow, in a few hours, from a small size into a beast that weighs hundreds of kilograms. Although the movies rarely show the aliens eating, it now appears that the aliens still need to eat, with a greater eating capacity than humans.”

At this point, Chen Chen suddenly took two steps forward, walked to the window of the isolation room, and looked at the subject carefully.

As Chen Chen approached, the test subject in the isolation room suddenly burst out in a screech and immediately pounced toward Chen Chen!

“Bang!”

There was a loud impact as the test subject hit his head on the glass of the isolation room. A large amount of light yellow blood splattered on the glass.

“Intelligence is very low.”

Little X sounded rueful. “And the blood isn’t corrosive. Besides a head that’s somewhat similar to the alien’s, the experimental subject before us can only be regarded as an alternative zombie at best.”

“That’s to be expected. In the movie, the process of the aliens’ birth requires the participation of the Engineers.”

Chen Chen explained, “The alien exoskeleton has a mechanical aesthetic, which is derived from the exoskeleton structure worn by the Engineers. Later, in Alien: Covenant, there’s a spore plant infected by the black goo. After the spores infect the human body, a white alien form without a mechanical-style exoskeleton will be born, which illustrates the problem.

“Thus, the acidic blood of the alien may also come from the Engineers.”

With this in mind, Chen Chen snapped his fingers lightly. “Okay, let’s dissect this monster into sections. I need a diagram of his organs and a gene map...”

“Understood.”

...

This took a few more hours. When Chen Chen walked out of the laboratory, he heaved a long sigh.

To Chen Chen’s disappointment, when the subject was completely dissected and sliced, Chen Chen did not find anything unusual. The subject did undergo genetic recombination, but after the recombination, it was just a savage beast without intelligence.

Even its brain structure had become much smaller and its plasticity was not as good as human’s.

Still, Chen Chen knew that this was a normal phenomenon. Human beings could be regarded as the supreme product of the Earth, the only high-level life form. It was impossible for the black goo’s mutation to make humans even better than before.

The only thing that surprised Chen Chen was that the test subject was originally a male, but when it was dissected, an immature reproductive organ was discovered in the body.

It was a cyst structure that existed in the lower abdomen. After opening it, he found that there were dense piles of larvae that looked like tadpoles.

Those tadpole-like larvae were fully transparent. Their biological structure was simple, but they were alive. They were first conceived in the cyst but would be released later at a critical time.

Looking at the structure of this cyst, Little X could roughly determine that when these tadpole-like larvae matured, they would be ejected through the belly button of the test subject before they penetrated their target’s body as parasites.

This was how this new species reproduced — parasitic reproduction that was similar to the aliens, simple yet horrifying.

At present, it seemed that Chen Chen could forget about using the black goo to strengthen the human body. Drinking it would certainly be a fatal move. Its lethality was comparable to paraquat. However, if it was used to develop chemical weapons, that should be relatively easy.

Next, one of Chen Chen’s research directions would be to combine the black goo with spore plants to form the kind of spores that could parasitize the human body, as in the movie Alien: Covenant.

He wondered how David in the movie discovered this idea. After parasitizing the human body, the spores would not cause humans to mutate but instead form an embryo in the human body.

Following that, the embryo would grow silently, until it was able to break away from the human body. Then it would break out through the back or chest and grow into a white-skinned creature, like the alien.

Chen Chen had to admit that he still had a long way to go in terms of researching the black goo.

Even so, at present, it appeared that the reproduction method of the monsters produced by the black goo was parasitic reproduction. Even the perfect creature that was finally born — the Alien — was the same. This reproduction method reminded Chen Chen of a virus.

A virus called Golgotha or G-virus for short.

This virus was almost the same as the black goo. It could also mutate biological genes, resurrect the dead, and even give birth to a new species on the basis of infecting a life form.

It even earned itself a label: a “thoroughly evil substance” that went against God.

The crucial thing was that the G-virus was modified from the T-virus.

“I could try to combine the black goo with T-virus. Maybe there will be new discoveries...”

Chen Chen had a flash of inspiration and he started to muse in silence.

chapters.title: Chapter 276: G-Virus
chapters.body:
Chapter 276: G-Virus

Chapter 276: G-Virus

A few days later, when Chen Chen received the results, he went to the level-five biosafety laboratory again.

At this time, the Black Knight assistants were going through a complicated and tedious process, separating one conical-bottom centrifuge tube after another.

The centrifuge tubes were full of red liquid. This translucent liquid contained many fine and dense sediments.

This was the new substance formed by the combination of black goo and T-virus.

“Click!”

The screen behind Chen Chen turned on automatically, displaying a black and white image. At the same time, the voice of Little X rang out. “The process of combining the T-virus with black goo wasn’t stable as it was affected by the dosage and the culture medium. In general, it’d produce unique variations in three directions.

“Ultimately, we obtained 87 new types of viruses. These are also filoviruses. After destroying those viruses with duplicate functions, I finally selected the three most representative types of viruses.”

Chen Chen looked at the image when he heard this, only to see three pictures displayed side by side on the screen.

Each of the three pictures featured a special filamentous substance. As these were taken from an electron microscope, there was no color. One could only tell that the three filamentous substances were almost indistinguishable, just like three slender, hideous worms.

Nonetheless, these worms were less than one micron in length and less than a hundred nanometers thick. Their entire body was made of simple protein shells and RNA nucleic acids.

As he stared at these three special viruses, even Chen Chen felt a chill run down his spine. The T-virus itself had the power to threaten mankind in the first place. Now that it had been strengthened by the black goo, what sort of strength had it attained?

“After some experiments on animals, I’ve determined the properties of the three viruses.

“Of these three, the first virus has enhanced infectivity and can be transmitted through the air. Organisms that can be infected include almost all carbon-based life. After being infected, the same thing will happen as when one is infected with black goo. When the host dies, a new life will be born in the place of the host. Because there were too few experiments, it’s still uncertain whether there’s an immune group.”

Following Little X’s explanation, a mouse-like monster with a black shell and red eyes appeared on the screen. Ripples of red muscles could be seen in the cracks of the shell, which looked extremely sinister and terrifying.

“The second type isn’t highly infectious and cannot be transmitted through the air, but it strengthens the virus’ ability to mutate the host. After the host is infected, the degree of mutation is far more acute than the other two viruses.”

Immediately afterward, the picture changed again and Chen Chen was greeted with a palm-sized monster with seven or eight pairs of fleshy feet on its abdomen. This monster did not have a black outer shell. Instead, all over its body were large and small eyeballs, meaningless organs, and swarms of tentacles.

It looked just like a pile of monster corpses piled up and fused to form an indescribable thing.

“Besides these two, there’s the last type.”

Little X continued. “The third virus isn’t very capable of mutation nor is it extremely contagious, but it’s indeed the most incredible of the three viruses.”

Chen Chen’s eyes lit up upon hearing these words. “In what way is it incredible?”

“You’ll find out soon enough.”

Little X kept Chen Chen in suspense. Following that, the screen flashed and another peculiar picture appeared.

The screen showed two pictures spliced together. On the left side was an ordinary-looking white mouse with big, shining eyes, looking endearingly at the screen.

On the right, the mouse was still standing, but it had become a monster about the size of a rabbit, with a black outer shell and red muscles.

“This...”

Chen Chen raised his eyebrows and realized what was off at a glance. “The biggest change in this third mouse seems to be its body size?”

“That’s right. What’s surprising is that although this mouse infected with the third virus has also eaten a lot, it only consumed about 50 grams of sustenance. Yet it has grown from 20 grams to a monster weighing 300 grams.”

“You mean, its weight has increased by 260 grams out of thin air?”

Chen Chen looked up sharply as if he could not believe this.

“It’s definitely true!”

Little X quickly explained, “It’s true. I have all the video records here. Like the other experimental mice, it only ate twice its weight. The weight of the first two mice only increased from 20 grams to roughly 40 grams. Only this mouse practically broke the law of conservation of energy. Plus, all this happened within a few hours!”

“Then what’s the reason?”

Chen Chen asked, “What caused it to increase in size?”

“After dissection, I discovered a special organ in the body of this little white mouse.”

Little X replied, “This mouse’s skin has a special absorption mechanism that directly absorbs moisture and even microorganisms from the air to replenish itself. Not only that, I’ve noticed a lot of high-energy covalent bonds in the mouse’s blood. It seems that it’s able to directly react with the nitrogen in the air to create a shell. Its weight could increase out of thin air probably due to these factors.”

After hearing this, Chen Chen could not help looking flabbergasted. “Isn’t this too implausible?”

“Not at all.”

Little X explained, “Perhaps there are many factors we haven’t yet discovered, but what can be confirmed is that the growth rate of the third mouse does exceed the material energy it takes in. This is beyond doubt. I think this holds the secret key to why the alien could grow up rapidly.”

“Then let’s focus on the second and third viruses.”

Chen Chen nodded. “As for the infectiousness of the virus, I don’t think there’s a need for further research. Just seal off the first virus.”

“Understood. I’ll use the most stringent storage technology to seal the first virus.”

Little X agreed. “Even so, should you give these three viruses a name?”

In response, Chen Chen simply pulled up the gene sequence diagrams of the three viruses and thought aloud, “These three viruses can be regarded as different subspecies of the same type of virus. They have an inextricable link to the T-virus, similar to the G-virus in Resident Evil. In that case, let’s just call them G-viruses.”

“Then these three types would be G-001, G-002, and G-003?” Little X asked.

“Nothing wrong with that. I’ll leave that up to you.”

Chen Chen smiled, then turned and left. However, as he reached the door, he suddenly had a very preposterous thought...

G-virus, G-virus... Why was this new type of virus, synthesized from the black goo and T-virus, so similar to the G-virus in the game? No, one could even say that they were identical....

‘Why did I have to name it G-virus? What was going through my head?’

Chen Chen could not help but narrow his eyes.

“Sir Godfather?”

Seeing that Chen Chen had halted in his steps, Little X called out instinctively.

Chen Chen did not answer, but took another step forward and disappeared outside the door of the laborator

chapters.title: Chapter 277: Brain-Machine Interface Technology
chapters.body:
Chapter 277: Brain-Machine Interface Technology

Chapter 277: Brain-Machine Interface Technology

After obtaining the holographic imaging technology, the bionic technology quickly broke through its previous bottleneck. In less than a month, a bionic prosthesis was sent to Chen Chen.

This gadget called a bionic prosthesis had no head, only extending up to the neck. Its overall appearance was masculine, only there was no genitalia. In addition, the torso and limbs were painted to be flesh-colored, with only the gaps between the joints revealing that this was not a normal human body.

As he stared at this gadget that looked like a sutured monster, Chen Chen could not help raising his eyebrows.

“This is the bionic prosthesis based on the integration of bionic and mechanical technology from many movies.”

Little X explained, “The arms, spine, legs, internal organs — the bionic prosthesis can be divided into these four sections. The four sections are assembled to form a complete bionic prosthesis. When they are taken apart, they become separate bionic prostheses and artificial internal organs.”

As Little X spoke, the Black Knights standing on both sides of the bionic prosthesis began to take it apart before their eyes, quickly dismantling it into eight pieces, then spent a few minutes to reassemble it.

“The bone structure of the bionic prosthesis is made of titanium alloy, the outer shell is made of elastic polyethylene to simulate muscle tissue, and the joints are constructed with normal-temperature superconductors. The Meissner effect induced within can perpetually maintain a gap of 0.5 mm between the bearings. This is a completely frictionless bearing technology.”

“In other words, the joints will have zero resistance and zero wear?”

Chen Chen stepped forward and asked while fiddling with the arm of the bionic prosthesis.

“That’s true in principle, but the actual effects will need to be tested before we reach a conclusion.”

Little X replied. Seeing that Chen Chen did not ask any more questions, it said again, “The best control mechanism for the bionic prosthesis is still the God chip, which can convert the nerve impulses transmitted from the human brain into electrical signals, then assign them to the bionic prosthesis. In the bionic prosthetic body, we have implanted nano-scale electrodes to act as nerves, according to the technology in the movies. This allows the user to experience tactile sensations that are very realistic.

“In terms of strength and agility, it can be adjusted according to power. Its maximum strength is 500N. If this is exceeded, the titanium alloy skeleton of the body is likely to become deformed.

“As for the battery, what we’re using for now is the high-efficiency hydrogen fuel cell, which allows users to remain mobile for a week to half a month.”

After listening to Little X’s explanation, Chen Chen asked again, “What about the digestive system?”

“That’s the function of the artificial internal organs.”

Little X continued. “In terms of internal organs, there are three — the artificial heart, artificial lung, and artificial stomach. Both the artificial heart and artificial lung can supply adequate oxygen and blood pressure according to the needs of users, while the artificial stomach can only digest nutrient fluid and water for now. It’s unable to consume food like ordinary people.”

“In other words, even if the user has only one brain left or even only one part of the brain, they can still be carried around upon this body and move like a normal person?”

Chen Chen asked.

“Yes, although we’ve only scratched the surface by combining the bionic technologies of various movies, we’ve still developed a mature bionic prosthesis. If the user has only lost their arms or legs, the limbs of this bionic prosthesis can still be taken off and transplanted to the user.”

Hearing this, Chen Chen smiled spontaneously. “Very good. But its level of technology is still too high. We need to simplify it.”

“You’re planning to commercialize it?” Little X understood at once.

“That’s right. Only commercialization will expand the influence of Blacklight Biotechnology in the world.”

Chen Chen replied, “I’ve been taking NZT-48 and reflecting for a long time recently, and the more I think about it, the more it seems that danger lurks all around us. X, aren’t you curious?”

“You mean the various anomalies of the Earth Federation?” Little X immediately understood what Chen Chen was referring to.

“Yes, during this period, I’ve been thinking, what has happened to humankind that led to the resolute decision of various countries to form the Earth Federation in 1992? And recently, how did they foresee the danger of the sculptures and the epidemic?”

Chen Chen paced slowly around the room. “Besides, what worries me even more is, will there be a possibility that one day, our existence will be anticipated by the Federation?”

“If they really have the means to predict crises, that’s indeed possible to some extent.”

Little X exclaimed, “Moreover, we don’t have any defensive measures.”

“Thus, with each day that I don’t have an answer, I can barely sleep or eat peacefully.”

Chen Chen said solemnly, “I need to continue becoming stronger. While improving the company’s hard power, I’ll gradually approach the truth and try to find out the answer. Now it has been a long time since the launch of the three anti-cancer drugs. Launching a new product every half a year is the perfect frequency, so this time, let’s unveil the bionic prosthetic technology.”

“So, if it’s simplified, besides the elimination of the frictionless bearing technology, what other technologies should be eliminated?” Little X asked.

“In addition to the frictionless bearing technology that requires normal-temperature superconductors, the God chip must be taken out. As for its replacement, I think the brain-machine interface technology is good enough.”

Chen Chen said thoughtfully, “The ‘lace electrode’ that Musk launched in 2020 is a readily available choice. We can send someone to contact them and see if we can obtain the rights to use their company’s ‘lace electrode’.”

The brain-machine interface, in simple terms, was the use of a device to fuse the human brain with a computer. The brain-machine interface was such a device that played the role of this connection.

Generally speaking, brain-machine interfaces were divided into three categories, namely, invasive brain-machine interfaces, semi-invasive brain-machine interfaces, and non-invasive brain-machine interfaces.

With invasive brain-machine interfaces, the device was inserted into the cerebral cortex. This type of brain-machine interface would obtain high-quality neural signals. This equipment was mainly used for treatment purposes, such as restoring the vision of the blind or restoring the motor functions of paralyzed patients.

Semi-invasive brain-machine interfaces were those that were implanted in the cranial cavity but did not penetrate deep into the cerebral cortex.

The last type was non-invasive brain-machine interfaces. As the name suggested, this referred to those brain-machine interfaces that functioned without being implanted in the brain, such as electroencephalography (EEG).

Across all types, useful control signals must be parsed from the complicated cerebral cortex for the interface to work. Still, the invasive brain-machine interface would undoubtedly be much more accurate, while the least precise one was the non-invasive kind.

Now that brain-machine interfaces were leading the world, there existed companies like Neuralink, owned by the famous “real-life Iron Man” Elon Musk. This was why Chen Chen’s first choice was the “lace electrode” technology.

“In addition, we can hold back the artificial organ technology for now.”

After thinking about it, Chen Chen said analytically, “After all, these technologies are still a little startling. Our customers are only those with disabilities. Few people would be willing to abandon their entire body and preserve only their head unless they have amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.”

“Understood. Shall we continue researching bionic prosthetic technology then?” Little X asked.

“Of course.”

Chen Chen said matter-of-factly, “Not only that, but I also hope to come up with the mass-produced versions of Terminator’s T-800 and NS-5 as soon as possible. These can make up for our lack of personnel. Moreover, we can try to manufacture the artificial skin from Alien, just like the cyborg’s.”

With that said, Chen Chen looked at the bionic prosthesis in front of him. He had to admit that although the bionic prosthesis was flesh-colored, its plastic gloss could be discerned at a glance. “Only when we combine the bionic prosthesis and the artificial skin will users feel a much stronger desire to buy it. After all, one type can be easily recognized as a prosthesis and the other type looks no different from the human body. Do users still need to think about which one to choose?”

“Artificial skin technology isn’t too difficult. We can replicate it in about two months.” Little X promised definitively.

chapters.title: Chapter 278: Project Xingtian
chapters.body:
Chapter 278: Project Xingtian

Chapter 278: Project Xingtian

Blacklight Biotechnology Ltd. had a dedicated website on the Internet.

The core server of the website was located in Shangdu of Mainland China and was operated by the data center that was under the head office. This was the same center that Chen Chen once bought.

However, at this time, inside the head office’s building, the employees of the technical department who received the information were initially shocked.

“No way! Is this real or not, real or not!”

A young man in a suit and leather shoes, who had graduated only recently, was yelling, “Did I read it right? This is the information for the website update sent by GM Xia? It can’t be an April Fool’s Day joke, right?”

“What happened?”

The rest of the technical department gathered in front of the computer, then read the copy on the screen. “Bionic technology? It’ll restore the mobility of the disabled... Is this a bionic prosthetic?”

“Isn’t our company a business that develops biomedicine? Why is it suddenly taking on bioengineering?”

“And why didn’t you question the fact that we sell translation software too?”

Everyone joked with each other and started discussing.

Just as the entire technical department was chattering noisily, the supervisor of the technical department suddenly walked in with a stern expression. “Everyone, get ready. In ten minutes, GM Xia will have a meeting with us in the conference room on the 30th floor.”

“Just us?” the young man asked.

“Yes, there’s no one else in the meeting other than the technical department.” The supervisor nodded, then left in a hurry.

“Little Zhang, don’t ask that question anymore. It’s not appropriate.”

As soon as the supervisor left, an older man smiled and came to the young man who had asked the question and patted him on the shoulder.

“Why?” The young man became even more puzzled.

“You’ll understand after you’ve worked here for some time.”

The older man said kindly, “I know that when you applied for a job here, you must have come for the name of Blacklight Biotechnology. Although our company didn’t make it into the Global 500, it was because our company was not listed. Many people said that once our company goes public, the market value would immediately exceed 100 billion USD.”

“Yes, I applied for a job here based on the potential of Blacklight Biotechnology, but...”

The young man called Little Zhang was rather mystified. “From what I can see, it doesn’t feel like a high-tech company at all.”

“That’s right.”

Another employee who looked like a stale fried dough-stick came over, chortling. “Our name sounds awe-inspiring. The world-famous Blacklight Biotechnology is a company that has developed an age-reversal treatment project and a variety of anti-cancer drugs as well as the Blacklight virus vaccine. Here is the head office of Blacklight Biotechnology, so it should be pretty awesome, right? We were deceived.”

“Deceived?”

“Because these projects are all developed in Africa and the company’s real focus is in Africa too. To put it bluntly, our head office is just an external platform.”

The employee with the appearance of a stale dough-stick exclaimed, “Surely you understand now. Although our head office has hundreds of people, there are almost no other tasks besides maintaining the website and operating the Little X Translation System. Despite being a biological company, there isn’t even a research center in Shangdu, which is f*ckng ridiculous!”

“Doesn’t that mean that although GM Xia is the general manager, second only to the boss, she doesn’t even have any real power...”

Little Zhang opened his mouth as if there was something he wanted to blurt out, but the man hurriedly covered his mouth. “It’s enough that you and I understand this. Don’t say it out loud.”

“In truth, it isn’t like what you think.”

Seeing this, the stale dough-stick employee once again said ruefully, “I’ve joined since the establishment of Blacklight Biotechnology. I won’t even conceal the fact that the boss and GM Xia were schoolmates. The two met at university. It’s too bad that this is a classic example of a one-sided love...”

“Is the boss that young? Only a few years older than GM Xia? So, the boss is less than thirty years old?”

Someone who was listening raptly suddenly exclaimed.

“He’s younger than you think.”

The stale dough-stick employee laughed and said, “I met the boss before he went to Africa. He’s three years younger than GM Xia. In other words, if the boss had not dropped out of university, he’d be a fresh graduate this year!”

“That can’t be...”

Everyone burst out yelling and exclaiming.

“Alright, alright, look at what time it is. Shouldn’t we hurry to the conference room so that we’re not late?”

Someone suddenly gave a reminder. “Don’t gossip about GM Xia anymore. Even if she isn’t involved in those research projects, it’d be a piece of cake for her to fire you and me.”

Upon hearing this, everyone dispersed quickly.

...

At the same time, in the conference room on the 30th floor, Xia Yin was standing on the podium, gazing at the empty seats below. She seemed a little lost in thought.

“GM Xia. GM Xia?”

A secretary popped up from the side and clattered loudly toward Xia Yin, then stood there, swaying. “Manager Guo has notified the technical department and they’ll be there soon.”

“Very well.”

Xia Yin came back to her senses and nodded to the secretary. Then she turned on the computer while asking, “Has the information been organized?”

“The materials sent by the African branch have been sorted out. You only need to play them in order,” the secretary replied, then walked to the door of the conference room and looked outside. “GM Xia, they’re here.”

Xia Yin nodded without speaking and watched as the group from the technical department filed in and found their seats. After waiting for a while and seeing that no one else was coming in, she turned to the supervisor who was sitting in the first row before the stage. She asked, “Manager Guo, are all your people from the technical department present?”

“Other than those who are on leave, all the rest are here,” Manager Guo looked back and replied immediately.

“Very good.”

Xia Yin smiled in satisfaction. At the same time, she suddenly exuded the shrewd, capable aura of a strong, independent woman. She seemed to be a different person entirely. Meanwhile, she pressed a button on the desktop and the screen behind her instantly began to display a mix of text and image.

“You should’ve received the email sent to you just now, so I won’t beat around the bush. I’m happy to announce that our Blacklight Biotechnology has achieved another glorious success. According to the update from the African branch, the first-generation bionic prosthetic project we’ve launched has finished its development and will soon enter the commercial stage.

“The bionic prosthetic project developed by the African branch uses the ‘lace electrode’ brain-machine interface technology from Neuralink of North America. We’ve obtained the rights from Neuralink. The reason for this meeting is that, for the official website, we hope that everyone in the technical department can come up with a good plan and creative ideas.”

Xia Yin said in a low voice on the podium, “After all, our company’s website hasn’t changed for two or three years. Let’s use this opportunity to revamp the company’s look...”

As Xia Yin spoke, the screen behind her began to flicker, quickly revealing a humanoid monster with only a body and without a head.

Xingtian?

Everyone in the audience immediately recognized the figure when they saw the ink painting that was influenced by Chinese culture.

“I believe that, as Chinese descendants, you must’ve heard of our own myths and legends. Xingtian is one of them. According to legend, Xingtian was an official of the Yan Emperor. Because he couldn’t accept that the Yellow Emperor had defeated the Yan Emperor, he went to challenge the Yellow Emperor. After Xingtian was beheaded by the Yellow Emperor, he continued the fight with his two nipples as his eyes and his belly button as his mouth...

“This is the origin of Xingtian. I’ll suggest this idea to you — if this project is publicized, I hope to use this Chinese myth from the Classic of Mountains and Sea[1], ‘Xingtian’, as the theme. First, we can promote the culture of Mainland China. Secondly, Xingtian’s persona is indeed in line with the inspirational spirit of being ‘disabled but not disheartened’.”

As Xia Yin explained all this, a line from an ancient poem suddenly appeared on the screen beside Xingtian —”Jingwei[2] bites hold of twigs, determined to fill the deep-blue sea. Xingtian wields his spear and shield, his old ambitions still burning fiercely!”

“Therefore, I think the new bionic prosthetic project can be called —

“Project Xingtian!”

[1] A classical Chinese text of mythic geography and beasts.

[2] A mythological bird who was once the Yan Emperor’s daughter. See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jingwei.

chapters.title: Chapter 279: Corporate Empire
chapters.body:
Chapter 279: Corporate Empire

Chapter 279: Corporate Empire

A few days later, Chen Chen sat in the conference room and quickly flipped through a planner sent over by Xia Yin.

“Project Xingtian?”

After turning to the last page of the planner, Chen Chen could not hold back a smile. With his mouse, he clicked on a checkbox on the computer.

At this moment, the company’s new website style and the name of the bionic prosthetic project had been formally confirmed.

Next, it was time for the head office to begin selling and promoting.

However, in the planner sent by the head office, Xia Yin expressed the hope that Chen Chen could make more public appearances. It would be best if he could become a symbol, an emblem, of Blacklight Biotechnology, just like Tencent’s Ma Huateng, Alibaba’s Jack Ma, and JD.com’s Liu Qiangdong.

After all, Chen Chen did have the qualifications to become a “symbol”— he had developed an age-reversal treatment project at the age of eighteen, then dropped out of college and started his own business. After only three years, he became the boss of a corporation with a market value of tens of billions.

Youth, career success, countless triumphs and accolades — these were all part of Chen Chen’s “symbol”.

Xia Yin hoped that Chen Chen could attend TED to give a speech for this reason.

TED, an acronym of the three words “technology”, “entertainment”, and “design”, was a private non-profit organization in North America. The organization was known for its TED conference, with the objective of “ideas worth spreading”.

Those who qualified for the TED conference were also preeminent talents from various fields such as Nobel Prize winners or billionaires like Bill Gates and Musk who had created new legends or scientists, philosophers, artists, explorers, psychologists, linguists, religious leaders, philanthropists, and so on.

Since 2006, the TED Talks had been posted online and quickly gained worldwide attention. It had become one of the most prestigious speech platforms in the world. If Chen Chen could get on this stage, Blacklight Biotechnology’s Project Xingtian would swiftly spread throughout the world. The effect of this would be much better than spending hundreds of millions or even billions of dollars on global advertising.

Seeing this, Chen Chen thought for a while and finally agreed to this proposal.

With the increasing influence of the company, Chen Chen’s name was destined to be known to even more people. As he was the first to create the Blacklight virus vaccine, Chen Chen was even nominated last September in the list of candidates for this year’s Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine.

If there was no unexpected plot twist, Chen Chen’s Nobel Prize this year was guaranteed. This was something the North American government had no choice but to do because if Chen Chen lost the nomination, it would affect the perceived authority and fairness of the Nobel Prize.

After all, in the field of medicine in 2023, there would be no one who had performed better than Chen Chen.

As Chen Chen closed the webpage, he started to ponder silently.

Now, the company had almost reached its peak in the development of biopharmaceuticals, owning several patents and also the trump card that was the Eco Science City. As long as the three anti-cancer drugs passed clinical trials in a few years and they released the AD-001 drug that could completely cure Alzheimer’s, not even companies like Pfizer and Novartis would be able to compete with them.

At that point, Blacklight Biotechnology would officially become an unprecedented world-class pharmaceutical monopoly. It would be immensely difficult for even one or two continents or countries to do anything against them.

However, as a rule, when Blacklight Biotechnology had developed to that extent, it had reached its limit. It was impossible to continue expanding, for instance, reaching the level of the Umbrella Corporation.

After all, the potential of a single industry was limited. Even if the global pharmaceutical industry was united, there was no power that could equal the Earth Federation. If Chen Chen wanted to go further, he had only one option —

Horizontal cross-border development!

If a group wanted to achieve long-term development, it must actively expand into other fields and reach the top in all walks of life. This was the path of development for the corporate empire.

What was a corporate empire?

It was a corporation with the characteristics of “imperialism”. It did not act in full accordance with economic laws and had its unique means of doing things such as private armed forces that answered only to the company, a unique economic monopoly system, and top-secret projects in cooperation with the government that were hidden from the public.

Even the average country would be somewhat wary of such a corporation. Any of its actions could cause a global sensation and its political influence was no less than that of a nation.

Even for continents, their control over this sort of corporate super monopoly was very limited. Chen Chen’s goal was to become an enterprise like this or even stronger!

Still, cross-border development was not that easy. One would have no connections, technology or talents. Everything must begin from scratch, just like starting a business with nothing in hand.

Therefore, for ordinary companies, cross-border expansion was indeed a very dangerous move and might even lead to bankruptcy. However, for Chen Chen, it was not a problem at all.

After all, Chen Chen had a key that could open the door to various worlds.

Through that key, he could obtain any advanced technology that he wanted. These technologies would allow him to stand at the forefront of any field including energy, electronics, network, industry, even aerospace and military.

As long as he had enough time, Chen Chen would be able to create a real-life Umbrella Corporation sooner or later...

...

Three days later, a major change suddenly occurred on the official website of Blacklight Biotechnology Ltd.

Previously, there were only four sections on the homepage of Blacklight Biotechnology’s official website, namely the biopharmaceutical section, the medical project section, the infectious disease section, and the electronic technology section.

The four major sections were represented by the colors green, white, gray, and black, so they were clearly distinct at a glance.

The biopharmaceutical section, naturally, listed the three anti-cancer drugs that had entered the clinical trial stage while the medical project section included the “age-reversal treatment” project, the “Alzheimer’s disease reversal treatment” project, and so on.

As for the infectious disease section, the research materials on the Blacklight virus and vaccine were publicly shared for anyone in the world to browse through and learn from.

Each section had its own independent forum. Due to Blacklight Biotechnology’s prestige, these forums had gradually attracted the attention of countless scholars. Many experts in the medical field had registered accounts on the official website of Blacklight Biotechnology, which had become a professional website similar to an academic network.

Under the management of Little X, the forums under the official website of Blacklight Biotechnology had been managed in an orderly manner, thus winning the favor of those academic experts who now regarded it as a permanent haunt.

However, just this morning, some academic experts and editors of various journals were surprised by Blacklight Biotechnology’s new official website when they opened it up to search for materials as usual.

Among the four sections that were once arranged in a square, a new section had suddenly appeared.

The color representing that section was gold and there was a new name on the section — tissue engineering.

If someone clicked in curiously, they would be greeted with a large blank page. In the center of the blank screen, there was a big “question mark” and behind the question mark was a human figure.

This figure was shadowy and vague, and one could just about tell that it was a human figure. Still, the peculiar thing was that this figure did not have a head...

When they saw this new additional section, many people who were silently watching the company now understood that Blacklight Biotechnology had new products.

As a newly emerging pharmaceutical giant in the field of biopharmaceuticals, Blacklight Biotechnology did not have a very high profile, but its every move still roused the attention of countless people. After this information was released, many international media competed to make the first report:

[Blacklight Biotechnology Is Suspected to Be Releasing a New Product. This Time It May Expand Into Tissue Engineering!]—Wall Street Journal.

[Boom! According to Industry Sources, Blacklight Biotechnology Is Suspected to Be Joining Forces With Musk!]—Tencent News.

[Blacklight Biotechnology’s New Venture Is Confirmed to Be Related to Tissue Engineering. Its Emblem Is Suspected to Be the Ancient Chinese Figure Xingtian!]—Sina Express.

[When People Opened the Main Page of Blacklight Biotechnology’s Official Website, the Entire Earth Federation Was Stunned!]—UC Subscription.

With the exposure from major media, Blacklight Biotechnology had publicly announced the release date of the new product and also announced that on 1 April, the founder and chairman of Blacklight Biotechnology would give a TED Talk. The venue of the talk was arranged by the TED officials, in a rare exception, to be in Blacklight Biotechnology’s Eco Science City!

This gesture greatly boosted Blacklight Biotechnology’s reputation. Once again, Blacklight Biotechnology had become a discussion topic among the biotechnology crowd. Some netizens who were more invested in the biological field had even more enthusiastic discussions.

“The project is in the field of tissue engineering. If you consider this along with the headless horseman, it’s very easy to guess. It must be related to head replacement surgery!”

Under the new section of Blacklight Biotechnology, some netizens speculated.

“My *ss! If it’s head replacement surgery, do you think the International Bioethics Committees would agree? Not to mention that stem cells still haven’t solved the problem of reconnecting the spinal cord and nerves!”

Someone more knowledgeable quickly refuted this.

“Maybe it’s cloning. Recently, wasn’t there news circulating online that many wealthy people are cloning their bodies privately for future organ transplantations? Maybe this silhouette of a figure without a head is a clone without a head, who can say?”

Someone else guessed again.

“Impossible, any human cloning is prohibited by the international law of the Earth Federation. So, all of you saying that it’s human cloning and head replacement surgery can give it a rest.”

...

chapters.title: Chapter 280: Press Conference On Xingtian
chapters.body:
Chapter 280: Press Conference On Xingtian

Chapter 280: Press Conference on Xingtian

Many people noticed that the city built by Blacklight Biotechnology, the Eco Science City, had launched several major projects.

For example, there were several factories of unknown purpose. These factories spanning dozens of kilometers, plus the Blacklight Nuclear Power Plant by the sea, all comprised a vast industrial park that was practically the largest place for manufacturing in Africa.

In addition, in the center of the Eco Science City, a building that had been under construction for more than a year was finally completed during this time.

This was a hemispherical building with an area of about four hectares and a diameter of about 200 meters. Based on its interior, it should be a public facility akin to a multifunctional stadium.

Nonetheless, unlike most stadiums, this hemispherical building had a huge dome made of the latest lightweight-material technology. Beneath this was 40,000 seats and a high-tech stage that could be raised and lowered in the center.

The stage was shaped like a distilling flask. A straight passage extended into the backstage where actors could enter the stage directly from backstage. Above the stage was a hexahedron, which every side featured a huge screen of 15000mm x 10000mm. This was large enough so that even the audience in the furthest seat could see everything on the stage.

The name of this enclosed multifunctional stadium was the Blacklight Dome.

Presently, a large number of people had arrived at this dome-like building. These people thronged in and out, transporting pieces of equipment into the building.

This was because the TED conference in 2024 would be held here.

Xia Yin handed the planner to Chen Chen. After Chen Chen approved it, Xia Yin again sent it to the CEO in Africa, Qian Wenhuan. Qian Wenhuan would run the event on the ground.

To get Project Xingtian an unprecedented amount of publicity, the head office even personally negotiated with the TED organizers and set the venue for 2024’s TED conference to be in the Blacklight Dome of the Eco Science City.

Moreover, instead of inviting only 1,350 attendees as was the case with previous TED conferences, this time, Blacklight Biotechnology had issued 30,000 invitations in one go, inviting all well-known audience members who had been to a TED conference before. They even waived the 7,500 USD ticket fee and all expenses would be borne by Blacklight Biotechnology.

This was not only because Chen Chen would be giving a speech at this TED conference, but also because the latest press conference would be held at this venue!

Such an extravagant move immediately attracted the attention of the world. There were some people who, despite not receiving the invitation letter, began to quickly apply for visas to come to Namibia, wanting to participate in this grand technological event.

At the end of March, the number of tourists in the Eco Science City exceeded 100,000 for the first time. The huge flow of people brought explosive economic benefits. It was hard to get a hotel room in the Eco Science City and even the hotels of nearby coastal cities like Walvis Bay and Swakopmund were already overcrowded.

Many people had no choice but to stay in the capital city, Windhoek, over a hundred kilometers away.

Besides the fact that this was TED, a world-renowned speech conference, where all the participants were leaders from all walks of life, the more crucial reason that everyone went crazy over this was that many of them came for Blacklight Biotechnology’s latest press conference.

Many people had figured out long ago that Blacklight Biotechnology was a biomedical company standing at the apex of the Earth Federation. Although its market value was not high, not even making it into the world’s top 500, this was only because the company had not been funded and listed.

This company had become what it was today entirely on its own...

...

On 1 April, in front of the Blacklight Dome in the center of the Eco Science City, there was a lot of busy traffic and bustling people.

At eleven o’clock in the morning, hundreds of news vehicles and media from all over the world gathered in the square before the main entrance of the Blacklight Dome. Many reporters and anchors had to stand in the crowd, face the camera, and start reporting.

“Ladies and gentlemen, this is Federation Info. It’s Anna here. We’re now in the Eco Science City of Namibia. As you can see, there’s a massive crowd! There are hundreds of media outlets besides us. One must say that today is a special day because today is when the hero of the Earth Federation — Blacklight Biotechnology — will hold its latest conference!

“As the benchmark for biomedical companies, Blacklight Biotechnology isn’t that high-profile. Ever since this mysterious company entered the public eye, this is only the second time it has held a press conference. What’s more dramatic is that the last press conference this company held was exactly this time last year. That press conference shook up the entire world. I wonder if this press conference will disappoint everyone?

“In any case, let us wait and see!”

The host of Federation Info spoke frankly and eloquently, and not too far from her was the reporter from Agence France-Presse.

“Dear audiences on the other side of the screen, this is AFP. Currently, I’m standing before the main entrance of the Blacklight Dome. We’re here in the Eco Science City of the famous Blacklight Biotechnology. Speaking of this legendary company, I think there aren’t many who haven’t heard about how Blacklight had developed a vaccine for the Blacklight virus one month in advance of many other continents, saving all of Africa, and even indirectly saving the Earth Federation...

“Moreover, they have also developed three anti-cancer targeted drugs, which they claim to have a 90% chance of curing cancer. In addition, they have also developed an age-reversal treatment plan. They’re practically a celebrity within the pharmaceutical industry and their existence has saved the equivalent of over a hundred million lives...

“And today, not only is it another one of Blacklight Biotechnology’s press conferences, but this time, the extremely mysterious founder of this company will appear in the public eye for the first time as a TED speaker to deliver a talk. And what kind of enlightenment will he bring to our lives? I’m Anson from AFP, please stay tuned in for our follow-up report!”

...

This kept happening in the square of the Blacklight Dome. As it got closer to noon, more people started flocking in. These people were dressed in suits and shoes, exuding an air of confidence. They waved their admission tickets, went past the usher, and verified their identities. Following that, they had to pass through a metal detector and an X-ray machine before entering the venue.

There were 40,000 seats in the Blacklight Dome. The reason why only 30,000 people were invited was that the remaining 10,000 were for the staff members who had signed up as volunteers. In addition, there were also seats for major media outlets.

The Black Knights were responsible for the event’s security. To ensure Chen Chen’s safety during his talk, five hundred security guards were placed throughout the venue to keep watch. A thousand Black Knights were also situated among the audience.

Furthermore, Little X was also monitoring the Internet around the clock. It would be able to respond quickly if it found any traces of information that hinted at danger.

At a quarter past 12, when everyone was completely seated, the venue lights suddenly dimmed. At the same time, a circular podium at the center of the venue slowly descended from the roof of the dome while the six enormous screens lit up one after another, fully revealing the stage before everyone’s eyes.

Without a doubt, the TED Talk was starting.

chapters.title: Chapter 281: We’Re Gods!
chapters.body:
Chapter 281: We’Re Gods!

Chapter 281: We’re Gods!

The hexagon-shaped screen by the top of the podium illuminated the stage along with the stage lights. After a brief moment, they began to dim out again.

The only light source that remained was a bright spotlight at the dome which was pointed onto the empty stage.

Soon, people caught a glimpse of a man approaching the podium from the edges of the stage untouched by the spotlight.

The appearance of the man was accompanied by a solemn piano symphony that resounded across the hall.

The hall that was at first still filled with the chatter of the crowd immediately became silent. The audience stayed mindfully silent as they turned their curious attention to the podium on stage.

The screen by the top of the podium slowly brightened. At the same time, several drones fitted for videography descended from the top of the dome and hovered in the air. The live footage transmitted from the drones were projected onto the screen.

Meanwhile, the display screen above the podium showed the man stepping into the spotlight, into the audience’s view.

It was a Chinese man of indiscernible age.

The man combed his hair regularly and wore a regular black suit without any distinctive features. He looked rather young and relatively plain. The only thing about the man that left a strong impression was his eyes.

They were a pair of piercing eyes with a strange glow underneath them. Despite the man’s young appearance, his pair of distinctive eyes seemed to render his very age a mystery.

If not for this pair of eyes, it would be sensible to gauge the man at approximately twenty-years-old. However, upon meeting those eyes, one may think that he was in his thirties, perhaps even in his forties.

Those were eyes that penetrated everything. They were sharp and familiar with the way of the world.

“Darwin’s ‘Theory of Evolution’ tells us that we evolved from ancient forest apes.”

The man suddenly began his narration without giving any form of introduction or attempts at warming up the crowd.

“But what exactly is evolution?”

The man raised his hand and gestured with his thumb and index finger. “Modern science has taught us that evolution is the transformation of genes.

“Many believe that evolution is a state of transformation from the simple to the complex, an ‘evolution’ from a lower-level to a higher-level. These are all misconceptions, it’s true meaning – the genetics that’s suitable for its surroundings are preserved while the ones which are unfit are eliminated. There’s no such thing as level classification in genetics.”

The speaker smiled. “In the Mainland, the Theory of Evolution was once also interpreted as the Theory of Progression. It was not a false claim at the time as the only distinctive difference between the two phrases is that the former discusses unordered evolution while the latter revolves around ordered evolution. In the modern mainstream scientific field, the former was more widely recognized.

“Just like how our ancestors have always lived in Africa but our relatives have left Africa two million years ago. Under different circumstances and environments, the accumulated mutation of genes caused prehistoric humans to embark on separate evolutionary paths.
