As for the PK Armor, Chen Chen had extracted a few more when he was bored. As of now he already had ten sets of PK Armor in possession. As they used nuclear fusion battery technology, the power in these armors was still very durable.

Chen Chen still remembered that he had tested the internal energy of the items after they were extracted from the USB drive into reality. The result of the test was that the energy stored in the extracted items would not be greater than the amount of electricity consumed.

Case in point, the “Galaxy” pendant. Originally, the pendant should have contained energy comparable to an actual star, but after it was extracted into reality, there was only a small amount of energy left inside. This tiny amount of energy was equivalent to the power consumed by the USB flash drive when it extracted the Galaxy pendant.

Therefore, the same applied to the PK Armor. According to the formula that 1,200 kWh of electricity was required to extract one gram of material, extracting the PK Armor from the USB drive would consume approximately 36 million kWh of electricity.

The energy of a kilowatt-hour of electricity was roughly 3.6 million joules, and the energy released by the explosion of 1 kilogram of TNT was about 4.19 million joules. That was to say, extracting a PK Armor required roughly the energy of a nuclear bomb equivalent to 31 million tons of TNT. This was about half the energy of the Tsar Bomba.

No matter how powerful the nuclear fusion battery in the PK Armor was, the capacity of half a Tsar Bomba was way beyond its limits. Therefore, the extracted PK Armor was basically fully charged every time.

Just in case, Chen Chen also extracted the escape pod that charged the PK Armor in The Predator (2018) into reality, so there was no need to worry about the energy of the PK Armor.

At this point, after everything was in place, Chen Chen clicked on Gantz again and paused the image in the middle of the movie, in the room where the protagonist was.

Although it was very likely that at the other end of the portal lay a world where civilization had been destroyed, it was better to be safe than sorry, after all. It was more prudent to open the passage in a location with fewer people. Surely this was better than opening the passage in a subway station or even the room that housed the Gantz black sphere, right?

As Chen Chen opened the portal solemnly, the computer screen suddenly went black and the entire screen seemed to be upgraded from 2D to 3D. A continuously rotating black orb emerged from the center of the screen and rotated ceaselessly.

Black tentacles swarmed restlessly within this black orb. As the orb rotated, the tentacles were drawn into an arch-like spiral, finally causing the black orb to turn into a black galaxy...

This small black orb that looked like a galaxy expanded sharply, swallowing the screen within a few seconds, and spilling out over the screen.

The portal was formed.

As Chen Chen stared at the portal. He could not refrain from asking, “X, is it just me? Why do I feel that each time I open the portal, it seems to be constantly evolving in some way?”

“It’s true and the portal is getting larger...”

Little X confirmed. “It’s not just you, Sir Godfather.”

“...”

Chen Chen pondered for a while before he wondered aloud, “Is this a phenomenon of the USB flash drive itself or is it that the second-form USB drive is imperceptibly evolving in a certain direction?”

He thought for a while but now was not the time to be worrying about this. Chen Chen finally waved one hand. Instantly, a Black Knight who was ready for defense, wearing a PK Armor, stepped into the passage.

From Chen Chen’s point of view, the Black Knight seemed to have walked into a circular door through which no light could pass, only an endless black mist. Then, the Black Knight disappeared completely.

One minute, two minutes, three minutes...

When the agreed time of five minutes was up, there was a jingling sound and a silver Rubik’s cube suddenly fell out of the dark passage, landed on the floor, and rolled over twice.

Chen Chen’s eyes lit up. He gestured with one hand and the Rubik’s cube flew directly into his hands. Then Chen Chen pressed a few of the keys familiarly. This silver Rubik’s cube immediately opened up a few panels and lit up the entire room, filling the place with a lifelike 3D image.

In the image, the Black Knight who had just walked through the passage was standing amid ruins. The room that used to belong to the protagonist of Gantz, Kei Kurono, had long collapsed. Looking around, the entire city of Tokyo was a wreck with endless weeds and vines surrounding the place. Those vines were even wrapped around the skyscrapers, dyeing the entire city as towering greenery...

Seeing this scene, Chen Chen only felt that it was rather ironic.

Although it was destined that the USB drive would open a portal to a world where civilization had fallen, it was still ironic to see the majestic steel capital city, which once stood proudly away from the natural world, was now once again a paradise of nature.

Although humans had the ability to challenge the natural world, they still could not defeat time after all.

“Could it be that, under the scouring of time, no matter how splendid civilization was, it would only end up destroyed one day?”

Chen Chen murmured.

This rueful feeling came and went in a flash. Following that, Chen Chen gathered himself and looked again at the 3D world projected by the silver Rubik’s cube.

He noticed that, in these five minutes, the Black Knight only walked around for a short time. Even so, he had seen many small animals living in the stretch of ruins including wild cats and dogs, along with many parrots and birds.

These were pets once raised by humans, but after the demise of humanity, they became the first masters of these ruins.

As the five minutes ended, the Black Knight turned off the images recorded by the silver Rubik’s cube.

“Something’s wrong.”

After the screening was over, Chen Chen spoke immediately.

“Sir Godfather, what do you mean?”

“From the picture just now, we can see that those buildings collapsed naturally.”

Chen Chen said at once, “Did you not notice that those buildings had collapsed naturally after decades of erosion from rain and vegetation? In other words, the city of Tokyo on the other side of the passage had only collapsed after the complete extinction of humans. The descendants of those pets also prove this point.”

“I see. You’re saying that mankind didn’t perish in a war against aliens?”

Although Little X was only an artificial intelligence, it was close to the level of ordinary people in terms of divergent thinking. At this time, it immediately understood Chen Chen’s thoughts. “If the world accessed by the USB drive must be a world where human civilization is extinct, based on the direction of Gantz’s plotline, their world should have been destroyed by aliens. In that case, after opening the passage, we should’ve seen a crumbling, fallen city that had been ravaged by war.”

chapters.title: Chapter 291: Entering Gantz
chapters.body:
Chapter 291: Entering Gantz

Chapter 291: Entering Gantz

“That’s right. That’s the fishy part.”

Chen Chen nodded. “According to the plot of the original comics, humanity had defeated the aliens. This is pretty far fetched. After all, humans have manufactured spacecraft, yet their weapons are still stuck at the level of conventional firearms and cold weapons... Even putting that aside, I prefer the aliens who have weapons of mass destruction as a trump card. Even if they can’t destroy Earth, it’ll be a piece of cake to destroy humans.

“But now, it seems that human civilization has not gone extinct because of war. In other words, either the aliens used some superior method or something else destroyed the human race.”

“So, do we still head to Gantz’s dimension to search for more advanced technology?”

Little X said, “I’m worried that their technology was annihilated along with human civilization.”

“Nah, that’s good news for me.”

Chen Chen waved his hand decisively. “If human civilization had perished in the ravages of war, there would be nothing left. However, now it seems that, after human civilization was wiped out for some unknown reason, those buildings have been preserved instead. Doesn’t this mean that the technology relating to the Gantz black sphere might have survived?”

“So, that’s it.”

Little X suddenly understood. “Just like in Elysium’s world?”

“Indeed.”

Chen Chen nodded but something occurred to him again and suddenly his expression shifted. “Still, I’d prefer that the extinction of mankind was caused by aliens and not something similar to those three sculptures...”

At this point in the discussion, Chen Chen and Little X had weighed the pros and cons. After Chen Chen silently calculated the energy left in the USB flash drive, he suddenly said, “X, is there a backup of Elysium?”

“Nope. But I can download it now.”

Little X asked, “Is there something you want to get from Elysium?”

“Of course. I want to take out that ‘Bugatti-Folge’ luxury spaceship’.”

Chen Chen replied with a serious expression, “This vehicle would be the easiest method to escape gravity and travel in outer space.”

Little X silently estimated the spaceship’s weight. “Indeed, this is probably the best choice for visits to alternate dimensions. In addition to lightweight and small size, it also has the ability to travel in outer space. It can just about cope with whatever conditions are on the other side of the passage. Still, you’re thinking of using it in Gantz’s dimension?”

“Yes, I’m going to look for the Gantz black sphere’s technology in the world of Gantz.”

Chen Chen affirmed. “The plot in the comics has revealed everything. The black sphere is created by the human government and the Gantz black spheres even have a dedicated assembly line. As for the source of technology, it came from an alien civilization that was about to be destroyed. Before being destroyed, they sent their scientific and technological data to Earth through an information channel. Thus, those empty rooms came about as well as the amazing teleporting and human replicating technology.

“But now it appears that the Gantz sphere’s technology is still incomplete. For the black sphere to teleport humans, it needs the support of other facilities. This would be the technology we must look for in Gantz’s dimension. Nonetheless, there’s a whole planet over there. Without any transportation, it’d be difficult to move around.”

With that, Chen Chen temporarily closed the channel that was connected to Gantz.

According to Chen Chen’s previous testing, the portal opening had a certain degree of repeatability, allowing temporary closure. As long as no other channel was opened, the previous channel would be retained.

Moreover, using the first form of the USB drive to extract items would not affect the reopening of the said portal.

At this time, following the discussion between the two, Elysium Space was downloaded again. Meanwhile, Chen Chen used the method of extraction to directly obtain the red Bugatti from the movie.

In an instant, a dark red car, over four meters long and less than two meters tall, appeared in their reality out of thin air.

This “car” was entirely streamlined, just like an actual red sports car, but the only difference from a sports car was that it had no tires. Only the two plasma pulse booster engines at the rear were in charge of breaking free of gravity.

The interior of the “car” had four seats, of which there was a main driver’s seat on the right and three passenger seats on the left. It could carry a total of four passengers.

In the movie, the protagonist used a delayed explosive gun to fire a bullet into the opponent’s engine when they were taking off. When the opponent was about to enter the supersonic state, he detonated the bullet and caused the spaceship to crash outside the city.

This also showed that this kind of spacecraft was only an ordinary civilian model and did not have advanced protection capabilities.

Of course, there were also military spaceships with protective capabilities in the movie, but their sizes were far beyond what Chen Chen could extract.

Chen Chen patted the car-like spaceship and said, “Bring the pressure sensor and weigh this car.”

Immediately, a Black Knight brought the pressure sensor over and everyone worked together to lift the spacecraft onto the sensor, then measured its weight at once.

The weight of this “Bugatti-Folge” spaceship was unexpectedly light, only about 1400kg, which was less than a ton and a half.

“It’s almost the same weight as a Medpod 3000.”

Chen Chen did some silent calculations but was not reassured and he asked Little X, “How about it? Can I enter the world of Gantz fully equipped and with the spaceship?”

“After calculation, the power left in the USB drive isn’t a problem, but the only problem is that, after the spaceship is brought into Gantz’s world, it can’t be brought back to reality because there isn’t sufficient energy for its return. That is, for this mission to Gantz, the spaceship must be abandoned regardless of mission outcome.”

Chen Chen was silent for a while before nodding. “Understood. You can insert your clone X-112 into the Bugatti Spaceship and let it take charge of piloting. This time I’ll go there myself.”

“... Got it.”

Little X knew that it could not stop Chen Chen, but still said, “Please be careful.”

“Don’t worry, as long as I don’t encounter the type of sculptures that were in Elysium or if they’re not in an active state, I’ll be fine.”

Chen Chen shook his head serenely. “And don’t worry about aliens or anything like that. The ruins of Tokyo have already told us that Earth is already in the state of an extinct civilization. If it were occupied by aliens, this wouldn’t be how it looks like...”

At this point, Chen Chen himself opened the portal to the dimension of Gantz. This time, he also wore a GS Combat Suit in addition to a PK Armor before he walked into the channel with the two Black Knights.

Behind the two Black Knights, the small Bugatti Spaceship also floated up amid the buzzing of its engines and drifted toward the passage...

In the next moment, the black light had swallowed their figures.

chapters.title: Chapter 292: Abnormality
chapters.body:
Chapter 292: Abnormality

Chapter 292: Abnormality

It was that familiar sensation again.

As soon as he entered the channel, Chen Chen was overcome by a sense of weightlessness. At the same time, his eyes seemed to lose their vision and his ears lost their hearing. In other words, Chen Chen lost all sense of his body, leaving nothing but an intangible consciousness, floating in the endless darkness...

Nonetheless, this feeling only lasted for a moment and the next second, Chen Chen’s eyes saw light again.

At this moment, he was already standing on a pile of rubble. Next to him, there were two other Black Knights and the small Bugatti Spaceship that had followed behind. The Black Knight who had come beforehand was standing quietly before him.

“Click, click, click...”

Chen Chen’s six scanners quickly popped out of his back and scanned the surroundings swiftly, but only detected a few wild cats and dogs in the ruins. Everything else was normal.

“Drone.”

After that, Chen Chen said softly.

At Chen Chen’s command, the four M-1 drones upon the Bugatti Spaceship took off in silence. As they took off, their shape quickly turned blurry. By the time they had flown for dozens of meters, these four drones had completely blended into the air and vanished without a trace.

The drones were much faster than humans at searching. After a few minutes, the four drones had examined everything within one kilometer. This was all sensed by X-112, the clone of Little X, who duly reported each update.

“Sir Commander, no signs of human activities have been found within one kilometer. There’s only a large number of animals and plants...”

“Expand the range and find out what the conditions are like within ten kilometers,” Chen Chen ordered.

After the order was issued, the drones moved toward the unknown to explore further.

During the drone exploration, Chen Chen jumped off the ruins and walked toward a nearby apartment that had not collapsed. Although this apartment was already entangled in vines, it was made of conventional reinforced concrete instead of ordinary wooden boards. Therefore, the structure had not collapsed under the assault of wind and rain over many years.

The original glass doors of the apartment had been damaged long ago. It was dark and empty inside, like a gaping abyss. As Chen Chen and the three Black Knights entered, a cloud of dust was stirred up by the breeze.

Chen Chen squatted and touched the ground. A thick layer of ash had accumulated. Based on the thickness of this blanket of ash, no one had entered this place for decades.

Even after seeing this, Chen Chen still did not take the situation lightly but walked in cautiously. After that, he let a Black Knight take the lead as they looked through each room in the apartment.

This apartment had five floors, each of which had ten rooms in a row, connected by a corridor. At both ends of the corridor were stairs leading up and down. This was the simplest layout for apartment buildings.

All the doors in the apartment were anti-theft doors made of alloy, which had remained intact for so long. However, most of the anti-theft doors on the first and second floors exhibited signs of damage. It seemed that someone had forcibly broken in. Even so, those marks were very old as well.

After walking around these rooms, Chen Chen did not find anything special. There was nothing else but some rotten furniture. Chen Chen even found a wallet that had not been taken away in time, with a large amount of cash still inside.

However, all food-related items had disappeared.

“In other words, the people who broke in later took only the food, but not the money.”

Chen Chen reflected out loud, “In that case, someone must’ve pried open these doors and broke into the apartment after the government collapsed. These people had adapted to a world of anarchy. They were searching for food all over the place, yet they wouldn’t spare a glance for money.”

After silently imprinting this clue into his mind, Chen Chen moved past these opened doors and walked toward the highest floor of the apartment.

Those who broke in must have had plenty of options, yet they only pried open the doors on the first and second floors. Starting from the third floor, only a few doors were pried open and most of them were intact.

There were even fewer doors that were pried open on the fourth floor and by the fifth floor, none of the doors had been pried open.

After noticing this, Chen Chen no longer hesitated. He beckoned and a Black Knight immediately stepped forward and tugged violently at the first door that had rusted long ago. There was the sound of metal being twisted, then a loud bang. The door was forcibly opened by the Black Knight.

These Black Knights were like mini supermen who were perfectly fused with the T-virus in the first place. After they put on the GS Combat Suits and the PK Armor, their power was augmented to a terrifying point. Twisting open a rusty iron door was a walk in the park.

When the door was opened, the sound of splitting metal ruptured the ancient silence of this apartment. The sound rippled into the distance, echo after echo...

At this point, Chen Chen frowned because he suddenly realized that this apartment seemed a bit too quiet. It was unlike the ruins that he had just come from. There, after just a glance with his PK Armor’s infrared vision, he could detect many insects and a few wild animals.

However, in this apartment, forget about wild animals, there was not even a single insect chirp.

“Something is wrong.”

Chen Chen took a step back abruptly and said, “Let’s withdraw!”

Without any hesitation, the Black Knight who had just opened the anti-theft door followed Chen Chen down the stairs.

Along the way, Chen Chen could not help but feel his nerves tensing up. He felt that even the air had solidified for some reason. There seemed to be something in this apartment, which was slowly awakening after the noise they made.

‘Is it just in my head? Or...’

Chen Chen felt cold sweat drenching his back. He had not seen anything but for some reason, he felt his hair standing on end. This chill crawling down his spine made him bite the bullet and quickly flee from the apartment.

Only when they had gotten out of the area of the apartment did Chen Chen slowly exhale. The heavy feeling had faded gradually after he left the apartment.

He could not tell what kind of feeling it was. The air seemed to be weighed down by something heavy. The atmosphere that was once light and relaxed became as sluggish as a swamp, turning into a more suffocating pressure.

This was something that only Chen Chen could feel.

It was just that this feeling was rather indefinite.

Chen Chen could not help but glance down at the PK Armor he was wearing. It seemed that this armor had blocked off his feeling, only...

“What kind of feeling was that?”

Chen Chen frowned. He could tell that this feeling did not arise from the five senses, so it must have been an instinctive mental reaction to a part of reality. Strictly speaking, it should fall under the category of his sixth sense.

“In other words, I’ve sensed something that I’ve never noticed before and whatever it was caused a direct response in my mind, resulting in a sense of danger?”

This feeling was not groundless. Rather, it was more subconscious. For example, when Chen Chen scanned the area just now, he had observed many animals living in the ruins. Although the surroundings were deserted, they were full of life.

However, after entering the apartment, although Chen Chen had overlooked the presence of those small animals out of habit, the difference in the surroundings still put his subconscious on guard. Thus, Chen Chen’s conscious mind would only feel that something was off, without being able to pinpoint what exactly.

It was only in the next moment that the subconscious mind realized what was wrong — the small animals around were gone. Only then did Chen Chen experience a shocking realization and he left the apartment quickly.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen turned around again and looked at the apartment behind him.

Even so, when he got a good look, his pupils shrank furiously!

At some point, all the doors in the apartment had been opened...

It was as if the apartment was waking up from a deep sleep. It had opened its dense rows of “eyes”, staring vacantly at Chen Chen who was standing before it...

chapters.title: Chapter 293: Investigation
chapters.body:
Chapter 293: Investigation

Chapter 293: Investigation

“Gasp...”

This sight caused Chen Chen to inhale sharply. The scanners on his back seemed to sense that their wearer was emotionally disturbed and popped out once again, scanning the surroundings frantically. Even so, no matter what they did, they could not detect any signs of life in the apartment in front of them...

“Let’s retreat further.”

Without any hesitation, Chen Chen took the initiative to retreat again, until they were back at the portal from which they came. Only then did Chen Chen secretly heave a sigh of relief.

Following that, with his right hand, Chen Chen quickly pressed a button on his left arm. There was a beep and a jet injector shot out into the air. Chen Chen grabbed the injector while he tilted his neck. At once, a small piece of armor on his neck popped open, exposing the airtight membrane.

After that, Chen Chen directly inserted the jet injector into the membrane!

“Hiss!”

A thin stream of liquid was injected directly into Chen Chen’s vein.

About thirty seconds later, Chen Chen felt his mind clear up. The initial feeling of being spooked quickly faded, leaving only an endless flow of speculations and reflections in his mind.

“How interesting...”

Chen Chen supported his chin in one hand, wordlessly stared at the apartment a hundred meters away, then looked at the Bugatti Spaceship beside him. “X-112, did you see when the doors were opened just now?”

“I didn’t.”

X-112 replied, “I pulled up the visual signals of the three Black Knights beside you. Just now, when you left, there were 5.3 seconds when everyone looked away from the apartment. Thus, there’s a hundred percent chance that the doors in the apartment were opened within these 5.3 seconds.”

Chen Chen nodded. He had thought as much. The Black Knights needed to be constantly vigilant, so it was impossible for them to keep their eyes fixed on the apartment behind. However, the rusty doors of the apartment were opened silently, which was something ordinary people could not do.

First of all, there was no electricity in the apartment. Chen Chen was very sure of this. Even if there were electricity, the doors were specially equipped with switches, which would open automatically as long as they were powered on. Nonetheless, what happened just now would not have been possible.

This was because those rusty doors could no longer be opened with electricity. Even if they were opened, they could not be opened without a sound.

Chen Chen directly discarded the possibility that the doors were controlled by an electric switch.

However, if it was not controlled by someone, then what was it?

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes out of habit. He had to admit that this was no longer a question that could be solved by taking NZT-48. The end of civilization in this dimension was caused by some existence and it was very likely that this apartment behind him was related to all this.

“Is this situation similar to Elysium’s dimension?”

Chen Chen shook his head. At this moment, the voice of X-112 came from the PK Armor’s communication device again. “Sir Commander, the M-1 drones have examined the general circumstances within a radius of ten kilometers. We found thirteen human skeletons exposed on the street.”

“Can the animals and plants be detected?”

Chen Chen asked again, “Can you turn on the camera’s thermal imaging mode and search for animals in the ruins?”

“We can try.”

In accordance with Chen Chen’s order, the four drones switched to thermal imaging mode and started the exploration mission again.

As they were exploring, a map with a radius of about ten kilometers around Chen Chen’s location was also gradually taking shape.

This map was directly transmitted to Chen Chen’s armor. The PK Armor had a unique AR system that could directly project images on Chen Chen’s retina.

Currently, at the lower right corner of Chen Chen’s field of vision was the map that was taking shape from the drones’ relentless exploration.

When the map was formed, Chen Chen did not continue to move, but kept quiet and stayed in place while paying close attention to the small animals around him.

In Chen Chen’s opinion, since these small animals could thrive so abundantly after the extinction of humans, it should be possible to prove that the “thing” that had wiped out humans was harmless to animals and plants.

No, maybe it was not harmless, just that these little animals could keenly detect the presence of that “thing” and evade it in advance.

As Chen Chen considered this, half an hour later, the four drones finally scanned everything within ten kilometers.

Chen Chen clicked on the map at the lower right corner and the map was immediately enlarged. It was translucent and occupied Chen Chen’s entire field of vision. There were densely packed squares scattered over it. Each square represented a building.

It was like an actual bird’s-eye view of a metropolis.

When he zoomed in on the map, a large number of small red dots appeared. Still, most of these red dots were dark red or orange-red and some were orange or even bright yellow. These red dots were all over the entire map. At first glance, they seemed to be scattered at random like stars in the sky, with no pattern to speak of.

These little red dots were all wild animals hiding in the buildings or ruins. The brighter the red dot, the larger their bodies. The dark red dots might be mice and birds while the red-orange and orange ones were wild cats and dogs. The bright yellow, which was the brightest color, would be a medium or large creature such as cattle and sheep.

“X-112, based on the density of wild animals, divide the area explored into squares with sides of 100x100m, then categorize the density into four levels.”

Chen Chen thought for a while and continued. “For the first level, the number of wild animals in the square area is greater than five and the area is represented by the color blue; the second level, where the number of wild animals in the area is greater than two, is represented by yellow; the third level will have a number of wild animals in the area that is greater than one, represented by dark red; the fourth level, where the number of wild animals in the area is zero, is represented by bright red.”

“Understood.”

As X-112 executed the order, the map in front of Chen Chen became separated by a series of shallow dotted lines. The entire map which had a diameter of about 4 kilometers was divided into 1500 tightly packed squares. After that, these squares began to change color rapidly.

After the map had fully changed color, Chen Chen suddenly narrowed his eyes.

He noticed that on this map, there were many individual red areas and dark red areas, but these were indeed scattered here and there in a disorderly jumble. Only three spots displayed the most regularity — all three of them consisted of a bright red center with edges that were dark red, further edges that were yellow, and an outermost fringe of blue.

“X-112, perform calculative corrections to the map.”

Chen Chen ordered again.

After Chen Chen’s order, most of the random reds and yellows disappeared from the map in front of him. Those locations were either empty roads or open spaces without any houses. The scarcity of animals in these spots was not an abnormal phenomenon.

Only the three locations, with an area of several thousand square meters, were highlighted. One of the abnormal locations was the apartment building that Chen Chen just entered...

“As I thought.”

Looking at the map before him, Chen Chen smiled placidly, displaying a look of realization. “These three locations must be some kind of restricted zone to life forms.”

Now that he had ascertained the security of his surroundings, Chen Chen was not apprehensive anymore. He directly boarded the Bugatti Spaceship. “X-112, take me to the location where you found the human skeletons while keeping away from those three abnormal areas, as well as those small red areas that still remain after the map was corrected.”

“Very well!”

X-112 replied decisively.

chapters.title: Chapter 294: A Strange Diary
chapters.body:
Chapter 294: A Strange Diary

Chapter 294: A Strange Diary

After they flew for only two or three minutes, the spaceship landed on an empty, crumbling street.

This was a wide crossroads. Chen Chen remembered that the movies from the East China Sea Continent would always begin with an opening shot of crossroads like this one during rush hours.

The massive traffic flow and the heavy crowds on both sides of the road created a bustling, fast-paced scene.

As the green light flashed, the traffic that was as majestic as a river appeared to be interrupted by an invisible force. The flow of people, packed like sardines on both sides of the road, suddenly surged past like a flood, moving out of this thriving city scene.

Only now, the endless traffic that used to be there was no longer there and the throngs of people had disappeared, leaving only the dilapidated streets and vines spreading everywhere.

As Chen Chen walked out of the spaceship, he immediately saw a skeleton on the side of the road. It was practically a part of the soil.

This corpse had been dead for a long time and only the jumble of white bones was left. Due to the long-term effects of the weather, it was half-buried in the soil. It was likely that in a few years, it would completely sink into the soil and vanish.

The bones looked like they had been frequently gnawed by animals.

When he saw this, Chen Chen could not help frowning. With a corpse like this, there was nothing to learn except for the fact that this person had been dead for a long time. “X-112, are all the skeletons like this?”

“Yes. It may be because the search range is too small and the drones aren’t able to search inside the buildings, so no well-preserved corpses have been found for the time being.”

“Expand the drones’ search and try to ascertain the conditions within a hundred kilometers by today.”

As he said that, Chen Chen looked at the dilapidated house fronts on both sides of the road. He reconfirmed that there were no forbidden red areas nearby, then led the Black Knights toward the house fronts.

These street-side houses were all shops. In Tokyo, each inch of these shops would be worth an inch of gold, but now they had long since declined and became the homes of wild cats and dogs.

Those small counters selling snacks had long been raided and Chen Chen even noticed the shattered glass smithereens and a hammer on the ground. These were all traces of the plundering.

The same thing had occurred in clothing stores and tool stores. Conversely, the ones that nobody was interested in were the most expensive jewelry stores and the banks that used to be filled with people.

Chen Chen simply asked the three Black Knights to explore on their own. As they searched through the shops, Chen Chen soon found what he was looking for.

A well-preserved dried male corpse.

When he heard this from X-112, Chen Chen walked into an office building and found a corpse that had turned as dried as bacon long ago, in a room on the third floor.

Nonetheless, it was different from the exposed corpses outside. This office was sealed shut and there were no windows open, so the corpse that had been dead for many years still remained as it was before its death.

The corpse remained sprawled over the desk, seeming to be asleep, but it was wearing an outdoor jacket. The clothing itself had withered to tatters long ago due to the liquid seeping out of the decayed corpse. Beside the table, there was an opened package with a coil of rope, as well as daggers and other tools scattered on the ground.

There was even an open military canteen by one side.

This corpse entered this office alone when he was alive, then took a nap on his desk, but unexpectedly, after falling asleep, he never woke up again.

The closed office had preserved perfectly the scene of the corpse’s death. After a brief evaluation, Chen Chen promptly figured out the time when the corpse died.

The person that was once this corpse had died four to five years ago, and the age of that person had only been about 30 years old.

Did he die suddenly at such a young age?

Chen Chen frowned instinctively. There seemed to be something more to the corpse in front of him.

Thinking of this, he made a quick gesture and the Black Knight on the side immediately came forward and began to rifle through the body while another Black Knight looked through the corpse’s package.

Soon, the first Black Knight found a portable tool bag in the pocket of the corpse. In the tool bag, there was also a book that was similar to a diary.

Chen Chen took the palm-sized book, opened it gently, and was greeted by a line of scribbled Japanese.

After taking NZT-48, Chen Chen could easily master various foreign languages. Therefore, he often set aside time to learn some of the most common or more conventional languages in the world including ancient Greek, Latin, and Japanese.

Presently, as Chen Chen looked at the words, he could swiftly read out these Japanese lines:

“26 March 2028. Weather: fine. I must continue working hard today!

“Now is my favorite time — dinnertime. I’m about to begin eating. Today’s meal is the canned beef that Daitetsu-kun brought back from the city. I can’t believe that Daitetsu-kun is so kind. He’s willing to share with us the canned beef he obtained after risking life and limb. I must remember Daitetsu-kun’s kindness!”

After reading the first page, Chen Chen could not help frowning. This was indeed a diary and it was also the thing in which the corpse in front of him had recorded the dates and trivial matters of life. However, it did not mention any information that Chen Chen wanted to know.

Still, seeing that there were a few more pages, Chen Chen turned to the second page and continued reading.

“16 April 2028. Weather: rain. Let’s do this, let’s go!

“After changing to a new diary, I found that I was gradually losing the habit of writing a diary. Perhaps it’s because I’ve been softened by how safe and comfortable life was recently? But there’s no other way. I’m still too weak to help everyone. Fortunately, everyone is working hard to survive!”

On the second page, there was still no valuable information. The only information was that the corpse before Chen Chen had probably joined a group during this time.

Did they band together to survive?

Chen Chen shook his head and continued to turn to the third page.

What followed next was also some daily affairs. As long as there was no vital information, Chen Chen would just skim it. It was only near the end that he was suddenly attracted by an entry —

“2 September 2028. Weather: fine. Let’s do this! Today’s a sunny day as well!

“It’s been a long time since I wrote an entry. Ever since ‘it’ started to happen all over the world, the glorious civilization of mankind has completely fallen. The remaining survivors inherit the will of the deceased and struggle to live on. I’m no different but lately, I’ve suddenly had a bad premonition because I have had nightmares for two consecutive nights. Each night I’ll dream of something terrible...

“Haha, it should be just a coincidence. I won’t have that dream again tonight. I won’t.”

It?

What was “it”?

Chen Chen eagerly read on and flipped to the next page, which was the day after this entry.

“3 September 2028. Weather: fine. I really can’t believe it!

“Why? Why did I have that nightmare again last night? It shouldn’t be. It’s just made-up, right? It must be a coincidence... Ando, please don’t frighten yourself! It must be just a coincidence. I will not have that nightmare again tonight. I must be strong!”

chapters.title: Chapter 295: “It”
chapters.body:
Chapter 295: “It”

Chapter 295: “It”

Did he see something in his dream?

Upon reading these two entries, Chen Chen immediately discovered something unusual. This man named Ando seemed to be afraid of his own dreams.

According to the diary’s description, he had had the same dream for three consecutive nights and this dream seemed to signify something.

It seemed to be signifying something dreadful. In other words, this was that “it”...

Chen Chen wore a thoughtful look, but he did not dwell too long on this. In any case, the answer should be found in the next few pages.

Thus, Chen Chen flipped to the next page.

“4 September 2028!!!

“D*mn it, I had that dream again last night. It’s that woman with disheveled hair. She’s getting closer and closer to me! D*mn it, d*mn it! On the first day, she was still outside the door, but last night she was already standing by the head of my bed. I could even see her white eyes, d*mn d*mn d*mn d*mn d*mn d*mn!!!

“I daren’t tell Daitetsu-kun about all this. Otherwise, if everyone knows about this, I’ll be kicked out of the team. I’m only 30 this year — I haven’t lived enough of life. I don’t want to die!”

Woman with disheveled hair? White eyes? A dream that continued where it left off?

After reading this paragraph, Chen Chen narrowed his eyes in response. He glanced at the corpse in front of him again and observed how the corpse was sprawled quietly across the desk. Its face shrank into a pucker, its mouth turned into a black hole due to the loss of moisture...

At this moment, Chen Chen suddenly realized that the hideous expression of this corpse was not caused by postmortem shrinking, but rather, that the person had seen something extremely terrifying when he was alive, which resulted in this look...

Chen Chen stepped back subconsciously. Something was weird about this corpse...

Chen Chen turned to the next page again:

“5 September 2028. Weather: fine.

“I’ve left the team — Kotetsu-kun and Sakura-ko and the rest. They drove me out, but I know that this isn’t their fault. It was I who caused everyone trouble.

“I’ve made preparations for my death. I didn’t sleep last night, so now I have to stay active all the time. Otherwise, if I’m not moving, my eyelids will start becoming heavy. I don’t know how long I can hold on. Maybe I can try to sleep for a short five minutes and wake up before the next move has been made. Perhaps I might be able to escape death in this manner?

“Still, I’m terrified. I’ll probably die, right? And after being kicked out of the team, how long can I last by myself?”

On this page, the handwriting started to become smudged. When the author wrote this passage, he was crying the entire time.

Chen Chen instinctively flipped the page again but was taken aback to see that the diary was completely blank after this.

This diary was cut off on 5 September 2028.

Chen Chen could not believe this at first, so he turned to the back pages again, but they were all blank. This was the last time the person had written his diary.

That was to say, it was either because in the following period, he was in no mood to write an entry as he was attempting to avoid the dream or he had not even had time to write anything before he died right here...

Chen Chen looked at the corpse again with a pensive look.

Could it be that this corpse in front of Chen Chen was killed by his own dream?

“‘It’, ‘continuous nightmare’, ‘woman with disheveled hair’...”

In this diary, from beginning to end, the man named Ando never mentioned what was it that had destroyed human civilization, but only alluded to it by saying “it”. These three key phrases comprised all the information that was revealed in the diary.

“However, based on his tone, this ‘it’ he mentioned that destroyed human civilization seems to be different from the nightmare he encountered.”

Chen Chen pondered out loud, “Or maybe the nightmare he had was a part of ‘it’?”

There was still too little information. Chen Chen did not learn much that was useful.

With this in mind, Chen Chen took a long final look at the corpse, then gestured to everyone. The three Black Knights followed Chen Chen and left the office.

Next, Chen Chen simply asked the three Black Knights to split up, each searching a floor, to look for new clues as quickly as possible.

Although it was like looking for a needle in a haystack, what Chen Chen was facing was a whole lost world after all. There was no other way except this most straightforward method.

Even so, fortunately, this office building was not as eerie as the previous apartment. After the Black Knights split up into three, they did not encounter any abnormalities. Instead, as the search continued, more and more bodies were found.

When Chen Chen received the updates and came to the fifth floor of the office building, he immediately saw the word “Bvlgari” that was carved in large Japanese characters upon the door using green and yellow acrylic panels.

Chen Chen still remembered that this was an internationally renowned luxury brand.

Nonetheless, that level of prosperity had long since vanished with the passing of human civilization. At this time, in the office hall that should have been extraordinarily lively, there were five withered corpses lying in disorder on the floor.

It was clear that these five corpses were older than that “Ando” guy, much older. They were practically air-dried and blackened, looking like lumps of charcoal, full of indescribable, grotesque wrinkles.

Based on their clothes, the five bodies had all been women.

Without Chen Chen having to give an order, the three Black Knights squatted and started searching for clues on the bodies. However, this time Chen Chen was disappointed. There was nothing on these corpses.

Even so, Chen Chen noticed that these five corpses all had one common feature. That was, if one lifted them while maintaining the posture they had when they died, one would find that their faces were all turned in the same direction...

Chen Chen looked in the direction where the five corpses had looked. It was the floor-to-ceiling window opposite the elevator.

At this time, the floor-to-ceiling window was no longer as clear as it used to be as it was covered in dust and fallen leaves. Still, Chen Chen could just about see the world outside through the glass.

The external world was blank. There were only the buildings on the opposite side of the road and the old mottled billboards that were near collapse.

Other than that, there was nothing outside the window.

“In other words, these five women seemed to have seen the same thing, but when they saw that thing, it also harvested their lives?”

Chen Chen murmured.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen’s eyes flashed decisively. Then he pulled out the GS Blade that was hanging over his PK Armor and flicked it lightly. Suddenly, the GS Blade that had only been a handle in his hand ejected a ten-centimeter-long blade.

After that, Chen Chen squatted directly and cut the belly of one of the female corpses.

The GS Blade came with a high-frequency shock effect. Even though the corpse in front of him had hardened into a tough leather-like substance, Chen Chen only had to swipe lightly to cut it open at once.

However, after cutting it open, Chen Chen discovered that everything inside the corpse was as usual. Although it had been thoroughly air-dried, the shape of the organs from when they were alive could still be vaguely seen.

This meant that these corpses did not die from some internal injury, but something else.

chapters.title: Chapter 296: Autopsy
chapters.body:
Chapter 296: Autopsy

Chapter 296: Autopsy

Still concerned, Chen Chen pried open the skull of the corpse again. He saw that the brain inside the skull had shrunk into substances resembling a walnut and there were no signs of external damage to be seen.

Chen Chen spent several minutes performing an autopsy on the corpse but still was not able to find any vivid signs of injuries. There were hardly any scratches or bruises to be found.

There were no signs of organ or brain damage inside the corpse either. This suggested that these corpses may have died an abrupt death.

Otherwise known as sudden death.

In common terms, the definition of sudden death referred to cases where an otherwise usually healthy person suddenly succumbed to a natural disease within a short period. There were two types of such natural diseases:

Sudden Cardiac Death and Non-sudden Cardiac Death.

However, it had to be noted that these corpses had dried out for extended periods. Their organs had displayed signs of corrosion prior to drying out, but without access to professional equipment, Chen Chen could not determine the precise cause of death of these corpses. He could only conclude that they had died of sudden death.

“Could this mean that this is something that causes sudden death just by ‘seeing’ it?”

Chen Chen furrowed his eyebrows. He considered for a moment before switching the PK Armor to thermal imaging mode, which gave him vision similar to what was seen in Predator.

Unlike regular thermal imaging which only displayed heat radiation in large blobs, the thermal imaging of the PK Armor provided finer details, even surpassing details that were otherwise glimpsed by the naked eye.

Under the effect of the NZT-48, it only took Chen Chen several minutes to adapt to this vision system.

The only question was, would this be able to help him negate the “instant death upon sight” rule?

As Chen Chen followed down the path, he felt a crushing weight set upon his once relaxed mood.

Of all the large dimensions he had been to, the dimension of Gantz was the strangest of them all. It was somehow stranger than Elysium.

Furthermore, this dimension was unlike Elysium which was equipped with advanced technology and used a “central system” AI to oversee the day-to-day activities of humans, where he could get a grasp of all of the ongoings by taking over the central system.

This dimension was hardly any different from the real world.

While Chen Chen was occupied with his thoughts, he suddenly saw a corpse holding onto a phone. He immediately lit up at the prospect and took the phone while he had the Black Knights continue searching the room. Meanwhile, he returned to the Bugatti Spaceship where he used a cable to connect it with the spaceship.

After charging it for a short while, he managed to reboot the phone that had been shut down for many years at this point.

The moment it was turned on, X-112 immediately infiltrated its system and quickly lifted all permission restrictions while also thoroughly inspecting it.

“Successfully extracted social media software chat logs, currently scanning...

“Message scanned, filtering content for valuable information... The phone was powered off on 3 June 2025.”

“When was the calamity?”

Chen Chen asked without skipping a beat. By calamity, he was referring to the time which the aliens officially invaded, which happened in the latter half of the comic plot.

“It was in 2000.”

X-112 replied, “According to the chat logs extracted from the LINE software, the aliens invaded during the year 2000. They would later be defeated by Kei Kurono and the other black sphere forces who would go on to restore the peace of mankind. The peace lasted for twenty-five years until the moment this phone was powered off. This is all the information I was able to extract from the chat logs. I cannot investigate any further due to lack of Internet.”

“Can you communicate with the satellites?”

Chen Chen asked again, “Even if mankind vanished, some of the satellites should still be operating for several years at least.”

“Unable to establish a connection with any satellite signal, they may have malfunctioned,” X-112 replied again.

Chen Chen tipped his head in response before continuing. “In that case, dispatch the drones to be on the lookout for a data center in Tokyo. If we can find a data center, we should be able to acquire plenty of information.”

“Understood.”

X-112 responded, “I’ll remain alert.”

After that, they uncovered more corpses. The Black Knights went through every nook and cranny of the office building, finding more than hundreds of corpses in the process. Most of these corpses had dried out while some of the corpses in poorer condition had long since corroded and turned into skull remnants.

One thing Chen Chen could be sure of was that they all had died at the same time. The instrument of their death was a shocking one. They must have seen something outside the window at the same time which instantly triggered this sudden death phenomenon.

Returning to the spaceship, Chen Chen went through the diary again while he further contemplated his findings.

Based on his current observations, all of the corpses discovered so far had died at the same time. The only exception was this Ando who left behind the diary.

Based on the contents of the diary, the downfall of human civilization began since “it” came to the world. Could this be a strong indication that the “it” mentioned in the diary was directly connected to the sudden death of the people in the office building?

Meanwhile, Ando had survived the first wave of death and had become one of the “survivors” as he had described themselves in the diary.

These survivors even managed to form a small group to survive together in the end times.

After that, there was a chronicle of Ando’s nightmares.

Chen Chen knitted his eyebrows together. He could not find any mention of the source of the nightmares in the diary. However, upon reading between the lines, he figured out that the group was also aware of this frequent nightmare phenomenon. They had mercilessly expelled Ando from the group once they found out he was having these nightmares.

“Why would they exile him, was it out of fear of...”

Chen Chen suddenly thought of something, he flicked an eyebrow. “Infection?”

However, Chen Chen debunked his theory as soon as he had conjured it. “Impossible, I’ve never heard of such a thing as a contagious nightmare. But Ando didn’t mention anything about why he was having these nightmares nor did he try to look for the source of these nightmares and how he rid himself of them. Could it be that they believed there was no cure for these nightmares?”

Unfortunately, Ando did not have a phone with him. Still, that was to be expected since despite having survived several years longer, Ando was already living in the end times. It was the post-civilization world where all electronics were rendered useless since they had run out of battery. Mobile phones were no exception to this rule.

Except that there seemed to be something too peculiar about this devastating world...

After the Black Knights surveyed the entire office building, Chen Chen had them continue searching further down the street. Shortly after, they arrived at a high-rise residential area.

Searching the residential area took more effort than the office building but the findings were more worthwhile as well. The Black Knights produced new findings very quickly.

When Chen Chen received the Black Knight’s report and walked into one of the units, he immediately saw the state of the mess the house was in.

chapters.title: Chapter 297: Corpse Of The Hanged Lady
chapters.body:
Chapter 297: Corpse Of The Hanged Lady

Chapter 297: Corpse of the Hanged Lady

It was a middle-class residential area. There were 3 units in total, each one of them was 33-stories tall. Each one of these units housed two households on each floor, where each household came with two bedrooms and a living room area. At the present, Chen Chen had stepped into the tenth floor of one of the units.

The entrance to the apartment was effortlessly cut down using the GS Blade. Upon entering, Chen Chen saw countless food packaging littered across the living room floors. They were sprinkled with many scrap papers, pieces of cloth, and various waste.

A sofa that had been broken down into an almost unrecognizable form was set behind the door. All of the fabric had been cut off from it and pasted on to the windows.

Yes, all of the windows were obscured using pieces of fabric and newspaper. There was hardly any source of light in the room. Sparse glimpse of rays slipped through the gaps on the window and cast a depressing and strange aura into the room.

“Windows again?”

Chen Chen squinted his eyes upon witnessing this scene.

After that, Chen Chen proceeded to head inside. Very quickly, he saw the figure of a person through the open bedroom door.

It was a figure with its head drooped in an unnatural angle, drooped over its withered body was a set of pajamas that were so dirty that its color was indistinguishable.

It was a lady hanging from the ceiling.

The lady’s appearance had long become unrecognizable. The only thing about her that left a strong impression on Chen Chen was her hair that had become this bizarre clump of substance like wool felt, probably caused by it having gone unwashed for some time.

This lady had killed herself.

Chen Chen only had several peeks at it before he went into the other room. Just like the living room and the other bedroom, the windows were blocked out with pieces of fabric and newspaper as well, there was hardly any gap to be seen. The floor was littered with plastic packaging of all sorts of junk food.

Sure enough, this lady had lived in this place for quite some time.

Taking note of this fact, Chen Chen went to the toilet. When he pulled open the toilet door, he saw the floor covered in excrement. Even the toilet bowl was filled to the brim with excrement.

Chen Chen seemed completely unbothered by the excrement in the toilet as he went to the faucet to turn it on. Not a single drop of water came out.

These sorts of apartments usually came with a water storage tank. Since no water came out, that meant it had all been used up.

The foul toilet, empty faucet, and a floor littered with empty food packaging. Everything he saw here seemed to point toward the lady having lived here for a significant period.

Chen Chen had the Black Knights search for anything that may resemble a diary to see if he could find anything worthwhile, but the results of the search would disappoint him.

It seemed that this lady had just been scraping by a day at a time and did not keep a habit of writing a diary.

Just as Chen Chen figured that there were no clues to be found in this place and prepared to leave, a section of the wall beside the corpse suddenly caught his attention.

There were marks on the walls on the other side of the bedroom which suggested they may have been beaten in.

Chen Chen slowly approached and touched it delicately. The tips of his fingers grazed against several pits formed by hard objects bashing against the wall. Even some of the debris in the wall was cracked.

After that, Chen Chen shuffled his feet around the layer of trash piled up around his feet. Very soon after, he felt the soles of his boots knocking against a hard object which produced a rhythmical rolling sound upon being kicked.

It was a dark yellow hardwood baseball bat.

It was apparent that the caved-in pits on the wall were left behind by this baseball bat.

With this in mind, Chen Chen went to the other bedroom and saw the same marks left behind on the wall of the bedroom as well...

“Interesting...”

Chen Chen muttered to himself. Upon closer inspection, he realized that following the floorplan layout, these two bedrooms were located on the west side of the east wing. This meant that the bedroom walls were shared between the two adjacent apartment units...

“Was she trying to smash through the wall to enter the adjacent unit?”

Chen Chen knitted his brows curiously. There was no doubt in his mind that this method would lead to nowhere. No matter how strong a person was, there was simply no way to penetrate a wall without the proper tools, especially if the subject at hand was a lady wielding nothing but a baseball bat.

Only a fool would even attempt to ram through a wall using a mere baseball bat.

However, if not to penetrate the wall, what else could the hanged lady’s intentions be?

While Chen Chen stood motionlessly before the wall, he suddenly noticed patches of black marks on the wall in addition to the caved-in pits.

Since he was wearing the PK Armor, the marks were only around Chen Chen’s chest height. They were pitch-black textures. Based on the location, they appeared to be located around the deceased’s height.

“Perhaps she frequently put her face against the wall while she was still alive?”

An idea suddenly came to Chen Chen as he put his head down slightly. With both hands placed against the wall, he turned his head sideways and put his face against the black marks on the wall.

Although he was wearing the PK Armor, there was no mistaking this position. This position implied...

Eavesdropping?

Chen Chen suddenly made the connection. The deceased was regularly trying to spy on movements coming from the adjacent unit.

Could there be some sort of clues in the adjacent unit?

With this in mind, Chen Chen quickly took the Black Knights with him as they exited the unit and went to the adjacent unit on the west wing.

Previously, the Black Knights had inspected the west wing and failed to find any object of interest. When Chen Chen stepped into the unit, he saw that this was a regular apartment no different from the common household.

There was not a soul in the unit. The furnishing was normal, so were the bedrooms and the layout. There was nothing peculiar about it whatsoever.

Chen Chen stepped right into the bedroom of the west wing unit.

The layout of the east wing and west wing unit were completely identical. The only notable distinction was that the two were symmetrical. The living room in the east wing unit was on the east side and its two bedrooms were on the west side. It was the other way round for the west wing unit, with the living room placed on the west side and the two bedrooms on the east side.

As Chen Chen stepped into the bedroom, his pupils abruptly shrunk into a tiny circle!

What he saw was a gigantic mark left behind by a sizable impact. The surface of the wall was nearly completely shattered, even exposing the red bricks underneath the wall!

It appeared that something had been repeatedly smashing against the wall which left it in such a state.

What was the deal with this?

Chen Chen approached it and reached forward to touch it. There were no other remains left behind apart from the smashed wall.

Once again, Chen Chen went to the other bedroom and saw the same image as he saw in the previous bedroom. There was nothing else notable apart from a shallow hole formed on the bedroom wall.

Chen Chen frowned. While his mind was occupied by this bizarre observation, a message suddenly came from X-112. “Sir Commander, the drones had detected signs of life while patrolling along the edges of the city!”

Upon hearing this, Chen Chen immediately jolted to his senses. “Where is it?”

“8720m northeast of your current position. The target seems to be scavenging for resources on the edge of the city. Should we track him?”

“Stay close to him but don’t let him notice you, I’ll be right there!”

Chen Chen responded immediately. He had no time for these bizarre rooms. With a quick gesture of his hands, three Black Knights immediately trailed after him as they left the area together.

Following Chen Chen’s sudden departure, the strange rooms had once again fallen into an eerie silence.

Only moments after Chen Chen had left, the corpse of the hanged lady suspended on the ceiling suddenly began to sway lightly, seemingly blown by a gentle breeze.

It swayed from one side to the other as if following a set of rhythm...

chapters.title: Chapter 298: Youngster
chapters.body:
Chapter 298: Youngster

Chapter 298: Youngster

A youngster in his mid-twenties walked along the desolate highway infested with weeds with a large army green backpack strapped to his back. He carried a sharp knife in one hand and a water bottle in the other.

Potentially due to lack of grooming over a long time, the youngster wore long and untidy hair. This was complemented with uneven patches of stubble around his lips.

He was not a particularly fast walker. He walked slightly slower than the average walking person. Each time he took a step, his eyes full of caution would dart across his surroundings.

With the given context, this was not a particularly peculiar behavior, just common practice of a scavenger in a wasteland. What was truly eye-catching was the macaw[1] the size of half a forearm perched on the youngster’s shoulder.

The macaw had bright red feathers that slowly transitioned to a blue tint when they met its wings. Its posture appeared extremely alert and no matter what movements the youngster made, it remained fixed on his shoulder. It looked very intelligent as well.

The young man lifted the water bottle and swallowed a mouthful of water from it, to which he let out a content sigh. After that, he glanced at his left shoulder and produced a cup which he poured half a cup of water into, which he then held up to the beak of the macaw.

The macaw immediately leaned over with its head and buried itself into the cup, glugging away at the water.

After the macaw on his shoulder had enough to drink, the youngster finished what was left in the cup before continuing onward.

Upon paying attention to the pattern of the youngster’s movements, it could be observed that he was largely sticking to the middle of the road as if instinctively keeping a distance from the dark buildings lined across both sides of the road.

As the youngster continued on his path, the macaw on his shoulder suddenly became restless and cried out in what sounded like a call for caution.

There was an immediate change of color on the youngster’s expression. He quickly shot a look at the macaw perched on his shoulder. He saw the macaw with its head turned to an elevated position behind him while it restlessly flapped its wings about.

Without sparing a moments’ hesitation, the youngster took a pronounced step and charged into a shop lot beside the highway.

“It seems the target noticed us.”

Meanwhile, several kilometers away, Chen Chen was just getting into the Bugatti Spaceship. X-112 immediately informed, “The parrot on the target’s shoulder appears to be extremely sharp.”

“No surprise there. Due to the need for climate-induced migration, birds had developed extreme sensitivity to magnetic and electric fields. It’s nothing out of the ordinary for them to detect the electromagnetic waves emitted by our drones.”

Chen Chen silently observed the display in front of him. The display had switched into thermal imaging mode so despite the youngster immediately retreating into the ruins, his position was still clear as day under the thermal imaging display.

“But I just noticed that this person seems to be making a conscious effort in sticking to the middle of the road without straying off the path. Is he abiding by some form of rule?”

Chen Chen suddenly mumbled to himself in a low tone. The way he saw it, if some sort of unknown object or beings were to strike the remaining surviving humans, walking on such a plain terrain was as good as asking for death.

Walking on such a path was exposing yourself to countless peering eyes from all directions. You would be better off using the intertwining alley roads and streets since it would be safer.

Unless...

“Unless, he believes there’s more danger lurking inside the buildings?”

Chen Chen turned into a frown. He suddenly remembered the apartment building he was in just now. If he were to encounter some sort of threat, it would certainly be more viable to make a quick escape in the wide-open roads.

Chen Chen was starting to feel the weight of the situation.

The Bugatti Spaceship continued gliding across the air, steadily approaching the survivor at the speed of 120 kilometers per hour.

Something to consider was that this was the first time Chen Chen found a living person in the alternate dimension. He had opened so many passages to different dimensions and each time, he was faced with a crumbled civilization where there was not a single survivor. At one point, Chen Chen was convinced that there was not a single living soul inside the dimensions accessed from the USB drive.

Or so he believed, until the youngster made his appearance and rekindled a new hope in Chen Chen.

Four minutes later, Chen Chen stealthily arrived in a location a short distance away from the youngster’s position. After the Bugatti Spaceship had descended, Chen Chen got off the aircraft with three Black Knights and they began heading toward the youngster’s location.

Even after several minutes, the young man still did not make any attempts to escape. He remained curled up under the counter inside a shop. If not for the thermal imaging device revealing the target’s position, Chen Chen may have thought that the youngster had silently snuck away.

Could this stealth tactic allow him to evade whatever or whoever was responsible for the destruction of this world?

Chen Chen found it rather curious. Still, his feet continued pressing forward toward the direction of the shop lot.

As Chen Chen and his crew pressed forward, they emanated steady rounds of footsteps which were further amplified in the idle, quiet street. From Chen Chen’s thermal imaging vision, he noticed the youngster trembling upon hearing their footsteps.

Surely enough, he was afraid.

Just as Chen Chen stepped foot into the shop lot decorated with shattered windows, the youngster finally made a move. He abruptly shot out from beneath the counter and darted further inside the building without so much as a glance at Chen Chen’s group!

In most cases, these street-side shop lots had a back entrance. The youngster knew that there was no way he could remain hidden and decided to escape using the back door.

“Can you get away?”

Chen Chen knitted his brows and motioned with his hand. One of the Black Knights immediately took a meteoric step forward which caused the tile under his feet to shatter with a sudden “crack”!

The Black Knight almost instantly vanished into an afterimage and launched himself forward like a bullet shot out of its chamber!

The youngster felt a strong gust of wind roaring from behind him. Before he could dodge, he felt a twitch of something grabbing him from behind!

“It’s over...”

The youngster felt a biting cold surge across his body. He instinctively grabbed the macaw perched on his shoulder and flung it in front of him. With all the strength he could muster, he yelled, “Run!”

“Chirp, chirp!”

Unprepared, the parrot flapped its wings frantically in the air before landing back on the ground and charged into the darkness behind.

The other two Black Knights did not try to stop it as Chen Chen had ordered them to capture the man, not the parrot.

The youngster felt a tremendous force propelling him. Before he could even react, he found himself suspended mid-air. With just one arm, the Black Knight had flung the youngster backward and viciously slammed him onto the floor.

“Ow!”

The youngster yelped terribly but got back up on his feet the very next second, like a carp cast out of the water. Without sparing a thought, he rocketed toward the main entrance!

However, just as he was trying to escape again, he met Chen Chen and the remaining two Black Knights head-on.

The youngster immediately halted his footsteps and fell into a daze.

[1] A species of parrot

chapters.title: Chapter 299: Fujiwara Aoki
chapters.body:
Chapter 299: Fujiwara Aoki

Chapter 299: Fujiwara Aoki

“Calm down, I’m one of the living.”

Chen Chen was compelled to give the PK Armor a gentle tap upon seeing the youngster’s reaction. The armor immediately shrunk. Following a faint blue glow, the PK Armor reverted to its armguard form.

After the PK Armor disappeared, Chen Chen appeared in front of the youngster wearing the GS Combat Suit.

“You, you’re a Gantz member!”

The youngster’s eyes grew wide upon seeing Chen Chen’s outfit as he glowed with disbelief. “No way! Wasn’t the savior Gantz troop called off by the united nation a long time ago? How could you be here?”

“You’re certainly correct, I’m a member of the Gantz.”

Chen Chen nodded affirmatively. “The governments of each country never suspended the Gantz troops. Instead, they created countless underground training bases. We received training there in preparation for the arrival of the next calamity.”

The youngster revealed a look of joy which was immediately succeeded by a sorrowful expression. “It’s no use, human civilization has perished for five years now. When the second calamity comes, even you Gantz troops cannot do anything about it...”

There was a subtle change in Chen Chen’s expression. He then said earnestly, “It’s a long story. There have been some issues in our underground base and nobody ever came to fix it so we were stuck and isolated from the surface for five years. This persisted for five years until we managed to pry open the entrance to the base, but we never expected to see this upon surfacing...”

“What?”

The youngster was shocked. “You’re saying that this is your first time leaving the underground base since the second calamity?”

“That’s right. This is only the third day since we’ve left the military base that we’ve been stuck in for five years. Our leader divided us into several teams and our team is assigned to search for clues in the East China sea area here.”

Chen Chen nodded. “And you’re the first living person we encountered. My name is Chen Chen, a member of the Mainland division of Gantz. The three teammates behind me are from the Mainland as well. What’s your name?”

“So, you’re from the Mainland, no wonder your Japanese has an accent to it.”

The youngster lit up and announced ceremoniously, “My name is Fujiwara Aoki, you guys can just call me Aoki for short.”

“Aoki-san it is.”

Chen Chen gave the young man several firm pats on the shoulder before announcing in a serious tone, “This is no place for chatting, let’s find some other place for that. Also, could Aoki-san give us a brief on the current situation of human civilization, please!”

The youngster named Fujiwara Aoki informed them to wait for him briefly. He quickly turned around and blew a whistle into the shop lot, which immediately after, the macaw walked out taking small tip taps.

It looked at Chen Chen and his crew up and down for a brief moment before climbing back onto Fujiwara Aoki’s shoulder following his prompting. After settling down, it began grooming its ruffled feather that was messed up by its previous frantic escape.

After that, following Fujiwara Aoki’s request, they came to the center of the road to continue their conversation.

Before they began, Chen Chen noticed Fujiwara Aoki appeared to have not eaten anything for a long time. He produced several energy bars and handed them over. This gesture immediately won over Aoki’s trust.

“The vision range here is excellent. This way, we can avoid being suddenly ambushed by ‘it’...”

Fujiwara Aoki advised while nibbling the energy bar, “To be fair, it’s hard to get away once ‘it’ locks on to you. Still, you have a good shot at escaping if you’re in a wide-open street. It’s better than being stuck in a desolate corner...”

“It?”

Chen Chen produced a diary upon hearing this mention. “So, what exactly is this ‘it’ you’re referring to? This isn’t the first time I’ve heard of this. I found this diary of a deceased which speaks of ‘it’ as well.”

“Diary?”

Fujiwara Aoki’s expression changed when he saw the palm-sized black leather book Chen Chen held in his hand. A bitter smile greeted his lips. “It seems like you guys don’t know anything at all. Are you really reading something like a deceased’s diary?”

“What makes you say so?”

Chen Chen leaned against the Bugatti Spaceship and raised the question again.

“...”

Fujiwara Aoki became silent for a brief moment before starting. “It’s very difficult to explain everything in one go. Let’s start with the second great calamity.”

“I’m sure you’re all aware of the first great calamity which happened in 2000, which was thirty years ago...”

Fujiwara Aoki began recalling the events. “The aliens went all out with their wartime weapons, nearly wiping out the greater half of human civilization. They treated humans as food and turned us into livestock, imprisoning us in cages...”

“After that, our Gantz troops defeated them.”

Chen Chen added.

“Yes. In the end, you guys saved Earth along with mankind.”

Fujiwara Aoki nodded. “But nobody ever expected that just twenty-five years after the first great calamity, the second great calamity broke out. The catalyst of this calamity stemmed from the deepest regions of the Internet...”

“Internet?” Chen Chen was immediately taken aback.

“That’s right. On that fateful day, a picture suddenly appeared on the websites and forums across the globe.” Fujiwara Aoki tipped his head. “It was a strangely-shaped object, a black and white abstract pattern composed of countless spirals...”

Fujiwara Aoki furrowed his brows together upon mentioning this. He tried his best to describe the shape of the picture, “I’m not sure if you’ve seen that picture, but I guarantee you that even if you scribble on a paper a billion or tens of billions of times, you’d still have no luck reproducing it. It doesn’t follow any predetermined pattern or rule. It seems to spiral and blink endlessly. It reaches deep into your mind and stirs something deep inside it. It’s like touching a high voltage cable.”

Chen Chen could not resist asking upon hearing Fujiwara Aoki’s narration, “So, what you’re saying is that a sensory-disorientating image appeared on the Internet. What does this image do?”

Even after having said all that, Fujiwara Aoki could only scratch his head. “I don’t know what it does, but all I know is that all of the people who see this picture die instantly. Only a tiny population can withstand it...”

“What?”

Chen Chen was baffled. “A person who sees the picture dies instantly, is this some sort of joke?”

The imagery of the bizarre death of the several hundred people in the office building suddenly flashed by Chen Chen’s mind. He realized that there was still a clear distinction between the two subjects as Fujiwara claimed that the picture had appeared online, while the people in the office building seemed to be killed by something outside the windows...

“I’m not messing with you, everything I just told you is true and I’m one of the people who are immune to the picture!”

Fujiwara Aoki quickly defended his claims. “But I’ve already forgotten what the picture looks like, the people in my group can’t remember either. That’s because the picture isn’t something that the human mind can remember!”

chapters.title: Chapter 300: Basilisk Serpent
chapters.body:
Chapter 300: Basilisk Serpent

Chapter 300: Basilisk Serpent

“The lethal image first appeared on websites all over the world. It was the work of a notorious hacker organization. Thousands on websites with enormous traffic were hacked overnight and the image was stamped on the front page of these websites...”

Fujiwara Aoki took a deep breath and suddenly chuckled. “On that day, caught completely off guard, death toll exceeding the billions was tallied all across the globe.”

“How did each government react?”

Chen Chen asked, “Also, what did the picture turn out to be, was it deciphered later on?”

“With it being such a major event and all, of course, the officials weren’t going to standby idly.”

Fujiwara Aoki nodded, his expression growing gloomier as he went on. “The hacker organization was wiped out in just three days. No one knows where the pattern came from. The government probably does, but they won’t tell us anything.

“As for the reason the pattern could kill people, the experts gave a detailed summary – this was a specially structured pattern. In essence, they were a string of geometric paradoxes that cannot be processed by the human brain. This creates a paradoxical chain in the human brain akin to a computer, which induces a physiological phenomenon known as ‘Gödel’s shock’. Upon experiencing this phenomenon, the human brain will proceed to shut down.”

“Gödel’s shock.”

Chen Chen repeated it to himself. Gödel was the greatest mathematician and logician of the 20th century and his most famous work was the renowned Gödel’s incompleteness theorems.

“Yes, many people began circulating the message that this pattern seemed to be closely related to Gödel’s discoveries in the field of mathematics and physiology. And so, the effect triggered by the pattern was hence named ‘Gödel’s shock’.”

Fujiwara Aoki explained, “When the brain shuts down, the heart stops functioning as well, which completely kills the person. This was also the reason why the pattern which claimed billions of lives was later named Basilisk. It was formed from the fresh blood of Medusa upon her death and it possesses the same ability to petrify humans.”
